name: Sakura-Con 2026

events.title: Registration Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Cosplay Contest Pre-Judging
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Mandatory in-person judging for anyone participating in the Cosplay Contest

events.title: AMV Showcase
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] AMVs to make you laugh, cry, cheer and just plain enjoy.

events.title: Open Mic
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Manga Library Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Manga Swaps Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Rhythm Gaming Freeplay
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Get your groove in with up to three other players in Just Dance, mimic silhouettes to iconic pop hits! Featuring other rhythm surprises as well!

events.title: Flesh and Blood – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Dear Con-goer, do you desire Flesh? Do you desire Blood? Perhaps even both at the same time? In that case, we at the TCG Room have you covered. Come see us and one of our Flesh Experts (Blood Experts available upon request) will teach you all the ins and outs of the Flesh and Blood TCG. Should you survive the trials, you will be rewarded with a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

All materials for Flesh and Blood were graciously donated by Legend Story Studios, who are a fantastic partner.

events.title: Flesh and Blood – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Come play a casual Commander game with friends, strangers, or even DRAW PHASE Staff!

Pods of 4 will be seated on-demand. No deck? No problem! We've got a library of pre-constructed decks you can borrow for the duration of your game. All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

events.title: DRAW PHASE Presents TCGs @ Sakura-Con - Friday
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Welcome to the Sakura-Con x DRAW PHASE 2026 Trading Card Games room! We'll be running totally FREE events wall to wall all weekend long, from Learn to Play events, to Drafts, Sealed, Constructed, and more for some of your favorite games! Any event you do in the room will earn you a ticket that you can jealously hoard until Sunday when we have our Grand Giveaway, full of prizes generously donated by our lovely sponsors, donors, and friends. 

Whether you're a seasoned pro or a you've never played a card game in your life, we'll have content for you! Come learn a game you have always wanted to play or one you've never even heard of. And best of all, every single thing in this room is FREE! So if you want to come hang out with your fellow con-goer, meet some new friends and learn some fun games along the way, our team of dedicated volunteers will be here all weekend to guide you through the wonderful world of cardboard.

DRAW PHASE is a 501(c)(3) Non-Profit Organization that provides TCG Content in the PNW at no cost to the attendee! If you're interested in learning more, ask a volunteer.

events.title: Opening Ceremonies Doors Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Grand Archive – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Welcome to the Archive, Champion – do you have what it takes to forestall an apocalypse?

Come find out and learn Grand Archive, a growing new TCG fusing anime aesthetics with the best of western game design! Along with your demo kit, all participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

All material graciously provided by Grand Archive’s developer Weebs of the Shore. Thank you for your continued support, WotS!

events.title: AMV History 2000+ and Fundamentals
events.panelDescription:
[Fan Panel] Presented by Chris Allen and Cheri Allen

Join us on a fast‑paced ride through the evolution of AMVs! Starting from the law-breaking chaos of the early 2000s to today’s high‑power, pro‑level creations. We will unpack the tech, tricks, and cultural turning points that shaped the AMV community. Whether you’re a first‑time editor or a seasoned master, you'll learn sharp insights on storytelling, audio, visual quality, and the tools that make modern AMVs shine.

events.title: Arcade Open Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming]

events.title: So You Want to Start a Cosplay Group
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Thinking about starting a cosplay group—or joining one—but not sure where to begin? This panel covers the basics of creating and running a cosplay group, from finding members and choosing themes to communication, planning meetups, and handling group dynamics and more.

events.title: Airbrush 101
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Learn the basics of airbrushing in a slideshow demo style lecture. Things including where to buy, how to clean and maintain, technics and dos and donts of care and maintenance.

events.title: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle! Integrating Sustainability with Cosplay
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Did you know textiles are the fastest growing type of waste in the country? This panel will give an overview of the problems surrounding textile waste by a recycling industry expert and explore how we can use creative approaches to incorporate sustainability into cosplay and crafting.

events.title: Embracing Cringe: Enjoying Media As Autistic
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] "Cringe"—the terrifying buzzword that criticizes a certain way of enjoying media and fandom that shares a lot of overlap with autistic traits. At this panel, we will examine the inherently ableist nature of "cringe" and why media/fandom are common comforts for those with autism.

events.title: Lets Talk About Cosplay and Character Design!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A panel for those who are interested in the inner workings of how to dissect an existing character design to make or redesign into a cosplay

events.title: So you wanna rig a VTuber? Waffo's Live2d Crash Course
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you been interested in making Live2D models like the ones your favorite VTuber uses? Join Waffo and Koko as they walk you through how to get started making models in Live2D Cubism! We'll cover everything you need to start your Live2D Cubism journey, from the initial sketch to the detailed physics!

events.title: Arts and Crafts Room Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: RPG Character Creation and Solo RPGs
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Learn to build an RPG character, or play one of the Alexandria RPG Library's collection of solo RPGs! Alexandria's crew of Librarians and GMs will be on hand to help you flesh out your character concept, or demonstrate RPG games that can be played entirely on your own.

events.title: Pathfinder Society: The Chitterwood Walks, Part 1: Scrambling the Tribes [PFS2 7-09]
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "When the longshanks of Isger start to gather troops, the goblins of the Chitterwood Forest get nervous. As an ally of the Chitterwood goblins, the Crookedtoes tribe has requested that the Pathfinder Society offer some protection during the discussions, making sure that the only fires and explosions that occur are from goblin hands!
---
Game Host: Mike
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Pathfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Alexandria RPG Library Presents: Classic, Modern & Indie RPGs
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Visit the Alexandria RPG Library and explore over a thousand RPG rulebooks, modules, and player aids from countless game systems. From classic D&D to indie darlings, from cyberpunk noir to magical pirates. We bring all the dice, maps, books, and supplies for you to play in or run a game.

Browse our books, roll up a character, or grab a ticket to join our events. Tickets for each day’s games are available in-person at the RPG Library (Arch Tower, Tahoma 4) at 10AM.

The Alexandria RPG Library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit based in Tacoma, Wash.

events.title: Board Game Library - Presented by Dragonflight Game Con
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Since 1980, Dragonflight Game Con has been introducing board game enthusiasts to a wide variety of games through their convention's extensive lending library of games. Now, you can experience that library here at Sakura-Con! Visit the Dragonflight Game Library in the Board and RPG Gaming Room, located on the Tahoma level of the Gaming Annex, to browse the collection of available games, check out a game, and play in the room's free-play area.

Dragonflight Game Con is a nonprofit, volunteer-run gaming convention that takes place every August in Bellevue, WA. Visit their website at https://dfgamecon.org/ to learn more.

events.title: Exhibits Hall Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Shogi lessons/Free play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come learn/play the ancient game of Go! Beginners very welcome

events.title: Shogi lessons/Free play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come learn/play the ancient game of Shogi! Beginners very welcome

events.title: Learn to play mahjong
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Learn to play Japanese riichi mahjong with one of our teachers!

events.title: Mahjong freeplay
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Play mahjong with other attendees at one of our many tables. An annual classic.

events.title: Opening Ceremonies
events.panelDescription: [Main Stage]

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: Mecha Modeling Room Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Photo Booth Room
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Scavenger Hunt Open
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] Have questions about this year's SakuraQuest Scavenger Hunt or ready to turn it in and get your pin? This is the place.

Go to sakuraquest.org or pick up a paper form here or at any Info Booth to get started.

events.title: Retro/Console Freeplay
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Check in at Console Gaming for live content and more all weekend!

events.title: Social Deception: Open-Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social Deception invites you to come play games from our collection! Self-guided, or run w/ staff help!

events.title: Social Deception: Open-Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social Deception invites you to come play games from our collection! Self-guided, or run w/ staff help!

events.title: Sonic: An Unfortunate History
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Sonic the Hedgehog is a staple holding together the childhood of any proper 90s kid, but just like Soap shoes he’s tragically out of fashion. We’ll be going over the history of Sonic The Hedgehog games, Sega Consoles, Crush 40, and various chili dogs.

events.title: Examining the Cultural Impact of One Piece
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Let's set sail for adventure and dive deep into analyzing the impacts of the cultural phenomenon that is One Piece. From its impact on media to the intersection of manga and real world politics, join the FWHS Anime/Manga Club to discuss why this formula has won out over a crowded field of Shonen manga and anime to reshape the medium.

events.title: Digimon Card Game – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Di-Di-Digimon! Digital Monsters, The Card Game! Come help to save the digital world and learn to play this rich card game that has captivated audiences since 2020! Change yourself into a digital champion and save the digital world. And after you're done digivolving, you'll receive a pack on us to start your journey and a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: Netrunner -- Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Presented by MTGPlatinum.com!

Come and enjoy a completely free, curated draft experience reaching through MTG history to provide you with an unpredictable experience you won’t find anywhere else

All Curated Chaos drafts are three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Netrunner -- Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come learn the Netrunner TCG directly from the team developing it! Null Signal Games has sent us their very best to teach you how to be the best Runner you can be! All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: Project Justice Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Rival Schools is back! Form teams, pull off team-up attacks, and settle high school drama the only way that matters: with your fists. Win prizes!

events.title: Project Justice Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The most unhinged fighting game you've never heard of. Absurd characters, chaotic gameplay, and more memes per minute than your Discord server. Win prizes!

events.title: When Anime Takes the Stage
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] What happens when our favorites leave the 2D world to enter our 3D space? We get the wonderful world of 2.5D. We’ll examine the origins of 2.5D and the many aspects that bring our favorite stories to the stage! This panel focuses specifically on adaptations for anime, manga, and video games for the stage. From the stage design to original songs and casting there’s so much to explore!

events.title: Warrior Cats Adventure Game: A Quest for Herbs
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Leaf-Bare is here, sooner than expected. There is not enough Cat Mint for all clans to use. Before fights break out StarClan has sent a message: There is enough Cat Mint for all but they must retrieve it in nearby Two-Leg territory. Each Clan must send one warrior and apprentice to make this trip. The trip seems simple but StarClan warns otherwise...
---
Game Host: NiiNya
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players 6 and older welcome)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: The Weird History of Fire Emblem Localizations
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] From the differences in gameplay to the OVA, Fire Emblem has had a weird history of localizations. Let's explore all of the wacky choices that Intelligent Systems has made and how it has affected localized audiences.

events.title: Idol Random Dance Play!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Get ready to dance your heart out at the idol Random Dance play! From Anisong, to Vtubers, Jpop and odottemita classics, you won’t wanna miss the chance to dance to your favorite idol media!

events.title: Lupin the 3rd: A Beginner's Guide
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] The thrilling criminal exploits of gentleman-thief Lupin III have stolen the hearts of viewers for over 55 years! Come find out more about this this long-running series with a tour of the anime's TV and movie highlights—from the groovy '70s, to 21st-century Toonami, to the theatrical Firsts—and learn where to start watching or explore next!

events.title: Out of Pocket (Monsters) Hot Takes!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Calling all Trainers, Masters, and Poké-enthusiasts! Join us for a lively, no-holds-barred panel where we dive into the most controversial, bold, and sometimes downright unpopular opinions from across the Pokémon fandom. From “Starter choices that got robbed” to “regions that deserve more love (or less)”, battle your strongest opinions with other trainers!

events.title: All the Feels
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Why settle for just one feels, when you can feel ALL the feels!

events.title: maimai DX Tournament - LOWERS
events.panelDescription: [Gaming]

events.title: Artist Alley Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Eyes on the Prize: Catseye Crown
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Eyes on the Prize is a collaborative game of court intrigue and fake dating, in which players assume the role of characters in a couple pretending to be very much in love in order to climb the social ladder of the court for personal gain: be that wealth, status, their families approval, or whatever other motive. Players enact a series of scenes to develop their characters, the relationship -however it turns, and to advance their goals. 

For this session, the backdrop of the drama is the courtiers, some legitimate, some less so, vying to be chosen as the successors to, or position themselves in advantageous relationship to, the hairless Catseye Queen. 

Will your character find wealth, power, friendship, failure, embarrassment, or even true love of one kind or another? Let's find out!
---
Game Host: Jack
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Cairn 2E: The Sinister Secret of Saltmarsh Part 1
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Desolate and abandoned, the evil alchemist's mansion stands alone on the cliff, looking out towards the sea. Mysterious lights and ghostly hauntings have kept away the people of Saltmarsh, despite rumors of a fabulous treasure. What is its sinister secret?
---
Game Host: Xander
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Deadlands: Savage Worlds Adventure Edition: Last Coach to Hallowed Ground
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "The posse is staying in a frontier town when an empty, bullet-riddled stagecoach rolls in at dawn. By noon, the graveyard bell rings on its own. The dead begin to stir—not to rise, but to hunt!
---
Game Host: Jay
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Shadow Scar: Syndicate Soiree
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Come join the creator of the Shadow Scar TTRPG for a night of fun and festivity as you enter the Hollow Eye Masquerade! Important people from across the multiverse have been gathered together for this once in a lifetime soiree thrown by the head of the Hollow Eye Crime Syndicate, Malacoda. As ninja agents of the secretive Shadow Scar Agency you've been sent to infiltrate the party and find out what the Hollow Eye Syndicate is planning with all these important people. While you're there you'll also have to keep your eyes peeled. Intelligence suggests that you won't be the only curious party. The monstrous yokai might be sending their own agents to kidnap the guests or worse.

---
Game Host: Cody Pondsmith 
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Charity Auction Viewing
events.panelDescription: [Charity Auction]

events.title: Dreamland Maid Cafe
events.panelRoom: Dreamland Maid Cafe - Elliot Bay Room
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Plurality in Anime and Real Life
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] The notion of multiple people sharing a body has always been intriguing to storytellers, but many people aren't aware that plurality isn't fictional! Japanese media in has depicted plurality in various forms, so we'll be taking a look at what's accurate, what's not, and what we love anyway.

events.title: Sakurumble Doors Open
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage]

events.title: Weiss Schwarz - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Have you ever wanted every Anime ever made in one TCG? Well then you'll love Weiss Schwarz! Come relive climactic battles and moments from your favorite Anime and learn a mechanically deep TCG while you're at it! Hosted by our resident Anime TCG Expert, you'll be guided through your first game and then be on your way to being a champion! All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

events.title: One Piece TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Obtain Fame, Wealth, and Power beyond your wildest dreams by coming to the TCG Room and learning to play the One Piece TCG! Will you be able to find the buried treasure- Or perhaps the treasure is actually the friendships you formed in your journey? No matter what, if you complete a learn to play session you will receive a ticket for our Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: Pokemon TCG – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Sure, maybe you can identify all the original 151 just by their silhouettes, sing every word of “Gotta Catch ‘Em All” to yourself in the shower, and have a full shiny living ‘dex on Pokémon HOME - but if you really want to be the Champion, you have to be ready to take on all comers, anytime, anywhere… even at the card table.

That’s where we come in! Swing by and learn to play from some of the best trainers around, courtesy of Tabletop Village’s elite roster.

All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway – so start your climb to the top here, and aim to become the very best. You know it’s your destiny."

events.title: Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Arc System Works' gorgeous anime graphics meets Granblue's beloved characters. Easy to learn, deep to master, and perfect for settling your crew debates. Top 3 win prizes!

events.title: Danmei 101: Pronunciation Guide
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you been meaning to start learning Chinese? Have you ever wondered how to say "Shizun!" while reading your favorite danmei? Ever wanted to talk about your favorite characters, but didn't know how to say their names out loud? Come on by and learn the basics of Mandarin pronunciation and aid your danmei journey!

events.title: Fic Binding: Bringing fanfiction from screen to page through bookbinding
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] We love fanfiction. We love books. So why not turn fanfiction into books? Learn all about the idea of and how to take your favorite fanfictions and bind them into proper books that you can actually hold in your names and show off in your bookcases. From one-shot quick binds to multi-chaptered hardbound novels, let’s get started.

events.title: Talking About Writing Talking: How to Improve Your Dialogue
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] "Writing dialogue is the worst!" she said, flopping down into her chair. "I can never get it to sound natural. Why do characters have to talk to each other so much!?" Does this sound like you? You're not alone! Join us for a discussion about writing dialogue - what to think about, how to find character voice, what to cut and how to use dialogue to effectively convey information and move your plot and character relationships forward.

events.title: Extremely Hard Take-Home Anime Quiz, Introduction
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Meet the panelist on Friday. The purpose is to answer questions participants may have about the rules and help clarify questions. No hints will be provided. Attendance at this panel is not necessary for participation.

events.title: Level Up Your Cosplay Sewing!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you a beginner to intermediate cosplay sewist wondering how to improve and polish the quality of your stitched cosplays? Come and learn easy tips and tricks to level up your projects with various cutting/marking, stitching, pressing, and finishing techniques.

events.title: The Fall of the House of Miyazaki
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Hayao Miyazaki did a lot of work to make anime acceptable to mainstream audiences. Not by creating high quality art, but by making something so bad that everything else looked quality in comparison.

events.title: Infrastructure of Japan
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Buildings, roads, power and urban planning are all apart of what makes as civilization function. Come and learn about some of the unique projects that make Japan work.

events.title: Symbology Of Japanese Textiles 2.0
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Every stitch tells a story. In The Symbology of Japanese Textiles 2.0, we’ll explore the meanings woven into traditional kimono and obi. These symbols aren’t just decorative; in anime, character outfits often use traditional patterns to signal personality traits, backstories, and hidden narrative clues. Join me as we uncover how textile symbolism enriches Japanese culture and storytelling, adding depth, identity, and intention to everyday garments and beloved characters alike.

events.title: Code-Switching No Jutsu: Navigating Fandom Spaces While Black
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Therapist AC Mateen knows what it’s like to love anime and have to shift your whole vibe depending on the room you’re in. This panel unpacks what it means to be Black in fandom... the pride, the exhaustion, the inside jokes that save us. We’ll talk about why we shrink ourselves sometimes, how that shows up even in our cosplay choices, and how to reclaim our joy without apology. Expect laughs, honesty, and maybe a little group healing (the anime kind, not the awkward trust-fall kind).

events.title: Fabula Ultima TTJRPG: PRESS START Tutorial: A Fated Descent
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "The award-winning Fabula Ultima is designed to recreate the wondrous worlds, characters and stories seen in videogames such as the legendary Final Fantasy series. In this BEGINNER FRIENDLY introductory adventure, set out on an airship to investigate a massive magic crater, the site of a mysterious, city razing arcane disaster.
---
Game Host: Andrew Harshman
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Maid: The Roleplaying Game: Luck Be a Meido Tonight: Casino Chaos!
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Total maidness awaits as you try to make your master's casino grand opening a winner, even as bizarre random events throw everything into chaos.

It's grand opening night, the culmination of your master's fondest wish: a casino themed after, and wholly staffed by, maids just like you. And they're betting it all that you'll be the crew that makes it a resounding winner! And if you can hit the jackpot and win the master's favor for your performance? Then you'll be the one to join them at the inaugural all-you-can-eat buffet, catered by your fellow maids.

Alas, lady luck's decided to leave everything up to chance. Fire in the kitchen? Long-forgotten ghosts? Love dodecahedrons? A heist run by aliens? A time vortex leading to a tragic past? Surprise costume upgrades? And come to think of it, isn't the master themself even an embodiment of random chance?
---
Game Host: Saoirse
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: 18+ (vulgar humor, sexual references, slapstick violence)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served.

events.title: Shadow Scar: The Final Test
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Come begin your journey into the multiverse as a ninja by…taking your graduation exam? You've trained long enough and you are ready to prove yourself. So come join your classmates as you try to pass the test to become agents working in the field to defeat the yokai plotting to destroy humanity. 
---
Game Host: Tarabella
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Mature/18+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Voice Acting Secrets with Cassandra Lee Morris
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Cassandra Lee Morris has voiced over 200 roles in video games, anime and animation. How'd she get there? What's the secret behind breaking into voice acting? How the heck does she create all those voices? Cassandra answers your questions and more in this one-on-one panel.

events.title: Cosplay Wrestling Federation: Sakurumble VI! - Day 1
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage] The CWF, THE MOST ELECTRIFYING show in Anime Sports Entertainment, returns to the Pacific Northwest at Sakura-Con to proudly present to you SAKURUMBLE as a two-day event! This show will feature wrestlers of the CWF from franchises big and small, young and old as they clash to settle grudges, vie for superiority, defend their pride, and more, in a series of (contactless) exhibition-style battles of mic skills, bravado, and possibly shenanigans! Who will stand tall and who will tumble, in the sixth annual Sakurumble?! Only you can decide! Come join us to not only watch, but with your cheers and jeers steer the course of this exciting new installment of C! W! F!

events.title: Kpop PC trading
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you looking for that last card to finish your collection? Or maybe you just love trading PCs of your bias. Either way, we may have what you need! Come and trade photocards with other kpop fans or maybe just come and hang out. All are welcome!

events.title: Grandpa's AMV Stash
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] The old farts running this thing have been AMV fans since long before you were a twinkle in your daddy's eye. Come see what was popular when they were your age, along with a few cloud-shouting rambles on what the community was like back in the "good ol' days!"

events.title: D&D 5e (2024): Uni and the Hunt for the Lost Horn
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Uni the unicorn is in trouble. The evil spellcaster Kelek has stolen Uni’s horn to power a fell ritual. If the characters don’t stop Kelek, Uni’s horn will be destroyed forever in Kelek’s quest for power.

Uni and the Hunt for the Lost Horn is part of a yearlong celebration of Dungeons & Dragons and its 50th anniversary. The adventure presented herein involves characters from the beloved 1980s Dungeons & Dragons animated series.

This adventure is designed for four to six level 4 characters. Accompanying the adventure are character sheets for six of the animated series’ protagonists: Bobby, Diana, Eric, Hank, Presto, and Sheila, all presented in young adulthood. The seventh character sheet presents Niko, a Cleric from a different set of adventurers who recently tumbled into the D&D multiverse. Each player can choose one of these characters or provide a character of their own.
---
Game Host: Joseph 
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Learn ParaPara Dance
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Come learn parapara! A Japanese style of synchronized dance commonly done to eurobeat music. Easy for beginners to pick up and and a lot of fun for more advanced dancers as well!

events.title: Brittney Karbowski AMA
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Naoya Nakayama Director's Panel Rhythm, Motion, and Emotion
events.panelDescription:
[Guest of Honor] Join us for the visual mastery behind YOASOBI’s “Idol” music video, and live performance direction on UMA MUSUME PRETTY DERBY: NEW ERA'S DOOR, dance animation for KAGUYA-SAMA: LOVE IS WAR, and ending sequences for OSHI NO KO!
Naoya is especially skilled at condensing story and emotional intensity into short runtimes, and is highly regarded as a creator who balances pop appeal with visual beauty. He is currently active across multiple fields, including music videos, ending animations, and live performance direction.
In this panel, Nakayama will discuss his creative process, approach to character performance, how he designs animation around music and timing, and what goes into directing short-form and music-centered animation. From pop spectacle to delicate acting moments, discover how he compresses story and energy into every frame.

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: The Seattle Oka Sho: Umamusume Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Bring your whole stable as we compete in a variety of races to see who the top trainer is! Top places will receive a trophy.

events.title: The Kompany Dance Workshop
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] An in-depth dance workshop open to all, featuring choreography from a popular K-pop song. The workshop will be taught by a Dance Captain from the University of Washington’s first and only K-pop dance team, The Kompany.

events.title: "Isekai": Japanese VS Korean VS Chinese
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join us in exploring the similarities and differences of the isekai (/isegye/transmigration) genre in Japanese, Korean, and Chinese media! This panel will cover various manga, anime, manhwa, manhua, webnovels, and some live actions.

events.title: Cardfight Vanguard!! Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Ever wanted to try out an Anime card game known for being easy to learn and incredibly fun to master? Cardfight Vanguard!! is right up your alley then! Come and play with one of our Starter Decks and be guided through a game by one of our volunteers! Complete a Learn to Play session and receive a ticket for the Sunday Giveaway!

events.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Free Standard Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come and enjoy a completely free Draft experience at Sakura-Con!All drafts are three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway, as well as one pack per win!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Free Standard Tournament
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Welcome, Duelist! Here's something a bit more modern for you – a Standard Format Yu-Gi-Oh Tournament. Come over to the TCG Room and prove you're the best duelist at Sakura-Con (besides Kaiba, of course) and put your skills on display. Eager to show off your Ryzeal deck? Or maybe you're still playing Tenpai Dragon? – No matter what you're playing, we encourage you to stop by and win some prizes.

Three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway, as well as one pack per win!

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Two loaner decks are available upon request (First come first serve) upon request as part of your reservation. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Japanese Tea Ceremony
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Presentation of Japanese culture through Japanese Tea Ceremony demonstration and lecture.

events.title: Cut it Out! An AMV Masking Deep Dive
events.panelDescription:
[Fan Panel] Presented by CMDR Sariah and Winterblade AMVs

Do you have incredible ideas for crossover AMVs? Want to smooth out your transitions and create a sense of flow? Looking for a new technical skill to add to your AMV editing toolkit? If so, then this panel will teach you everything you need to know about it.

events.title: Um, Actually: Con Edition
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Nerds like a lot of things, but above all else, they love correcting people! This is Um, Actually! Do you know a lot of nerdy facts about media and nerd culture? Do you want to test your nerdy knowledge for the chance to win a prize? Then come join us for another round of Um, Actually! Based on the DropOut TV show, contestants will be tasked with correcting pedantically incorrect for the chance to win points. Whoever has the most points by the end of each round will win, and claim a prize. There will be three rounds, each of which is themed based on the kinds of statements need corrected (e.g. Western Media, Japanese Media, Gaming, etc.) And the best part yet? Audience members are encouraged to participate! So come and join us!

events.title: Omegaverse from the 1980’s to AO3
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ever wondered how we got here? What about the fics and earlier tropes along the way? Their Tropes Battled for Dominance will take you through time as we take a look at some of our favorite recommendations of our fandom history. Come check out fics from the 80s and research published in scientific journals on Omegaverse history!

events.title: Branding as a Small Artist
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Artists, creatives, and aspiring creators of all backgrounds are welcome to spend an hour with a public relations professional to learn the basic necessities of developing a personal brand to elevate your reach to the next level. This panel will condense brand development 101 into a short presentation, created by a small artist for small artists. Learn about brand equity, developing audience personas, and much more.

events.title: The Legend of Zelda: A Link to a Panel
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] For forty years The Legend of Zelda has invited gamers to a world of adventure and wonder. Celebrate The Legend of Zelda's 40th Anniversary with a review of the games, the lore, and everything that makes this franchise so beloved.

events.title: Cosplay is for EVERY body, A plus size cosplay panel
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join me as we dive into the beautiful world of body diversity in cosplay! We will discuss practical ways to level up your fitting skills, ways to alter prebought cosplays, and photography tipsthat help you be seen exactly as you want to be seen. Cosplay is for EVERY body, so let's talk about it.

events.title: Forged in Plastic: Flexible 3D Printing and the Art of Cosplay Printing
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ever wondered how cosplayers and makers create armor that moves with them — not against them? Join Ann Grace Hub, a nonprofit makerspace in Bremerton, WA, for a deep dive into flexible 3D printing and how modern materials are reshaping what’s possible in costume design and functional fabrication. This session walks through the process of designing and printing scale armor and wearable pieces using flexible filaments like TPU and hybrid blends. We’ll cover the fundamentals — from printer settings and pattern slicing to assembly methods and heat-forming — and share insights learned from teaching these techniques in our community classes. Whether you’re a cosplayer, engineer, or curious maker, you’ll see how affordable printers and open-source models can help you build armor that bends, breathes, and battles con floors with style. Presented by Ann Grace Hub, a  nonprofit dedicated to empowering creativity through access to fabrication tools and education.

events.title: The Best of the West
events.panelDescription:
[Fan Panel] Presented by Tyken

AMV usually means Anime Music Video.  But Animation in the West (really anywhere besides Japan) hasn't just been sitting still behind the behemoth that is the Anime industry. There have been some truly unique and amazing Western cartoons in the past two decades.

events.title: Vocaloid Name That Tune!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Think you know your Vocaloid? Come test your knowledge of vocal synth music, new and old! There’s something for everybody, whether you got your start with Colorful Stage or you've been a Miku fan since the days of melody.exe. Audience members can compete in various rounds, with prizes for the winner of each round!

events.title: Speed Friending
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Looking for a con buddy? Expand your friend circle with speed friending! Making friends as an adult is hard, and the Seattle freeze is real. Whether you’re attending alone and looking for a nerdy bff, or with a group and looking for more buds, we’ll explore the common ground you have just by being a con goer and more. Who knows, you could meet a great weekend friend or a friend for life!

events.title: Pathfinder Society: The Chitterwood Walks, Part 1: Scrambling the Tribes [PFS2 7-09]
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "When the longshanks of Isger start to gather troops, the goblins of the Chitterwood Forest get nervous. As an ally of the Chitterwood goblins, the Crookedtoes tribe has requested that the Pathfinder Society offer some protection during the discussions, making sure that the only fires and explosions that occur are from goblin hands!
---
Game Host: Steven S
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Pathfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Pathfinder Society: The Chitterwood Walks, Part 2: The Battle of Logas [PFS2 7-12]
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "With the success of the Chitterwood Talks, the goblin tribes are ready to march on Logas and put a stop to the undead attacks, with support from the Pathfinder Society. As forces approach the city, rumors start to circle of a strange plague only affecting Hellknights, leaving the undead Hellknights covered in bizarre, circular scars and bearing the mark of an unknown order. Within Logas, a Hellknight necromancer has declared herself the head of a new Hellknight order and is using the city as her chance to prove its worth. Can the Pathfinders stand up to The Order of the Open Tomb?
---
Game Host: Mike
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Pathfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: JoJo Pose off! With Bill Butts
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Come dressed in your best JoJo cosplay and compete against other cosplayers in a JoJo Pose-Off set to the beat of JoJo intros, judged by Bill Butts! Cosplay not required but highly recommended. The winner gets 3 free prints of their choosing.

events.title: Blue Encount Q&A
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Two Rooms and a Boom
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social deception game of hidden roles and deduction, players are divided into 2 different rooms. Find your teammates, establish trust, and exchange hostages before time runs out and the bomb explodes!

events.title: Two Rooms and a Boom
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social deception game of hidden roles and deduction, players are divided into 2 different rooms. Find your teammates, establish trust, and exchange hostages before time runs out and the bomb explodes!

events.title: Love Live! Over the Rainbow Performance
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Strawberry Encore presents: a Love Live! Sunshine trivia and dance panel! We will be performing fan-favorite idol dances from the Over the Rainbow movie and presenting prizes for those who can answer our Aqours trivia.

events.title: Intro to Vtubing and Vtuber Culture
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ever wonder how to get started in vtubing or who are those anime people performing at anime concerts or talking to people at the con through the tv and ipad? Come swing by to learn about vtubers and vtuber culture!

events.title: League of Family Feud!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Come compete against fellow League of Legends players, Arcane enjoyers, and overall gamers in this League of Legends themed Family Feud! Which Arcane character didn't deserve what happened to them? How big of a loss streak do you have to go on before you go nonverbal? Why would a League player go outside? Find out the answers to all of these questions at this panel! Fun prizes for those who participate!

events.title: Echoes of Astra - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] This one's new! Echoes of Astra is a brand-new TCG from Parhelion Studios, an anime art themed game that mixes traditional TCG Gameplay with the tactical depth of war gaming! All wrapped in the package of some beautiful cardboard. If you want to learn a brand new TCG that is just starting this is your chance to get in on the ground floor! All participants will receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!

All our materials for Echoes of Astra were graciously donated by Parhelion Studios, so a big thank you to them for allowing us to show their game to you. Come over to the TCG Room and one of our experienced volunteers will teach you all about this tactically rich, exciting new game!

events.title: Digimon Card Game – Free Sealed Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] I've run out of Digimon puns and lyrics, so unfortunately you'll just have to settle with a Sealed tournament for free! Come visit us in the TCG Room and play a round of sealed Digimon on the house, complete with prizes and a ticket to all entries for our Grand Giveaway on Sunday!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Galaxy Fight (One Second Timer) Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Classic arcade fighter with a twist—rounds have a 1-second timer! Adapt or perish in this high-speed cosmic clash. Can you win before time runs out? Win prizes!

events.title: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate Tournament 1
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Everyone is here! Bring your main and prove they're top tier. First of four brackets, so there's plenty of chances to shine. Win prizes!

events.title: Intro to World Fusion Belly Dance
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you ever wanted to shimmy and shake until you couldn’t any more? Move your body in a creative way to express yourself for skits or just in general? Look no further! Join Eekie and learn some of the basics of belly dance. No prior experience necessary!

events.title: Dungeons & Disappointment: Stop Rescheduling, Start Playing
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Cancelled another game session? Added another half-finished campaign notebook to the stack? We say no more! Come join us and identify keys for building a strong TTRPG table from both the GM and player perspectives. Learn tips to run and play in consistent, efficient, and engaging games. Receive resources for supporting scheduling, pushing plot, and crafting memorable (and complete) campaigns. Systems highlighted: D&D, Kids on Bikes, Obojima, Path Finder, and Call of Cthulhu.

events.title: So You Want to Enter an AMV Contest?
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] With so many AMV contests out there with floods of entries, it can be turbulent and sometimes even confusing to navigate the high seas of submission rules. Come on board and join our crew of AMV editors turned AMV Contest Coordinators as they provide tips and techniques to help steer you in the right direction. Oh, and questions are always welcome!

events.title: The Good Fan Etiquette Guide To Recommending Anime
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] The Good Fan Etiquette Guide To Recommending Anime is the must-see panel for any anime fan who needs a comedic how-to guide on how to recommend anime to their friends, family, loved ones, and even strangers they meet out in the world. From explaining the patented VAULTELF system of recommendation consideration to applying it to real-world examples. This guide is your one-stop shop to navigating the fraught world of recommending anime to those new to the genre.

events.title: Moriguchika Autograph
events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.title: Moriguchika Autograph
events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.title: D&D 5e (2024): Eight Kolbolds and a Baby (dragon): An Intro adventure
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "You have been hired to find and return the egg of a friendly copper dragon.
 A band of kobolds recently stole said egg and brought it to a nearby cave to eat. But when the egg hatched, and a screeching dragon wyrmling emerged, the kobolds quickly realized they bit off more than they could chew. The kobolds then tried to appease the fussy wyrmling with treasure, but they just can’t agree on what to do next. Should they return the wyrmling to its nest? Should they raise it themselves? They don't know what to do! The choice of how best to handle these draconic thieves? Now that is up to you! 
This is a lighthearted intro meant to take 100-110 minutes and give a taste of most D&D mechanics
---
Game Host: Courtney V
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players age 6 and up)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Hiroshi Nagahama Q&A
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Join Hiroshi Nagahama for an open Q&A session! With experience spanning animation, design, and episode direction, he’s excited to talk about the craft and share insights from across the production process. He’d also love to hear your thoughts and impressions on the works he’s been involved with.

events.title: Umamusume Osaka Hai Fan Festival!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Horse girls are coming to celebrate the JRA's annual Osaka Hai! Join us to test your wit training with Umamusume trivia and form teams to compete in mini races! Compete in your very own Osaka Hai by participating in an in-game Room Match!!

events.title: Cosplay on a Budget!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] This panel will teach you how to elevate your cosplay for less. Whether you want to make your own costumes or alter premade ones, we have tips and tricks for cosplaying cost-effectively. There’s no need for fancy tools or expensive materials — from wigs, props, costumes, and more, we have you (and your wallet) covered!

events.title: Cracking Open a Cold One With The Catboys | Your Guide to Fishing in Final Fantasy 14
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Fellow Warriors of Light, you've traveled to far off lands, fought foes threatening the very fabric of this star, striving endlessly for ever greater heights. Perhaps you seek a moment of respite, a quiet, peaceful opportunity to take it slow and drink in the sights. Just you, your rod, the crisp summer morning air, the sun cresting over the horizon, an alarm clock going off at 4 AM, an unreasonable number of spreadsheets, and a deep seated feeling of torment and despair. You stare into the deep inky waters. You see nothing but an abyss. You see a reflection of yourself. Fishing is not for the faint of heart, but together we can chart a course through these treacherous and terrible waters. Welcome to the True Endgame. Fishing has Raidplans too. This panel will not contain story spoilers, but will contain references to locations visited through the most recent expansion, Dawntrail. --- BORN TO FISH WORLD IS A LAKE Reel Em All 2025 I am FSH main 410,757,864,530 CAUGHT FISH

events.title: Getting Started with Content Creation
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you looking to started with content creation and live-streaming and don't know where to start? Join experienced content creators from the Seattle Online Broadcasters Association with experience in gaming live-streams, vtubing, creative and artistic content, and charity streaming for a question and answer session to answer any question you may have!

events.title: When Manga and Music Collide
events.panelDescription:
[Fan Panel] Presented by Violet Skies

Have you ever wanted to see your favorite manga come to life? Witness characters leap off the page as we explore the incredible artistry of Manga Music Videos.  With custom rigging, animation and color, we can even pretend that our favorite manga got the anime we knew they deserved!

events.title: Intellectual Property for Creators
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] This panel provides a comprehensive introduction to Intellectual Property (IP) for creators, including artists, writers, designers, and freelancers seeking to pick up the basics to support the creative industry. Explore the fundamentals of copyrights, trademarks, and other IP rights to understand how they protect your work. Learn practical strategies for safeguarding your creative assets, addressing infringement, navigating licensing, and fair use. Gain the knowledge and tools needed to protect your rights and ensure your creative work remains secure.

events.title: Whose Initiative is It Anyway?
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join the S.Rangers on an epic adventure that we are making up on the spot. Our talented cast of improvisational artists are ready to delight you in a live D&D session made of suggestions from you. Audience participation is a must. So come and join us for a panel of big dice rolls and big laughs.

events.title: Call of Cthulhu : Poison Soup (Doku-iri Sūpu)
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "After going to sleep in your bed, you awake in the night to an unnerving scene. You find your self in a square room surrounded by strangers and in the center is a lone desk and chair. On the desk sits a bowl of bright red soup and a note that reads, ""If you want to leave, you have one hour to drink the poison soup, or you will be collected..."" Will you escape with your lives? Find out in this scenario straight from Japan from their number one TTRPG, Call of Cthulhu! New players welcome, pregens provided. 
---
Game Host: AJ
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: 18+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Mahjong in anime and media
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Join us as we go through some of the best Mahjong scenes in anime and other media!  From classics like Akagi to modern shows like Pon no Michi, and even Hollywood blockbusters like Crazy Rich Asians, we'll analyze the quality of the Mahjong and maybe help you find a new show to watch!

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: Rhythm Gaming Freeplay
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Get your groove in with up to three other players in Just Dance, mimic silhouettes to iconic pop hits! Featuring other rhythm surprises as well!

events.title: Steel sword fighting through Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA)
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] A showcase of Historical European Martial Arts, a study of various sword arts dating from 14th century all the way to 17th and 18th century. Realistically weighted steel training weapon simulators are used alongside protective gear to allow for as close to sword fighting in its purest form. Weapons include: longsword, rapier, sword & buckler, and saber.

events.title: Intro to Improv
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Welcome to the world of improv comedy. We'll be teaching you some of our tips and tricks to come up with your best comedy skills with a series of sketches. A small lecture throughout as well. Come join us for all the fun and hilarity.

events.title: Flesh and Blood - Free Silver Age Precon Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Step into the arena with FREE ready-to-play Silver Age preconstructed decks, officially sponsored by LSS. Experience the thrill of strategic 1v1 combat while making new friends in a fun and welcoming tournament setting! This event is a great chance to learn your first hero or try out a new class.All participants also receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Vampire Savior Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Darkstalkers 3, baby! Fast-paced vampire hunters vs. creatures of the night. Chain those combos and show everyone why this is the hypest Capcom fighter. Win prizes!

events.title: K-Pop Demon Hunters Sing-Along!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] K-Pop Demon Hunters was the dazzling sensation of 2025! The music was chart-topping! Whether you saw this in the theater and sang along or not, the fun shouldn't be left behind last year because who hasn't had enough of listening and singing along to the soundtrack of K-Pop Demon Hunters? Come join this sing-along panel if you want sing these songs even more-more-more!

events.title: Love & Goonspace
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Do you enjoy fantastical stories about pretty princes, mesmerizing mermen, dazzling dragons, romantic robots, and glorious gods? Join us in a Love & Deepspace themed panel that will dive in to everything related to the popular otome mobile game. There will be trivia, games and wonderful prizes. Hope to see you there!

events.title: So, You think it's a Sports Anime...It's Actually a BL
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you ever watched a sports anime and just thought "those two boys should kiss?" Well, we are right there with you! Come join us as we dissect popular sports anime and come to the conclusion that,"yes, they really SHOULD kiss."

events.title: The Art of Lip Sync
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Learn the fundamentals of lip sync performance from start to finish. This panel covers the application process, how to get started with lip syncing, and essential on-stage tips to help you perform with confidence and connect with your audience. Perfect for beginners and anyone looking to level up their lip sync skills.

events.title: That's not how that works
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] If you've ever crashed out over a character design not making any sense, you aren't alone! Join me as I roast some character designs but also offer solutions and some tricks to working around these crazy impossible characters so you can make the cosplays of your dreams.

events.title: Studio Orange Autograph
events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.title: Studio Orange Autograph
events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.title: Alexandria RPG Library Presents: Classic, Modern & Indie RPGs
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Visit the Alexandria RPG Library and explore over a thousand RPG rulebooks, modules, and player aids from countless game systems. From classic D&D to indie darlings, from cyberpunk noir to magical pirates. We bring all the dice, maps, books, and supplies for you to play in or run a game.

Browse our books, roll up a character, or grab a ticket to join our events. Tickets for each day’s games are available in-person at the RPG Library (Arch Tower, Tahoma 4) at 10AM.

The Alexandria RPG Library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit based in Tacoma, Wash.

events.title: Board Game Library - Presented by Dragonflight Game Con
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Since 1980, Dragonflight Game Con has been introducing board game enthusiasts to a wide variety of games through their convention's extensive lending library of games. Now, you can experience that library here at Sakura-Con! Visit the Dragonflight Game Library in the Board and RPG Gaming Room, located on the Tahoma level of the Gaming Annex, to browse the collection of available games, check out a game, and play in the room's free-play area.

Dragonflight Game Con is a nonprofit, volunteer-run gaming convention that takes place every August in Bellevue, WA. Visit their website at https://dfgamecon.org/ to learn more.

events.title: Jill Harris Q&A
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Nerds doing science
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you ever realized that a lot of people with nerdy hobbies also happen to have nerdy jobs? Join our panel of scientists, engineers, and other STEM professionals who also happen to do cosplay, comedy, or nerdy art! Learn how they combine their professional life with their side-gigs or hobbies, how those two sometimes intersect, and leave inspired to use your creativity across all aspects of your life!

events.title: Zapp's Spaceship of Love
events.panelDescription: [Main Stage] 2025 sucked. We all know it; it was basically the worst year ever. However, like any bold, tanned leader with blond wispy hair, our beloved Captain heard this claim and promptly responded: "Hold my Slurm®." Captain Zapp has thus established the "Department About Ruining Everything", and needs the worst, most poorly trained group of individuals imaginable to run it into the ground. Think you're the worst person for the job and will only make the world a worse place than it already is? Come in, apply, and if you're right, we'll give you the job on the spot!

events.title: Super Happy Fun Time: Photo Scavenger Hunt - Summit
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Do you want to take photos with your friends or enemies? Come join the SHFT Ladies in their Photo Scavenger Hunt - Summit location, where you will be tasked to take selfies and photos for points! This game requires a smart phone with access to the internet.

events.title: Magic: the Gathering – Free Curated Chaos Draft
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Presented by MTGPlatinum.com!

Come and enjoy a completely free, curated draft experience reaching through MTG history to provide you with an unpredictable experience you won’t find anywhere else

All Curated Chaos drafts are three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: AMV Creators Showcase
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Meet the editors behind your favorite AMVs!

events.title: In My Y2K Era: Millennials and Fandom
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Setting up your VCR to record the next episode of Sailor Moon, reading manga at the bookstore, sub and dub wars, what is Baka Con anyway? Join "The Fangirl Diaries" author and fandom lore archivist, Erica V. Espejo as we look back at an era of anime fandom before streaming and social media.

events.title: Examining the Cross-pollination of Anime and Gaming
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] In an age where gaming and anime are both fording the zeitgeist into the mainstream, influences and subcultures intersect! Join the FWHS Anime/Manga Club in a historical review of the influence of our fandom on trends in the gaming industry and wild speculation over where we can go from here!

events.title: Smokey Cheesecake
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] 2025 was a hot year with even hotter desserts! Come watch some fire AMVs with sweet and tasty breaks in between.

events.title: Score Attack! - Sonic Blast Heroes, Strongest Punch
events.panelDescription: [Gaming]

events.title: Undergarments for Cosplay
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ever struggle with wearing your cosplay, and the issue isn't the costume itself, but what you are wearing underneath it? Veteran cosplayer Fabrickind gives a crash course on undergarments for cosplay, including temperature control, body morphing, and hiding what you don't want showing.

events.title: Yakuza Cult Classics: Three Films by Seijun Suzuki
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Director Seijun Suzuki (1923–2017) took yakuza b-movies to surreal and absurdist heights that continue to enthrall audiences and inspire moviemakers worldwide. Get a primer on this midcentury expressionist with three of his films: "Youth of the Beast" (1963), "Tokyo Drifter" (1966), and "Branded to Kill" (1967).

events.title: Break!!RPG: A Completely Normal Adventure
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "The peaceful domain of Millbrook faces its darkest hour. A monstrous beast stalks the woods, leaving destruction in its wake. The just Lord Vance grieves for his kidnapped daughter, Princess Helena, taken from her very tower. His scheming Vizier whispers plots in shadowed halls while a mad street prophet warns of apocalyptic doom. Anarchists stir in the taverns, an orphan child claims mythic power, and a traveling merchant dealing in secrets watches it all unfold with knowing eyes. 

You've been called to save the princess, slay the beast, and restore order to this troubled land. It should be simple, every hero knows this story. But as you peel back the layers of Millbrook's tragedy, you'll find things are not as they seem. 

Even monsters, they say, can love.

Expect twists, moral complexity, and the chance to either prevent-or survive-a spectacular disaster.
---
Game Host: Mia
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Kpop Fun and Games
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] If you are a fan of Kpop and all things related then please join us for fun games like dance charades, guess the song, and more! All who wish to participate are welcome!

events.title: Dreamland Maid Cafe
events.panelRoom: Dreamland Maid Cafe - Elliot Bay Room

events.title: Mallorie Rodak Q&A
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Karaoke Idol Round 1
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Social Deception: Tasters
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social Deception variety hours! Quick fast-paced games! A variety of choice! Experience a broad swath of Deception games you could play at home!

events.title: Vocaloid Song Trivia!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Come test your knowledge on Vocaloid and Vsynth songs in this Jeopardy-style trivia game! We've got a variety of different songs and genres to suit the tastes of all fans, whether you're a recent listener or a veteran! With songs ranging from the classics to the exciting new ones, you'll always have a chance to win a prize!

events.title: 18+ Cosplay Skit Contest Doors Open
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage]

events.title: Weiss Schwarz - Free Neo-Standard Tournament
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Riftbound! A TCG based off of the world of League of Legends, but this time in physical card form! A brand new game from UVS Games & Riot Games, Riftbound provides League of Legends gameplay without the searing rage of going 0/8/3 on Summoner's Rift. Unfortunately when your opponent defeats you however, you will not be able to brush it off as 'support diff'. On the other hand, if you win you experience the joy that can only be obtained from getting 22 kills as Garen while the enemy top laner prays for the surrender vote to succeed.

All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

Disclaimer: Riftbound is very new, extremely popular, and impossible to get product for, so unfortunately we will not have anything extra to send you home with, sorry!

events.title: Weiss Schwarz - Free Neo-Standard Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Did you remember to bring your Weiss deck to con? If so, we think you'll love this Neo-Standard format tournament. Come on down to the TCG room and battle it out with other players to prove you're the best Weiss Schwarz player at Sakura-Con - and win some prizes, too! All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

events.title: Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Terry Bogard is back! SNK's legendary franchise returns with new mechanics and classic characters. Are you okay? BUSTAH WOLF! Win prizes!

events.title: UNDER NIGHT IN-BIRTH II Sys:Celes Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] French-Bread's cult classic fighting game with the sickest music and deepest lore. GRD management, stylish combos, and absolute cinema. Top 3 win prizes!

events.title: Sketchbook Swap
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] It’s time to whip out our sketchbooks and doodle! We’ll be drawing in timed chunks, then handing our sketchbook to our neighbor. Draw something for your fellow con attendees, and get some wonderful drawings in exchange! Bring your sketchbook and favorite supplies, or don’t—we’ll have spares! Any skill level is welcome, so come stop by if you’d like to give some art, and get some art!

events.title: Using J-fashion in Cosplay
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A J-fashion lovers guide to more sustainable cosplay! Members of the PDX J-Fashion community are here to share tips and tricks on how to combine the two and expand your wardrobe for both at the same time! We will talk about our own character selection process, how we analyze character design to keep them recognizable, and how combining your personal style with cosplay can help in reducing overconsumption.

events.title: Compliment Dueling
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Tired of trolls, fighting in the comments, and insult culture? Want to make your conversational openers top-notch? Join us as we discuss the art of the compliment and host a tournament where contestants uplift each other and bring surprising insights while vying to be the very best at making others feel seen!

events.title: Hiroshi Nagahama Autograph
events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.title: Disability in Wuthering Waves
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Many anime and games tie in physical or psychological drawbacks to balance out their characters' superpowers; the open-world, post-apocalyptic gacha game Wuthering Waves takes this a step further. Main characters and NPCs alike exhibit a wide spectrum of conditions that echo real-word disabilities, which are closely integrated with the game's worldbuilding and storytelling. This panel explores the depth and breadth of Wuthering Waves's disability rep, analyzes how different in-game cultures deal with disability, and affirms the value of thoughtful representation through the eyes of disabled players.

events.title: Your Digital Innie: Modern Security and Identity
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A presentation about personal digital privacy and security. Links to themes from anime like Ghost in the Shell, Serial Experiments Lain, and Psycho Pass.

events.title: Cosplay Chess Pre-Meet
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Required in-person meeting for all participants in the Cosplay Chess games.

events.title: 18+ Cosplay Skit Contest
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage] See your favorite characters come to life on stage! The Sakura-Con 18+ Skit is a competition showcase of stage performance, open to Cosplayers of all levels of experience.

events.title: One Piece TCG - Free Starter Deck Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: It’s Time to D—D—D—Discuss Lore!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Let’s talk history and compare the Yu-Gi-Oh lore with its real world historical counterparts and just how accurate the series actually is! Also taking a deep dive into what the series used as influences for some of its most iconic cards!

events.title: AMV Category Courtroom
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Attention rules lawyers! Can YOU make a case for why that Action AMV was actually Romance? Do Grave of the Fireflies AMVs ever belong in Comedy? Join us for improvisational wackiness as we recruit “Category Lawyers” from the audience to zealously litigate for some very twisted category choices! YOU are the jury!

events.title: maimai DX Tournament - UPPERS
events.panelDescription: [Gaming]

events.title: QTBIPOC Panel/Meet-Up
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A safe space for attendees who find themselves in this "double minority" identifying as both LGBTQIA+ and BIPOC. Through sharing our own experiences and engaging in meaningful dialogue, this panel provides an environment to build awareness, community, support, and belonging for those looking to find their place within the convention.

events.title: My kid likes anime now what?
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Has your kid started liking anime/manga and you have no idea what is going on? Anime and manga can be a lot to take in and understand as a parent. Come learn some terminology and helpful tips and tricks for how to best understand this new world.

events.title: D&D 5e (2024): Eight Kolbolds and a Baby (dragon): An Intro adventure
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "You have been hired to find and return the egg of a friendly copper dragon.
 A band of kobolds recently stole said egg and brought it to a nearby cave to eat. But when the egg hatched, and a screeching dragon wyrmling emerged, the kobolds quickly realized they bit off more than they could chew. The kobolds then tried to appease the fussy wyrmling with treasure, but they just can’t agree on what to do next. Should they return the wyrmling to its nest? Should they raise it themselves? They don't know what to do! The choice of how best to handle these draconic thieves? Now that is up to you! 
This is a lighthearted intro meant to take 100-110 minutes and give a taste of most D&D mechanics
---
Game Host: Courtney V
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players age 6 and up)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Pathfinder Society: Bounty 1 - Whitefang Wyrm
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "A Pathfinder Bounty designed for 1st-level characters.
In the frigid region known as the Crown of the World, people from diverse cultures and backgrounds must all band together to survive the long nights of winter and deadly thaws of spring. The way of life of these hardy frontier folk is now threatened by a serpentine creature that slips in during the dead of night and empties their nets of fish and their smoking racks of fresh meat. Unable and unwilling to let this theft stand, the people of the tiny northern town of Aaminiut have placed a bounty on the creature known locally as ""the Whitefange Wyrm"".
---
Game Host: Mike
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Pathfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: D&D 5e (2024): Notice Me Senpai! Improvised Rom-Com Adventure
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Help your friend get a date with their crush at Adventuring Camp! We will do a bit of mad-libs style of improv to create the love interest and the romantic rival as a group. The players will decide who is going to be the pursuer, and everyone else will be their wing-person in this adventure. Get your friend a date before camp ends! We will use pre-generated characters for this adventure that follow character trope archetypes. No DnD experience needed!
---
Game Host: Dario
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: 15+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Pathfinder Society: Silver Bark, Golden Blades [PFS2 6-05]
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Silvertree Village is a small settlement within the Forest of Spirits, not far off the Spirit Road, access has been cut off by a mysterious fog.
