events.title: Rhythm Gaming Freeplay
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Get your groove in with up to three other players in Just Dance, mimic silhouettes to iconic pop hits! Featuring other rhythm surprises as well!

events.title: Retro/Console Freeplay
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Check in at Console Gaming for live content and more all weekend!

events.title: Flesh and Blood – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Dear Con-goer, do you desire Flesh? Do you desire Blood? Perhaps even both at the same time? In that case, we at the TCG Room have you covered. Come see us and one of our Flesh Experts (Blood Experts available upon request) will teach you all the ins and outs of the Flesh and Blood TCG. Should you survive the trials, you will be rewarded with a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

All materials for Flesh and Blood were graciously donated by Legend Story Studios, who are a fantastic partner.

events.title: Flesh and Blood – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Come play a casual Commander game with friends, strangers, or even DRAW PHASE Staff!

Pods of 4 will be seated on-demand. No deck? No problem! We've got a library of pre-constructed decks you can borrow for the duration of your game. All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

events.title: DRAW PHASE Presents TCGs @ Sakura-Con - Saturday
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Welcome to the Sakura-Con x DRAW PHASE 2026 Trading Card Games room! We'll be running totally FREE events wall to wall all weekend long, from Learn to Play events, to Drafts, Sealed, Constructed, and more for some of your favorite games! Any event you do in the room will earn you a ticket that you can jealously hoard until Sunday when we have our Grand Giveaway, full of prizes generously donated by our lovely sponsors, donors, and friends. 

Whether you're a seasoned pro or a you've never played a card game in your life, we'll have content for you! Come learn a game you have always wanted to play or one you've never even heard of. And best of all, every single thing in this room is FREE! So if you want to come hang out with your fellow con-goer, meet some new friends and learn some fun games along the way, our team of dedicated volunteers will be here all weekend to guide you through the wonderful world of cardboard.

DRAW PHASE is a 501(c)(3) Non-Profit Organization that provides TCG Content in the PNW at no cost to the attendee! If you're interested in learning more, ask a volunteer.

events.title: Final Boss Faceoff
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Watch top players take on the hardest boss challenges from fighting games. Can they defeat SNK bosses without cheese? Find out! Win prizes!

events.title: Morning Exercise (Move, Dance, Think)
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Let's get in those morning calesthenics with some AMV fueled exercise for your body and brain.

events.title: AMV 101: Editing Basics
events.panelDescription:
[Fan Panel] Presented by Violet Skies and Tyken

Ever wondered how to make one of those super cool AMVs you've seen at con or online? Or what an AMV even is? Come find out! We'll discuss tips, tricks, and recommendations for starting your AMV journey.

events.title: Grand Archive – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Welcome to the Archive, Champion – do you have what it takes to forestall an apocalypse?

Come find out and learn Grand Archive, a growing new TCG fusing anime aesthetics with the best of western game design! Along with your demo kit, all participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

All material graciously provided by Grand Archive’s developer Weebs of the Shore. Thank you for your continued support, WotS!

events.title: Cosplay Spelling Bee
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] From “thermoplastic” to “chiaroscuro,” how strong is your cosplay vocabulary? Step up to the mic for a cosplay-themed spelling bee where crafting knowledge meets friendly rivalry. Expect laughs, tricky words, and plenty of “wait… how do you spell that?” moments. Come compete or just enjoy the chaos!

events.title: Intro to Jirai and Ryousangata Fashion
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you seen a cute fashion style that looks like gothic lolita, but a little more casual? What about girly, princess-style outfits? Join us to learn more about the origins of jiraikei and how to put an outfit together!

events.title: Cosplay Planning 101
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A cosplay planning seminar, perfect for anyone just starting out, veterans, or people just thinking about getting into the cosplay-making scene! Join for a brief introductory lecture, followed by a Q&A and open discussion on tips and tricks from the community!

events.title: How To Order Hanfu
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] How to Order Hanfu is an hour seminar by Yuqihanfu and Ari from Cascadia Hanfu Association. It goes over the many options you have in ordering hanfu, the pros and cons, estimated costs, and some pro-tips to help you along the way. There is allotted time for attendees to ask questions or get a close look at some of our favorite hanfu pieces!

events.title: In Defense of the Legend of Korra
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] The Legend of Korra (or "LoK" or "Korra" for short) is generally considered a disappointing sequel to the widely beloved Avatar: The Last Airbender, even catching a consistent ire among some fans. However, that perspective overlooks much of the incredible context in which this show was made as well as the numerous groundbreaking elements that shaped animation today. This panel goes beyond the typical "fan panel" and goes deep into the context in which Korra was created, working in the Nickelodeon system, paying homage to much of its original source material, and the strides that built upon its beloved predecessor as a stand-alone property. In sum, Korra might not be better than A:TLA, but a case can easily be made for why it's great. This panel is hosted by someone who you are likely to see at the front of the queue of every Q&A line, and a certain 'Month', who you likely will hear clopping around cons as a horse to a legendary king.

events.title: Autism/Neurodiversity Meet-Up (10th Anniversary!)
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Since it was first held in 2016, this meet-up has been a welcoming space for autistic and neurodivergent anime fans to connect, share experiences, and celebrate our community. Whether this is your first SakuraCon or your tenth, all are welcome to join us for conversation, support, and friendship as we mark a decade of coming together.

events.title: Stations of the Cross…Over: Salvation and Sin in Modern Anime
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ever start watching an anime and suddenly a character is being crucified? Have you thought to yourself "wait what? why..." In this panel we'll explore the history of Christian imagery, theology, and religious syncretism in anime.

events.title: Arts and Crafts Room Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: RPG Character Creation and Solo RPGs
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Learn to build an RPG character, or play one of the Alexandria RPG Library's collection of solo RPGs! Alexandria's crew of Librarians and GMs will be on hand to help you flesh out your character concept, or demonstrate RPG games that can be played entirely on your own.

events.title: Pathfinder Society: Tanuki Trouble [PFS2 Q24]
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "A Pathfinder Society Quest designed for 1st- through 4th-level characters, playable in 2-3 hours.
During the Year of Unfettered Exploration, the reappearance of the planes of metal and wood destabilized the Maze of the Open Road, the extradimensional maze the Pathfinder Society uses to transport personnel and goods across vast distances. In trying to stabilize itself, new entrances and exits appeared, one in a Tanuki village in Minkai. The tanuki had some fun pranking travelers in the maze until some Pathfinder agents defeated them at their own games. Now the village is on good terms with the Society, but the Pathfinders would like to have a lodge in the village to oversee this new exit. The tanuki are amenable, but only if the Pathfinders do some favors for them first. Of course the PCs are the ones tasked with these favors.
---
Game Host: Nick H
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Pathfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Alexandria RPG Library Presents: Classic, Modern & Indie RPGs
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Visit the Alexandria RPG Library and explore over a thousand RPG rulebooks, modules, and player aids from countless game systems. From classic D&D to indie darlings, from cyberpunk noir to magical pirates. We bring all the dice, maps, books, and supplies for you to play in or run a game.

Browse our books, roll up a character, or grab a ticket to join our events. Tickets for each day’s games are available in-person at the RPG Library (Arch Tower, Tahoma 4) at 10AM.

The Alexandria RPG Library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit based in Tacoma, Wash.

events.title: Board Game Library - Presented by Dragonflight Game Con
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Since 1980, Dragonflight Game Con has been introducing board game enthusiasts to a wide variety of games through their convention's extensive lending library of games. Now, you can experience that library here at Sakura-Con! Visit the Dragonflight Game Library in the Board and RPG Gaming Room, located on the Tahoma level of the Gaming Annex, to browse the collection of available games, check out a game, and play in the room's free-play area.

Dragonflight Game Con is a nonprofit, volunteer-run gaming convention that takes place every August in Bellevue, WA. Visit their website at https://dfgamecon.org/ to learn more.

events.title: Exhibits Hall Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Vocaloid: Hearing The Producer's Voice Behind Virtual Idols
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Hatsune Miku is THE Virtual Idol, but have you ever wondered about who she is behind the hologram and blue twintails? At this panel, we'll be diving into Vocal Synth Producers! What they are, who they are, and the distinct styles of music that make each one of them unique. Everyone is welcome to come and attend; seasoned veterans, casual newbies, and the poor unsuspecting soul who needs something to do for an hour.

events.title: Shogi lessons/Free play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come learn/play the ancient game of Go! Beginners very welcome

events.title: Shogi lessons/Free play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come learn/play the ancient game of Shogi! Beginners very welcome

events.title: Photo Booth Room
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Prize Room Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Prize Room Open
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] Have questions about this year's SakuraQuest Scavenger Hunt or ready to turn it in and get your pin? This is the place.

Go to sakuraquest.org or pick up a paper form here or at any Info Booth to get started.

events.title: BOTC: Advanced Games
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Blood on the Clocktower: Lorics, Carosel, Funky Travelers, advanced scripts!

events.title: Spy x Family and Cold War Germany
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Espionage, assassins, surveillance, and secret identities, but what’s the truth behind the world of Spy x Family? Join us for a deep dive into the history of Cold War Germany and the true historical basis for the cool spy adventures!

events.title: Transitioning Humanity: This Is Not A Man
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] What do Arcane, Creature Commandos, Cyberpunk: Edgerunners, and The Witch from Mercury have in common? All four explore the ethics and effects of human augmentation – of transhumanism. They ask how far science and technology can take humanity, and whether the cost is worth it. Join us for an in-depth examination of these new transhumanist narratives, how (if) they’ve been updated for our modern age, and where these shows draw the line on humanity.

events.title: Oh! That's A Baseball Episode!!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you ever just been watching an anime when suddenly, out of left field, all the characters start playing baseball for an episode and it's the greatest thing ever? Whether it's there to throw a curveball into how you see the characters, or a change-up into the pacing, or just to bring out their animation aces, this is a panel about how anime's baseball episodes really hit it out of the park.

events.title: Kanto Pokemon Food Web
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you ever been curious about what Pokémon eat out in the wild? Well look no further than this presentation. Learn about what a food web created using the first generation of Pokémon would look like. It shows which Pokémon might prey on each other and how their size, typing and inspiration might affect that. It also tells us which species might be major producers, apex predators, or keystone species. Additionally we will be discussing what exactly a food web is, how it differs from a food chain, how Pokémon were categorized in order to put them onto the food web and how environmental factors change which Pokémon are included.

events.title: The 5 W's of Sumo - A Factual (But Fun!) Guide to the Basics
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Sumo is having a resurgence of popularity in Japan, thanks to new and powerful young wrestlers, but getting into the sport can be confusing. Who are all of these people? What are they doing? What are those poses?! Was that a FACE SLAP? Is that allowed??? Where are their clothes?!?! Why was this even invented? Let's run through the basics of this religious-ceremony-turned-national-sport to answer all of these questions and more!

events.title: Discover the Sound of Traditional Japanese Harps
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Join our interactive panel presenting beautiful Japanese music!Koto no WA, ensemble of local koto (Japanese harp) musicians in the Greater Seattle Area, will play both traditional and contemporary songs, including some songs from anime series. This workshop will be a combination of live demonstrations, a brief history lesson on Japanese music culture, Q & A sessions, and a hands-on experience with the koto. Don’t miss this rare opportunity to experience Japan’s traditional string instrument with more than a thousand years of history.

events.title: Discover the Sound of Traditional Japanese Harps
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Join our interactive panel presenting beautiful Japanese music!Koto no WA, ensemble of local koto (Japanese harp) musicians in the Greater Seattle Area, will play both traditional and contemporary songs, including some songs from anime series. This workshop will be a combination of live demonstrations, a brief history lesson on Japanese music culture, Q & A sessions, and a hands-on experience with the koto. Don’t miss this rare opportunity to experience Japan’s traditional string instrument with more than a thousand years of history.

events.title: Digimon Card Game – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Di-Di-Digimon! Digital Monsters, The Card Game! Come help to save the digital world and learn to play this rich card game that has captivated audiences since 2020! Change yourself into a digital champion and save the digital world. And after you're done digivolving, you'll receive a pack on us to start your journey and a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: Netrunner -- Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Presented by MTGPlatinum.com!

Come and enjoy a completely free, curated draft experience reaching through MTG history to provide you with an unpredictable experience you won’t find anywhere else

All Curated Chaos drafts are three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Netrunner -- Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come learn the Netrunner TCG directly from the team developing it! Null Signal Games has sent us their very best to teach you how to be the best Runner you can be! All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: Uma Musume: Pretty Derby Tournament 2
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Morning races are here! Bet on your favorite horse girl and watch them dash to the finish line. Winning bettors get prize tickets! Win prizes!

events.title: Tekken Bowl (Tekken Tag Tournament)
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Why punch when you can bowl? The most ridiculous Tekken mode returns! Strike out your opponents in this bizarre but beloved minigame. Win prizes!

events.title: Otaku Pin Trading
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Do you have pins that seem to have lost their charm to you? Looking to trade some of your former favorite pins? Join others to trade pins from all fandoms! Show off your Ita Bag! See what pins other Otaku's have collected! Let's get together and celebrate our love of pins!

events.title: K-Pop Dance Workout
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Want to start your con day off with a bang? How about a pop! This k-pop inspired dance workout is the perfect way to wake up your body for a long day of walking around. Follow along to some k-pop inspired exercises, listening to your fav groups. No experience needed, just come have fun!!

events.title: Ultimate Anime Trivia
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Need to prove that the hours you spent on anime fan wikis are worth something? Join us for Ultimate Anime Trivia! We'll cover a wide range of genres and shows. Feel free to form small teams with friends or with strangers around you and have some fun!

events.title: Evangelion AMVs
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Evangelion continues to delight and perplex audiences with it's apocalyptic stakes and teen angst. Come check out a similarly eclectic mix of AMVs!

events.title: Daggerheart: Witch of the Meadowbrook Bog
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "There is a distraught parent in the tiny town of Meadowsbrook, asking for assistance to have their lost infant returned.  With the challenge of political vying between the mayor Montgomery and the witch Agatha, duplicitous dealings from community members, and no right answer, the party will be thrust into a situation that challenges their morals, ethics, and notions.  This session will include topics of natalism, memory manipulation, and small community dynamics.  Character sheets and ability cards provided!
---
Game Host: Æther K (they/xe)
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Crunchyroll Presents: Witch Hat Atelier Premiere Screening
events.panelDescription: [Industry Panel] Everyone knows that magic-users are born, not made. This being a hard truth to swallow, Coco is about to give up her lifelong dream to be a witch ... until she meets the mysterious magician, Qifrey. By secretly watching Qifrey perform magic, Coco realizes that maybe what seemed like the truth is not exactly reality. Her “magical” dream may not be as far away as it seems… Join us for the premiere screening of Witch Hat Atelier.

events.title: An Intro to Traditional Japanese Instruments with Dekoboko Taiko
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Dekoboko Taiko invites you to learn about the rich history of traditional Japanese instruments, particularly percussion and winds, and their cultural influence in Japan and the world. This fun and interactive panel will include a performance and demonstration by members of the local Seattle group Dekoboko Taiko. Explore the festive beats of the taiko, the dancing clicks of the naruko, the lively melodies of the fue, and a few more surprises! Participants will be able to try out many of the featured instruments and enjoy a full performance from the group!

events.title: Do You Know Your Anime?
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] 3rd year in a row! Anime trivia with a twist. Volunteers will be interviewed and quizzed on all things anime, with the potential to win prizes and flex their knowledge in a fun environment. Guess the tune, badly described anime, and general trivia questions across the world of anime.

events.title: CHUNITHM Tournament - LOWERS
events.panelDescription: [Gaming]

events.title: Artist Alley Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Wander Witch: A Cozy RPG of Fairies and Witches: A Spoonful of Sunlight, A Saucerful of Stars
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "As students and staff of witchery and wizardry at the vibrant Brandysprite Castle, it isn't unusual for you, your classmates, and charges to be sent as envoys to friendly fairy communities. Whether it be to gather resources, catch up on the latest tales, aid villagers, or recruit newcomers, the school trusts you to be clever, creative, and kind as you lend your aid. This time, you are responding to reports of a mysterious dreaming sickness that has taken hold in the nearby village of Gossamer. It's up to you to explore, solve riddles, and forge friendships needed to restore the easy rest of its inhabitants. Will this cozy mystery be best solved through quick wits, an open heart, or a bit of both?
---
Game Host: Jack
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players 6 and older welcome)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: D&D 5e (2024): Notice Me Senpai! Improvised Rom-Com Adventure
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Help your friend get a date with their crush at Adventuring Camp! We will do a bit of mad-libs style of improv to create the love interest and the romantic rival as a group. The players will decide who is going to be the pursuer, and everyone else will be their wing-person in this adventure. Get your friend a date before camp ends! We will use pre-generated characters for this adventure that follow character trope archetypes. No DnD experience needed!
---
Game Host: Dario
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: 15+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Shadow Scar: Syndicate Soiree
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Come join the creator of the Shadow Scar TTRPG for a night of fun and festivity as you enter the Hollow Eye Masquerade! Important people from across the multiverse have been gathered together for this once in a lifetime soiree thrown by the head of the Hollow Eye Crime Syndicate, Malacoda. As ninja agents of the secretive Shadow Scar Agency you've been sent to infiltrate the party and find out what the Hollow Eye Syndicate is planning with all these important people. While you're there you'll also have to keep your eyes peeled. Intelligence suggests that you won't be the only curious party. The monstrous yokai might be sending their own agents to kidnap the guests or worse.

---
Game Host: Cody Pondsmith 
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.panelDescription: [Charity Auction]

events.panelRoom: Dreamland Maid Cafe - Elliot Bay Room

events.title: How to Make Your Creative Dreams Come True with Cassandra Lee Morris
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Emi Nitta Panel
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: Shadowverse TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Whether you've played the online TCG for Shadowverse or not, Shadowverse Evolve is a exciting and enjoyable game that promises to keep you on your toes constantly. Our resident Anime TCG Specialist will be here to teach you how to play and put you on your journey to becoming a champion. All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: One Piece TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Obtain Fame, Wealth, and Power beyond your wildest dreams by coming to the TCG Room and learning to play the One Piece TCG! Will you be able to find the buried treasure- Or perhaps the treasure is actually the friendships you formed in your journey? No matter what, if you complete a learn to play session you will receive a ticket for our Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: One Piece TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Oh, it’s all very fine and well to take your carefully-curated team to victory – but someone who’s true Champion material has that special something that lets them bond with a team they’ve only just met, intuitively understand their strengths and weaknesses, and lead them to glory.

How do you develop a talent like that, you ask?

Why, many rounds of Build & Battle, of course! Assemble your deck from the contents of the provided Build & Battle Box, and battle your way to to the top of the mountain… or at least to a good time. All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: 2XKO Tournament and Meet the Devs!
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Swag, Fight and meet the devs! Mixed format tournament.
Riot's 2v2 tag fighter brings League of Legends champions to the fighting game arena! Fresh mechanics, familiar faces, and new rivalries. Top 3 win prizes!

events.title: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate Tournament 3
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Saturday Smash starts here! Tournament 3 brings the competition to prime time. Let's settle this in Smash. Win prizes!

events.title: Dubs are Better than Subs
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Let’s be honest, you’ve all been watching anime wrong. The community needs to give more respect to the wonderful voice actors who bring dub adaptations to life. In fact, anime is even better dubbed than subbed. From not having to read to being able to multi-task, we will explore the range of benefits offered by dubs, the superior anime viewing method.

events.title: Anime OnlyConnect Game Show
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Based on the British Game Show, players will have to find the hidden connections and patterns amongst various anime related clues.

events.title: Intro to Fictionkin
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] The term “fictionkin” describes someone who self-identifies as having a deep-seated, persistent sense of identity as a fictional person, being, species, etc. In this panel, we will explore key terms and concepts, discuss the relationship of fictionkin to fandom as a whole, and share lived experiences in the hope of reducing the stigma surrounding fictionkin. There will be ample time for questions, so bring your curious minds, listening ears, and maybe even your own experiences.

events.title: Extremely Hard Take-Home Anime Quiz Turn-in
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] The quiz is due on Saturday. Submit it in the designated room. You may have someone else submit it if you cannot go yourself. Please remember to include your name and badge number on the first page.

events.title: Are YOU a Dinergoth?
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Anime fan? Discord native? Alt aesthetics? Dreaming of YouTube success? You might be part of a new American archetype. This panel explores the "dinergoth"—the convergence of geek fandoms, alt fashion, queerness, and downward mobility that's become the dominant youth culture of 2020s suburban and provincial America. We'll dig into why anime kids and emo kids and queer kids and neurodivergent kids are now the same kids, and what it means that this con might be the cultural center of the new American mainstream.

events.title: Pastel Roulette-Sky Solo Live
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] All the way from Utah, join Sky of Pastel Roulette in a high-energy performance that will be sure to get you hoppin~! This internet bunny will be performing their original music as well as high tempo covers of idol music. Turn your penlights to pink and bounce along with the music!

events.title: Name that Anime Tune 2.0
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] How well do you REALLY know your anime music? Compete against the room to identify the songs of anime, but with a twist: playback will start at a random point in each track! This is the spectacular trivia experience of Name That Anime Tune 2.0 but in a whole new light!

events.title: ElfQuest: An American Manga
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] For almost 50 years, ElfQuest has straddled the line of what defines an American comic and what defines manga. A look at the series, and why it could be considered an American manga.

events.title: Spoilers Ahead: Let's ruin everything!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Some people just don’t care about spoilers. Sometimes there’s a story you’re interested in, but just don’t have time to sit down and watch or play the whole thing. Or maybe you just want to kill some time listening to someone talk about stories they really like! Whatever you come in for, you’ll have a good time! I’ve got lots of things to spoil for you, including Silent Hill F, and one of Kamen Rider's most infamous moments! There are some things that I'll only talk about in this room!

events.title: Fabula Ultima TTJRPG: PRESS START Tutorial: A Fated Descent
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "The award-winning Fabula Ultima is designed to recreate the wondrous worlds, characters and stories seen in videogames such as the legendary Final Fantasy series. In this BEGINNER FRIENDLY introductory adventure, set out on an airship to investigate a massive magic crater, the site of a mysterious, city razing arcane disaster.
---
Game Host: Andrew Harshman
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Deadlands: Savage Worlds Adventure Edition: Last Coach to Hallowed Ground
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "The posse is staying in a frontier town when an empty, bullet-riddled stagecoach rolls in at dawn. By noon, the graveyard bell rings on its own. The dead begin to stir—not to rise, but to hunt!
---
Game Host: Jay
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Fabula Ultima TTJRPG: Fabula Ultima - New Beginnings
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Fabula Ultima brings a classic jRPG feel to tabletop. Each player MUST multiclass, and classes are designed to interact to create a unique to you hero. Over 20 different classes to mix and match,  the only limit is your imagination. We will cover character creation and pick out a scenario to play.
---
Game Host: Brandon Crawford
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: MAID RPG: This is What You Were Maid For....
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "MAID RPG is a slice of life TRPG from Japan where you play a Maid serving your Master (the GM) alongside a cast of fellow Maids. Your job? Keep the estate running smoothly… regardless of what it may throw at you. One moment you’re folding laundry and serving tea. The next, a literal god descends from the heavens to purge mankind.
Expect over the top anime nonsense, teamwork, and unpredictable chaos in this lighthearted, slice of life TRPG! 
---
Game Host: NiiNya
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: 18+ : Potential Adult Themes

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Shadow Scar: The Final Test
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Come begin your journey into the multiverse as a ninja by…taking your graduation exam? You've trained long enough and you are ready to prove yourself. So come join your classmates as you try to pass the test to become agents working in the field to defeat the yokai plotting to destroy humanity. 
---
Game Host: Tarabella
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Mature/18+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Iconic Kpop Random Play Dance
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you a fan of Kpop? Want to show off you moves! Then come join us for our iconic KPOP RPD!!! Songs from 2005 to 2026!!!

events.title: Flower Arranging with A Voice Actor (Kelsey Cruz)
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] From Florist to Voice Actress - she does it all! Kelsey Cruz used to work at a flower shop as a florist and would make arrangements for weddings, funerals, birthdays, and everything inbetween! Join as she takes you through how to arrange your own flowers at home while fielding your questions about flowers, anime, voice acting, etc!

events.title: AMV Contest Seating
events.panelDescription: [Main Stage] Seating for the AMV Contest

events.title: Strange Anime For Strange People
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you a weirdo who likes to explore the weird, offputting side of whatever media you consume? Well, this is the panel for you! Surreal humor, mind-bending visuals, outrageous characters, and more await as we highlight the anime and creators that dare to be different.

events.title: The Gojo Effect
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you ever wondered why people are obsessed with Satoru Gojo from the series Jujutsu Kaisen? Do you, yourself, love him? Do you hate him and want to know what it is about him that has hypnotized our community? Well, join us as we explore what about him has everyone captivated and how he has impacted the history of anime/manga and society going forward.

events.title: Cosplay Talent Show
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] We all know that cosplayers are talented: masters of sewing, crafting, wig-styling, modeling, and performing. But... can they make balloon animals? Play the clarinet? Tap dance?? Mime???Come celebrate your fellow Convention attendees as they show off a diverse range of their exceptional, unconventional, and secret talents. This is not a competition, just a fun supportive place to show off!

events.title: Ani-Family Feud by Anime Nation UW
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Anime Nation UW is for another round of Ani-Family Feud!! At the University of Washington, Anime Nation is THE student organization for anime/manga/game enjoyers alike. It is our honor to bring the spirit of ANUW to the convention for the second year in a row—and we're going all out once again! Come play anime-themed Family Feud with your fellow attendees for the chance to win very special prizes!

events.title: Star Ocean Ex Episodes 1-3
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] ANIME SHOWING (Subtitled)

Based on the video game series, the story follows Claude C. Kenni, a young officer aboard an advanced starship who is mysteriously transported to Expel, a medieval world of swords and sorcery. Mistaken for a legendary hero, Claude becomes entangled in a quest to save the planet from a looming catastrophe.

events.title: Exploring Origami
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Prepare yourself for an exciting hands-on introduction to origami, the Japanese-originated art of folded paper sculpture. From angles to self-similarity, bases to grafts, you'll learn about some of the fundamental principles that make up origami, and make several models in the process, from cranes to dragons. Beginners are welcome, this workshop assumes no prior folding experience.

events.title: Appleseed
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] ANIME SHOWING (1988 OVA - Subtitlted)

After World War III devastates the Earth, the utopian city of Olympus is governed largely by genetically engineered artificial humans known as Bioroids. When terrorists plot to use a powerful weapon called the “Holy Grail” to destabilize the fragile balance between humans and Bioroids, ESWAT officers Deunan Knute and the cyborg Briareos are drawn into a high-stakes conflict that threatens the future of the city.

events.title: Taboo! Game Show: Anime Edition
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] It's the Taboo! Game Show: Anime Edition! Compete in a team of 2-4 and try to get your teammates to guess anime-related terms. Think fast, flex your otaku knowledge to its limits, and get your team as many points as possible! Be careful not to use any of the "taboo" words, or we and the audience will buzz you! Glory and prizes are in store for the winners!

events.title: Origami Workshop with PAPER
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Discover the fascinating craft of origami and learn how to fold decorative, amusing and practical items with a sheet of paper.

events.title: Riichi Mahjong Etiquette
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Learn the do's and don'ts of etiquette of Riichi mahjong - just in time for the Sakura Cup tournament!

events.title: The Journey to Ende (Mallorie Rodak, Jill Harris, and Jordan Dash Cruz)
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Join Mallorie (Frieren), Jill (Fern), and Jordan (Stark) as they discuss season 2 of Frieren: Beyond Journey's End!

events.title: AMV Contest
events.panelDescription: [Main Stage] The best of the best compete in Sakura Con's annual AMV Contest!

events.title: Diabolo (Chinese Yo-yo) Workshop
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Apex Diabolo is a local performance group specializing in the diabolo, a traditional circus art and popular activity in Japan, Taiwan, and many other parts of the world. In this session, they will host a workshop to teach you the basics. Don't miss their special performance at the closing ceremony!

events.title: Two Rooms and a Boom
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social deception game of hidden roles and deduction, players are divided into 2 different rooms. Find your teammates, establish trust, and exchange hostages before time runs out and the bomb explodes!

events.title: Two Rooms and a Boom
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social deception game of hidden roles and deduction, players are divided into 2 different rooms. Find your teammates, establish trust, and exchange hostages before time runs out and the bomb explodes!

events.title: Yugioh Anime Jeopardy
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Want to test your Yugioh Anime knowledge? Then this is the panel for you. We'll be doing trivia questions in the style of Jeopardy on all of the Yugioh shows (from the original series to all of its various spin offs) So get your duel disk out and get your trivia on!

events.title: Cardfight Vanguard!! Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Ever wanted to try out an Anime card game known for being easy to learn and incredibly fun to master? Cardfight Vanguard!! is right up your alley then! Come and play with one of our Starter Decks and be guided through a game by one of our volunteers! Complete a Learn to Play session and receive a ticket for the Sunday Giveaway!

events.title: Magic: The Gathering - Free Final Fantasy Draft
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come and enjoy a completely free Draft experience at Sakura-Con!All drafts are three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway, as well as one pack per win!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Free Genesys Tournament
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Oops, looks like I ran out of time writing a description for this event! It's a totally FREE Genesys tournament though, so what's not to like? Bring your own deck and prove you're the best, and we'll have prizes for you along the way. And a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday as well! 

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Two loaner decks are available upon request as part of your reservation. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Japanese Tea Ceremony
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Presentation of Japanese culture through Japanese Tea Ceremony demonstration and lecture.

events.title: Lumines: Arise Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The hypnotic puzzle game with sick beats! Match blocks to the rhythm and enter the zen state of Lumines mastery. Win prizes!

events.title: Lumines: Arise Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The puzzle game that started it all! Stack blocks, clear lines, and outlast your opponents. Simple to learn, impossible to master. Top 3 win prizes!

events.title: Beyond the Print Bed: 3D Printing in Cosplay, Fabric & Fiber Arts
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join us as we dive into how 3D printing is changing the game in cosplay, embroidery, and other crafty worlds. We’ll explore how makers are using 3D tech to quickly prototype designs, try out new materials, and perfect those tiny details before going all in. Whether you're creating a costume or adding flair to your designs, 3D printing is making it faster and more precise. Come hear how this tech is shaking up the creative process and making crafts more fun, innovative, and how you can get involved to start incorporating your own crafting style to enhance your cosplays!

events.title: The Importance of Diversity in Youth Literature
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Books have long been a way for us to see ourselves in adventures. Nowadays, diversity has grown so much in books geared towards youth. Come celebrate why that's so important and get some new recommendations for your to be read list!

events.title: Cosplay Regrets
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] New to cosplay or even a seasoned professional? Come join three competitive cosplayers as they reflects upon their mistakes and regrets so you can avoid them in your own cosplays and projects

events.title: Beyond Epic! (The Musical)
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you a fan of a catchy tune? Do you wanna fight sirens and a cyclops? Are you looking for a friend or mentor? Find out when a man becomes a monster? Do you want to be legendary? Holy moly! Come join us and sing all your favorite songs from Epic! The Musical. Presented by a cast of cunning and powerful gods and a powerful sorceress we would love to singalong with you all. Just remember to greet the world with open arms and keep the bag closed!

events.title: Controversial Anime Opinions - Live Discussion & Audience Participation!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Think your anime takes are totally right… or ready to defend a hot take to the end? Join us for an interactive, high-energy panel where YOU become part of the debate! The room will be divided into four corners: Strongly Agree, Agree, Disagree, and Strongl

events.title: Emi Nitta Autograph
events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.title: Sakura Cup Orientation
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Matchmaking and table assignment for the Sakura Cup tournament.

events.title: Diversity in Anime with Bill Butts
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Join Bill Butts as he speaks on what it’s like being a person of color working in the anime industry. There will be a Q&A period, as well as a voice acting workshop to end the panel with!

events.title: Bandai Namco Filmworks Industry Panel
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Come to the Bandai Namco Filmworks Industry Panel to get our latest news on upcoming anime, with special guest appearance by Haruki Ishiya, the voice of the main character Hikaru Gero from MARRIAGETOXIN.

events.title: PRISM's Idol Kahoot!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Think you're an idol pro? Join PRISM*Rush! for a fun, 100-Question Idol Kahoot! Test your knowledge on idol terminology, your song recognition, and plenty of questions on various medias such as Vocaloid, Love Live!, Ensemble Stars, Project Sekai, and so much more! We'll have prizes for our Top 5 on the scoreboard, so study up!

events.title: Disability and Access in Witch Hat Atelier
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] In the world of magic, you are either born a witch or you aren't. This is supposedly to keep people from misusing magic to harm others, but Coco and company continue to see the inequity and suffering caused and emphasized by lack of access and judgment of ability. In this panel, we'll explore how Witch Hat Atelier's whimsical story is a clear commentary on issues we experience in our world on a daily basis, and why those who need access the most are often barred from it.

events.title: The GameSurge | Ultimate Video Game Trivia!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Put your gaming knowledge on the line in the ultimate trivia showdown! From retro classics to modern masterpieces, contestants face off for bragging rights in this high-energy, crowd-powered celebration of all things video games. Survive the rounds and earn your chance to raid The Treasure Trove of Trash—where fantastic prizes (and maybe a few surprises) await. Do YOU have what it takes to claim the loot and the glory? There’s only one way to find out!

events.title: Music from Anime: Live!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Live performance of music from some of our favorite anime; arranged for wind quintet, plus some special guest artists. There are many fantastic composers and song writers working in the realm of anime. We will be performing music by Joe Hisaishi (Studio Ghibli), Evan Call (Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End, Violet Evergarden), Yuki Kajiura (Sis Puella Magica Madoka), and more!

events.title: Rush 2049 Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming]

events.title: IE TAIGA!: Idol Call and MIX for beginners!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you seen ever seen an idol performance and heard fans cheer and clap and shout words in a certain way and thought, “Wow, I want to cheer like that! But how?” Well, you’re in luck! Your friendly wota duo Honeychuu is here to teach you the basics of call and MIX so YOU can cheer for your idols with confidence!

events.title: Pathfinder Society: The Chitterwood Walks, Part 2: The Battle of Logas [PFS2 7-12]
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "With the success of the Chitterwood Talks, the goblin tribes are ready to march on Logas and put a stop to the undead attacks, with support from the Pathfinder Society. As forces approach the city, rumors start to circle of a strange plague only affecting Hellknights, leaving the undead Hellknights covered in bizarre, circular scars and bearing the mark of an unknown order. Within Logas, a Hellknight necromancer has declared herself the head of a new Hellknight order and is using the city as her chance to prove its worth. Can the Pathfinders stand up to The Order of the Open Tomb?
---
Game Host: Steven S
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Pathfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Starfinder Society: The Beasts of Bo: Part One [SFS2 1-14]
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "A Starfinder Society Scenario designed for 3rd- through 4th-level characters, playable in 2-3 hours.
Dangerous raiders called the Beasts of Bo have proven to be a thorn in the side of the Starfinder Society. Armed to the teeth with tech salvaged from the Wreck of the Returned and audacious in their convoy attacks, the Beasts of Bo are a formidable foe, yet the group may still have its uses, . Do you have the courage to stand against the Beasts of Bo? 

This is an adventure in the ongoing Invasion’s Edge metaplot, and is part one of a two-part scenario series.
---
Game Host: Clem
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Starfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Yen Press Industry Panel
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] For over a decade, Yen Press has been one of the biggest publishers of manga, releasing the likes of Black Butler, Fruits Basket and Toilet-bound Hanako-kun. Yen Press has also given a home to fan favorite categories such as light novels, audiobooks and m

events.title: Sakura Cup
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Join the annual Sakura Cup mahjong tournament. Open to all attendees and free to enter! Registration opens Saturday morning and ends at 12:30pm.

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: A Study of Tatsuki Fujimoto: The Man Behind Chainsaw Man
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Following the release of 2022's Chainsaw Man, and the hit 2025 Reze Arc film, Tatsuki Fujimoto has quickly made a name for himself as one of the premier creators of the manga industry. With his bold, cinematic visual style, and gripping, eccentric storytelling, it is rare to find a creator that captures the hearts of millions of fans all over the world writing seemingly whatever he wants! If you are interested in exploring the underlying themes of works such as Chainsaw Man or Fire Punch, or even just learning some funny anecdotes from this largely mysterious figure, then join in for some lively discussion!

events.title: Echoes of Astra - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] This one's new! Echoes of Astra is a brand-new TCG from Parhelion Studios, an anime art themed game that mixes traditional TCG Gameplay with the tactical depth of war gaming! All wrapped in the package of some beautiful cardboard. If you want to learn a brand new TCG that is just starting this is your chance to get in on the ground floor! All participants will receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!

All our materials for Echoes of Astra were graciously donated by Parhelion Studios, so a big thank you to them for allowing us to show their game to you. Come over to the TCG Room and one of our experienced volunteers will teach you all about this tactically rich, exciting new game!

events.title: Digimon Card Game – Free Sealed Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] I've run out of Digimon puns and lyrics, so unfortunately you'll just have to settle with a Sealed tournament for free! Come visit us in the TCG Room and play a round of sealed Digimon on the house, complete with prizes and a ticket to all entries for our Grand Giveaway on Sunday!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: OC Sketchbook Swap
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join us for a sketchbook swap where we will draw our original characters (OCs) in each other’s books! Share your OC and walk away with pages full of new friends! All types of OCs are welcome, but the panel is open to ages 13 so please keep it PG-13 . Come with your art tools of choice, or pencils and paper will be provided.

events.title: Project A-ko
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed)

When Earth faces a looming alien threat, the outrageously wealthy and arrogant B-ko is far more concerned with defeating her rival A-ko and winning the affection of their sweet, clueless friend C-ko. Armed with custom-built mecha and an army of loyal followers, B-ko wages over-the-top battles in a fast-paced parody packed with classic anime references, exaggerated superpowers, and a stylish red power suit.

events.title: The Art of Armor Attachments
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Tired of your armor and heavy cosplays slipping and sliding everywhere? Looking for a more stable method to attach your foam armor? Come join Chaoticneutralcosplay and learn how to get more stability, mobility and functionality from your cosplay armor!

events.title: Fun and Trivia with FWHS Anime/Manga Club
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Live your Bukatsu slice-of-life episode with FWHS Anime/Manga Club by joining us in a variety of Test-Your-Fandom games with our club members. Would you survive our everyday teenage weirdness? What does an Anime Club look like in an American High School?

events.title: Hop Hop Hooray! Ra*bits Enstars Idol Panel Featured by Starlight Idols
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Looking for a fun panel to watch performances, play games and join in on a RDP? Hop on by Starlight Idol’s Ra*bits panel! This panel will be full of cute performances, a kpop & jpop random dance, exciting games and enstars themed prizes. Whether you’re an absolute enstars expert or just looking for a good time, you’ll be all ears from start to finish. So don’t forget to bring those penlights and get ready for a great time!

events.title: Studio Orange Autograph
events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.title: Call of Cthulhu : Poison Soup (Doku-iri Sūpu)
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "After going to sleep in your bed, you awake in the night to an unnerving scene. You find your self in a square room surrounded by strangers and in the center is a lone desk and chair. On the desk sits a bowl of bright red soup and a note that reads, ""If you want to leave, you have one hour to drink the poison soup, or you will be collected..."" Will you escape with your lives? Find out in this scenario straight from Japan from their number one TTRPG, Call of Cthulhu! New players welcome, pregens provided. 
---
Game Host: AJ
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: 18+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Beyblade X King of the Hill Tournament!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Hello everyone and I hope you are all excited for the second annual Beyblade X tournament at Sakura Con! We have swapped up the format for this year to help people get more play time! This years tournament will be a 1v1 king of the hill, we will have 4 hills and the top players on each hill will move onto the finals for a chance at glory, and some awesome prizes! We will have loaners beyblades available for anyone new to the game and a freeplay area for anyone who just wants to play and not compete! So come be a part of the Pacific NW's most anticipated and exciting Beyblade X events and let it rip!

events.title: The Fabulous Furoshiki – Japanese Cloth Wrapping Techniques
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Since ancient times, the Japanese have used a simple square cloth called a furoshiki to wrap and carry anything and everything. In today’s environmentally conscious age of maximizing use and minimizing waste, the furoshiki is making a strong comeback! Learn the basics of how to use this “eco-friendly” alternative to plastic bags and wrapping paper. This is a hands-on introduction to fun and functional Japanese furoshiki wrapping techniques!

events.title: Paradox Live Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Welcome to the Paradox Live Tournament! You will be chosen to represent one of the 8 Paralive Groups and go head to head with other groups, showing off your group's music in a tournament style fashion. Come join the tournament to support your favorite group(s) or to enjoy a friendly music competition! (No singing necessary)

events.title: Mr. “Ant” Tenna’s TV Time: A Deltarune Themed Gameshow!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you every wanted to participate in a game show? How about one right from Deltarune itself? Join A Certain Pole as he hosts his twist on "Mr. Tenna's Marvelous Mystery Board: TV Time!". Engage in trivia, minigames, and physical challenges to earn incredible prizes! (And maybe learn to love eachother along the way.) Now don't touch that dial! Because.... IT'S!! T.V!! TIME!!

events.title: What is the Omegaverse? : A Crash Course
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you new to the Omegaverse or a seasoned veteran? Come join us as we talk about the fan-fiction troupes legacy and how it reflects our society! Use what you learned to compete for alpha/beta/omega themed prizes!

events.title: Super Happy Fun Time: Photo Scavenger Hunt - Arch
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Do you want to take photos with your friends or enemies? Come join the SHFT Ladies in their Photo Scavenger Hunt - Arch location, where you will be tasked to take selfies and photos for points! This game requires a smart phone with access to the internet.

events.title: Neon Lords of the Toxic Wasteland: Mallpocalypse: All Sales Final
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Welcome to the edge of the Neon Wastes, Skumdogz. Your next paycheck squats on the rotting bones of an ancient shopping mall, a pre-war monument to consumer excess and terrible life choices. Wrecked during the Burger Wars and left to decay, the mall is now home to monsters, urban legends, and a notorious gang of mutant bicorns who rule the concourse by force.

Detroit Prime wants it cleared. Quietly was never on the table.

You are mercenaries. Scum-for-hire. Drop in, kick through flickering neon entrances, and erase everything inside with maximum prejudice. Expect warped muzak, killer machines, toxic snacks, and extreme violence.

Payment is cold hard cash… plus vouchers for a free weekly Whammy Burger meal for life.

Runs on a simple, fast-paced system. Level 2 pregenerated characters provided.

Gear up. Lock in.
The mall is open.
All sales are final.
---
Game Host: Steve Bulla
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Sonny Strait AMA
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Cenewulf speedruns
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Cenewulf speedruns- Space Harrier- Afterburner- TMNT (Turtles in Time)

events.title: Cosplay Chess ARENA! - FOR SCIENCE!
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] From the ashes of many cheesecakes, inspiration has arisen! The mad minds of the Cosplay Chess team have concocted a truly devious experiment. A cosplay battle arena with the fights themselves being performed FOR SCIENCE!

Witness the latest iteration of cosplay combat and see the greatest What If our mad scientists could concoct.

events.title: Social Deception: Tasters
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social Deception variety hours! Quick fast-paced games! A variety of choice! Experience a broad swath of Deception games you could play at home!

events.title: BOTC: Continuous Games
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Blood on the Clocktower: A social deduction game for 5-15 players! As many games as we can fit in the timeblock! Sign up for the next game, or join in midway as a traveler!

events.title: Moriguchika Fashion Show Doors Open
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage]

events.title: Flesh and Blood - Free Silver Age Precon Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Step into the arena with FREE ready-to-play Silver Age preconstructed decks, officially sponsored by LSS. Experience the thrill of strategic 1v1 combat while making new friends in a fun and welcoming tournament setting! This event is a great chance to learn your first hero or try out a new class.All participants also receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Japanese Tea Ceremony
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Presentation of Japanese culture through Japanese Tea Ceremony demonstration and lecture.

events.title: Street Fighter 6 Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] From the streets to Sakura-Con! Whether you're a Modern control newcomer or a Classic execution god, SF6 welcomes all challengers. Drive Impact your way into convention history. Top 3 win prizes!

events.title: Pokemon Film Review Hour
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] There are over 20 animated Pokemon films. Are any of them good? Do we only remember them because of nostalgia? Do any of them even pass the Bechdel test? We decided to watch and review them so you don’t have to.

events.title: Driving in the 90s, a primer on importing, maintaining and driving Japanese cars on U.S. roads
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] If you grew up playing the Kei Car races in Gran Tourismo, Watching the high-speed racing of Initial D, or just loving the look of cars used in the background of some of your favorite anime then this could be the panel for you! We're going to go over how to select, import, register, and maintain cars made for the Japanese market on the streets of the U.S. along with warnings about some of the pitfalls that come with it. If you saw my panel last year, this will be even more in depth and have even more information to help you make a better choice when buying a Japanese car. Including another year of ownership on the same vehicle I showed off last time. . So if you've ever found drive-thrus, ferry terminals, and automated parking garages to be just too darn convenient for you to enjoy, come to this panel by a silly guy who puts entirely too many miles on a vehicle that needs parts imported from the other side of the planet.

events.title: Toxic Yaoi Couples: That’s the Point and Why We Love Them
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Do you ever just look at a pair of guys fighting in a fit of passion, know that they make each other worse, and/or cheer for them to kiss? Wonder what the appeal is? It’s time to dissect and discuss the who’s and why’s of what makes certain yaoi couples toxic. But more importantly celebrate why we enjoy it.

events.title: Project Sekai Trivia
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Do you like Project Sekai? Do you like trivia games? If you answered yes to either, you'll love our Project Sekai trivia panel, complete with prizes -- Official and handmade! Come test your knowledge and hang out with us!

events.title: Level 1 Drama/Trauma Center: Medicine in Anime
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Come learn from an anesthetist how medical practice in anime intersects and compares with real life. There will be discussion on Demon Slayer, Apothecary Diaries, Ameku MD, Young Black Jack, and more. Also a great opportunity to learn about what working in the operating room and with surgeons is really like, how to perform an anesthesia airway exam, and thoughts on what makes good and bad "CPR songs."

events.title: What makes an Anime opening great?
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ever wonder what makes an anime opening great! Why some opening songs get stuck in you head long after watching the show? Well come and join us as we explore the evolution of the opening sequence and how some have climbed to the top!

events.title: Hiroshi Nagahama Autograph
events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.title: Alexandria RPG Library Presents: Classic, Modern & Indie RPGs
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Visit the Alexandria RPG Library and explore over a thousand RPG rulebooks, modules, and player aids from countless game systems. From classic D&D to indie darlings, from cyberpunk noir to magical pirates. We bring all the dice, maps, books, and supplies for you to play in or run a game.

Browse our books, roll up a character, or grab a ticket to join our events. Tickets for each day’s games are available in-person at the RPG Library (Arch Tower, Tahoma 4) at 10AM.

The Alexandria RPG Library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit based in Tacoma, Wash.

events.title: Board Game Library - Presented by Dragonflight Game Con
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Since 1980, Dragonflight Game Con has been introducing board game enthusiasts to a wide variety of games through their convention's extensive lending library of games. Now, you can experience that library here at Sakura-Con! Visit the Dragonflight Game Library in the Board and RPG Gaming Room, located on the Tahoma level of the Gaming Annex, to browse the collection of available games, check out a game, and play in the room's free-play area.

Dragonflight Game Con is a nonprofit, volunteer-run gaming convention that takes place every August in Bellevue, WA. Visit their website at https://dfgamecon.org/ to learn more.

events.title: Scandal Q&A
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Avatar: Seven Havens - What We Know So Far
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] 20 years after the debut of Avatar: The Last Airbender, co-creators Michael Dante DiMartino and Bryan Konietzko are expanding their beloved franchise again in 2027 with another 2D animated series: Avatar: Seven Havens. Set years after The Legend of Korra, both the physical and spiritual worlds have been devastated by a cataclysm, and the Avatar is feared as humanity’s destroyer rather than its savior. The premise signals a bold new direction for the franchise. Can Avatar: Seven Havens live up to the standards of what many consider one of the greatest animated series ever, or will it face the same divisive reception as Korra, Netflix’s 2024 adaptation, and a certain 2010 film best left unnamed? Join a PNW panelist, filmmaker, and lazy cosplayer alongside fellow local fans for an interactive discussion and Q&A on what Avatar fans truly deserve—and whether we’re actually going to get it.

events.title: Yaoi Bingo!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ready to put your Yaoi knowledge to the test and try to beat the odds and win grand BL prizes? Join us for a wild 18+ panel where we combine trivia of your favorite Boy Love media and the thrill of bingo sure to excite the Old Man Yaoi in all of us. No experience necessary, even the most newbie of fans have a chance at fun prizes and having a great time with fellow fujos.

events.title: The Evolution of the Magical Girl Genre: 1950 - 2010
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A survey of the Magical Girl genre from it's early beginnings in the 50's, to its codification in the 90's and explosion in the 00's. We'll be talking about notable anime that shaped this genre, as well as the media ecosystem that spawns them. This is part one of two panels, the part two will cover modern day magical girl anime.

events.title: Anime Music Pub Quiz!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Anime Music Pub Quiz is back!! Come on your own or group up with your friends to name different anime songs over several rounds. Whether it’s your favorite OP or an ending you’ve never heard before, there’s something here for everybody. Points, Prizes, and Pride for first place and best team name. Don’t skip the opening!

events.title: Moriguchika Fashion Show
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Magic: the Gathering – Free Curated Chaos Draft
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Presented by MTGPlatinum.com!

Come and enjoy a completely free, curated draft experience reaching through MTG history to provide you with an unpredictable experience you won’t find anywhere else

All Curated Chaos drafts are three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Anime Glasses Mega(ne) Panel!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Glasses are really versatile! Come see just how much anime can do with glasses as a prop and character design tool, with plenty of examples of funky frames and spectacularly bespectacled specimens. We've got the anime glasses gleam, we've got cyber glasses, we've got glasses with windshield wipers on them, we've got nerds and gentlemen, teachers and spies! The potential of glasses is truly terrifying.

events.title: AMV Showcase
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] AMVs to make you laugh, cry, cheer and just plain enjoy.

events.title: AMVs are a Joke!? How to Make Comedy AMVs
events.panelDescription:
[Fan Panel] Presented by CMDR Sariah and Winterblade AMVs

Have you ever seen a gut-busting AMV and wanted to make one just like it? Comedy AMVs can be some of the most memorable, diverse, and uplifting videos in any contest, and they can be either very simple or very complex! This panel will use several videos as case studies to showcase the many ways to brainstorm, edit, and fine-tune a comedy AMV. By the end, you’ll know how to squeeze every giggle out of your audience!

events.panelDescription: [Gaming]

events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "You are adventurers that have stumbled upon the funeral feast of Horwend the jarl. DCC is a fantasy adventure TTRPG and this is a 1st level adventure.

events.title: HEARTWAV3S All Around RDP
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Back again this year is HEARTWAV3’s all around RDP! A Random Dance Play (RDP) is where we play a variety of songs, and anyone who knows the choreography can jump in and dance. With 80+ songs across K-Pop, J-Pop, Vocaloid, and idol media, our RDP brings a wide assortment curated for everyone to enjoy!

events.title: Dreamland Maid Cafe
