events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Karaoke Idol Round 2
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: Cute or Creepy Romance in Anime the Final Chapter
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Back again for our fourth (and final?) year, and we've got all new clips from primarily your suggestions, but a few of our own finds as well. Romance in anime might look appealing on the screen, but if you saw it in real life, do you think it would be cute or creepy? Come join us as we watch some clips and decide just that! This is an audience participation panel, so be sure to bring your wit and romantic spirit.

events.title: Work in Japan on the JET Program: Info Session (Panel)
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Are you interested in working in Japan? The Japan Exchange Teaching (JET) Program has brought over 80,000 participants from more than 80 countries to work in schools, boards of education, and government offices throughout Japan! The JET Program is the only teaching exchange program managed by the government of Japan. Learn more about the application process and hear from former participants about their time in Japan and the impact that the JET Program has had on their lives.Note: A bachelor’s degree is a minimum requirement for this job opportunity.

events.title: HoloLive TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Whether you're just starting out with CFV or a seasoned veteran, if you have your own deck we invite you to come to this Constructed event and get to know your fellow CFV enjoying con-goer! So come jam some games, win some prizes, and earn a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday just for participating!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: HoloLive TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Do you like VTubers? Do you like Trading Card Games? Have I got news for you. These two things are together at last and you, yes YOU can come and learn to play this brand new TCG with all your favorite HoloLive personalities. Just come over to the TCG Room and we'll have someone waiting to teach you! All participants will receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday as well!

events.title: Riftbound - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Riftbound! A TCG based off of the world of League of Legends, but this time in physical card form! A brand new game from UVS Games & Riot Games, Riftbound provides League of Legends gameplay without the searing rage of going 0/8/3 on Summoner's Rift. Unfortunately when your opponent defeats you however, you will not be able to brush it off as 'support diff'. On the other hand, if you win you experience the joy that can only be obtained from getting 22 kills as Garen while the enemy top laner prays for the surrender vote to succeed.

All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

Disclaimer: Riftbound is very new, extremely popular, and impossible to get product for, so unfortunately we will not have anything extra to send you home with, sorry!

events.title: Guilty Gear Strive Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Rock and roll fighting game perfection! Simplified inputs but galaxy-brain strategy. Daisuke's vision delivered. Top 3 win prizes!

events.title: Guilty Gear Strive Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Blue shells, banana peels, and broken friendships! Race through Nintendo's finest tracks and prove you're the karting champion. Top 3 win prizes!

events.title: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate Tournament 4
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The final bracket before Top 8! This is your last shot to qualify for the championship. Bring your A-game. Win prizes!

events.title: Let's Take A Look at Chinese Animation
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Like anime but want to branch out in different languages/cultures? In this panel, we will take a look at Chinese animations (including ones made in Hong Kong and Taiwan) and get some recommendations from a longtime donghua watcher.

events.title: AMVs on the Gaydar
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] We're showing a curated list of our favorite Anime Music Videos from the last few years that showcase LGBT themes. To the delight of yuri and yaoi fans alike, with bi and trans works included in the lineup, these AMVs are just so gay!

events.title: Project Moon Trivia! (LCB/LC/LoR)
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join our Project Moon quiz show: Kahoot/Blooket-style questions that will put your knowledge to the test! We’ll have three categories, and the top two winners in each will walk away with prizes. Expect tough questions—you’ll need real lore-reading stamina to win. This is not a fair quiz! Expect SPOILERS FOR ALL GAMES Topic: Everything revolves around Project Moon media. From the games (Limbus Company, Library of Ruina, and Lobotomy corporation) Think you’re the smartest Project Moon fan out there? Prove it! Come hang out, have fun, and meet fellow fans of Project Moon!

events.title: D&D 5e (2024): Eight Kolbolds and a Baby (dragon): An Intro adventure
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "You have been hired to find and return the egg of a friendly copper dragon.
 A band of kobolds recently stole said egg and brought it to a nearby cave to eat. But when the egg hatched, and a screeching dragon wyrmling emerged, the kobolds quickly realized they bit off more than they could chew. The kobolds then tried to appease the fussy wyrmling with treasure, but they just can’t agree on what to do next. Should they return the wyrmling to its nest? Should they raise it themselves? They don't know what to do! The choice of how best to handle these draconic thieves? Now that is up to you! 
This is a lighthearted intro meant to take 100-110 minutes and give a taste of most D&D mechanics
---
Game Host: Courtney V
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players age 6 and up)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Avatar Legends: The Temple of Uktoa
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "The Earth Kingdom and the Fire Nation have both laid claim to Natsuo Island and the old temple that has been recently discovered there. Firelord Roku has asked the crew of the Unity to investigate the island and settle the matter to everyone’s satisfaction. Has the Earth Kingdom been the island’s stewards for generations as they claim? Was the temple built by Fire Nation sages to honor a dragon, as they claim? Can you get everyone to cooperate long enough to uncover the mystery of the island?

This adventure is playtest material from the upcoming Adventures of the Unity project from Magpie Games and Beadle & Grimm. Set in the era of Avatar Roku before the birth of Aang and the 100 Year War, these adventures focus on the team’s attempts to navigate rising tensions between nations that are preparing for war. No familiarity with the game or source material is required.
---
Game Host: Ryan
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players 6 and older welcome)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: David Matranga AMA
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Rhythm Gaming Freeplay
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Get your groove in with up to three other players in Just Dance, mimic silhouettes to iconic pop hits! Featuring other rhythm surprises as well!

events.title: Ghost in the Shell and the Stray Dog of Anime Mamoru Oshii
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] One of the most seminal anime directors to have ever lived Mamoru Oshii, has had an indelible mark on pop culture as a whole. While most famous in the United States for the 1995 Sci-Fi classic Ghost in the Shell, Oshii has created a number of other works covering ideas just as nuanced and thought provoking, with tones ranging from the melancholic to the absolutely absurd! If you are interested in exploring the underlying ideas of Ghost in the Shell, and finding other works that could possibly scratch that same itch, then join in as we discuss the career of Mamoru Oshii, a man always questioning whether our reality could really be true.

events.title: One Piece TCG - Free Starter Deck Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: The Secret History of Gundam Yaoi
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] As the beloved Gundam franchise nears its 50th anniversary, it's only appropriate that we hearken back to the beginning of what made this war story mecha franchise so great: its extremely dedicated fujoshi audience. This panel will examine the fundamental role that female fans of Gundam played in keeping the franchise afloat and how they shaped aspects of fan culture into the present day.

events.title: The Science of Pokémon Geography
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] From the volcanoes of Cinnabar Island to the caves of Mt. Moon, the geography of Pokémon hides a world of science waiting to be uncovered. Hosted by a geoscientist, we'll explore landscapes, biomes, and hazards that shape Pokémon habitats and cities. This panel blends science with worldbuilding to reveal the Pokémon world in a whole new way.

events.title: WACCA Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming]

events.title: Danganronpa 2 - Comic Con School Trip!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join the cast of Danganronpa 2 as they take a school trip to SakuraCon. After meeting the cast of characters, a class trial will commence. Attendees will have the opportunity to work with students like Mahiru and Nagito to help solve the mystery of who the blackened is.

events.title: Let's Talk About Major Character Death
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] There's no easy way to say it—death is an uncomfortable and deeply personal subject. A lot of people understandably avoid it. So why does fandom still have a small but dedicated population of writers telling stories where our favorite characters die, our ships are separated, and funerals are held? This craft panel seeks to create space for discussing the special role fandom and fanfiction can play in talking about grief and loss, how Major Character Death (MCD) is different in fanfic and original fiction, as well as how and why fanfic writers explore these themes in our work.

events.title: Girl's Intro To Cybersecurity
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Beginner-friendly panel inviting girls and young women to explore the career of cybersecurity. We'll break down the basics of the cybersecurity career, why it matters, and how anyone can get started.

events.title: AMQ Anime Music Quiz
events.panelDescription:
[Fan Panel] Do you like anime music!? Do you like game shows with fabulous prizes!? Then have we got the panel for you!

For those of you who don’t already know of it: Anime Music Quiz or AMQ is an online game where a 20 second random section of an anime opening plays and within that 20 seconds you have to guess the name of the anime it belongs to! We will be selecting contestants from the audience at random and giving out all manner of prizes depending on how well you do in the quiz and what difficulty you select!

 Whether you're a casual viewer or a hardcore anime fan, there’s something for everyone, and we can’t wait to see who’ll take home the title of Anime Music Quiz Champion and the GRAND PRIZE that goes along with it! Don’t miss out – come for the anime, stay for the prizes, and get ready to have a blast!

events.title: SCANDAL Autograph
events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.title: Call of Cthulhu: The Son of Nyx (Nyukusu No Musuko)
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "The year is 2098 and you are crew on the spaceship Sakigake conducting a mineral survey on the surface of Pluto. But when you begin your journey back to earth, disaster strikes. A red-black swirling celestial body crashes into Pluto causing untold destruction. The last thing you see before losing consciousness are the eyes of an unspeakable cosmic horror rising from the chaos. Can you escape your doom and return to earth? Find out in this scenario straight from Japan from their number one TTRPG Call of Cthulhu! New players welcome, pregens provided.
---
Game Host: AJ
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: 18+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Pathfinder Society: Lacking Respect [PFS2 Q23]
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "A Pathfinder Society Quest designed for 1st- through 4th-level characters, playable in 2-3 hours.
Not everyone in Irrisen has been happy with the rule of Queen Anastasia. A group of bandits found it much easy to sate their violent tendencies during the rule of the prior queen. Now they're causing trouble both in Irrisen and in settlements on its western border. It's up to the PCs to put a stop to their banditry.
---

Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Pathfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Starfinder Society: The Beasts of Bo: Part Two [SFS2 1-16]
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "A Starfinder Society Scenario designed for 3rd- through 4th-level characters, playable in 2-3 hours.
The Starfinder Society’s negotiations with Bo-Shek and the Beasts of Bo have been interrupted by a violent joint assault launched by agents of the Functionary and forces from the Wreck of the Returned. Amid chaos and explosions, the Starfinders on site team up with the Beasts of Bo to take down this greater threat and avoid their mutual destruction. Will you survive the assault? 

This is an adventure in the ongoing Invasion’s Edge metaplot, and is part two of a two-part scenario series. This adventure is a direct sequel to Starfinder Society Scenario #1-14: The Beasts of Bo: Part One.
---
