Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Starfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Pokemon Scarlet & Violet Tournament (Switch)
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Team Scyther's Swiss Doubles Tournament! Take part in a Pokemon doubles tournament using VGC standard rules (Regulation F). Bring your Switch and copy of Scarlet or Violet to participate! We will offer prizes to the top two spots and there will be door prizes! Detailed description of the format Using the most recent Pokemon games and switch console - must be provided by contestants to enter. Pokémon Restrictions: Available Regional Pokédex. Pokémon Limits: Team of 4 to 6 Pokémon from Level 1 to Level 100. All Pokémon are then set to Level 50.Time: 10 Minutes Team Preview: 90 Seconds Turn Time: 60 Seconds We are aiming to have side events and options for digital engagement in the Panel space, too. In previous years, we have had turnouts of 20-40 and have managed with smaller spaces, but we'd prefer to have enough space to have opponents facing each other at tables.

events.title: Aniplex of America Games Panel
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Calling all fans of Aniplex of America games! Come discover what’s next at our Aniplex of America Games panel, where we’ll dive into upcoming releases, exclusive content, and the latest developments across some of your favorite anime game titles.

events.title: Crunchyroll Presents: Classroom of the Elite Season 4 Premiere Screening
events.panelDescription: [Industry Panel] It’s their second year at the Advanced Nurturing High School and Ayanokouji’s class is already greeted with a new batch of exams and interesting first-year students to pair up with. They’ll need to get it together quickly because if they don’t, the second-years will be expelled! To make matters worse, one of the first-years is from the White Room. Can Ayanokouji avoid expulsion and figure out the identity of this hidden foe? Join us for the premiere screening of Classroom of the Elite

events.title: Learn to play mahjong
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Learn to play Japanese riichi mahjong with one of our teachers!

events.title: Cosplay Wrestling Federation: Sakurumble VI! - Day 2
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] The CWF, THE MOST ELECTRIFYING show in Anime Sports Entertainment, returns to the Pacific Northwest at Sakura-Con to proudly present to you SAKURUMBLE as a two-day event! This show will feature wrestlers of the CWF from franchises big and small, young and old as they clash to settle grudges, vie for superiority, defend their pride, and more, in a series of (contactless) exhibition-style battles of mic skills, bravado, and possibly shenanigans! Who will stand tall and who will tumble, in the sixth annual Sakurumble?! Only you can decide! Come join us to not only watch, but with your cheers and jeers steer the course of this exciting new installment of C! W! F!

events.title: Social Deception: Open-Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social Deception invites you to come play games from our collection! Self-guided, or run w/ staff help!

events.title: D&D in Media: A Critical Fail?
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you ever wished your D&D game was a cartoon? No? Well (un)fortunately for you it very much exists and unlike what your therapist tells you it CAN hurt you. And are there better D&D media examples now?

events.title: Moto Hagio: Reality, Gender and Identity
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Legendary writer and manga artist, Moto Hagio, called the "God of Shoujo Manga", has written some of the most influential stories across multiple genres but is scarcely known in popular media in the United States. This panel will discuss, in brief, some of the ground-breaking themes in her books and series that have contributed to and shaped many Japanese manga artists that followed after.

events.title: Pokemon TCG – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Sure, maybe you can identify all the original 151 just by their silhouettes, sing every word of “Gotta Catch ‘Em All” to yourself in the shower, and have a full shiny living ‘dex on Pokémon HOME - but if you really want to be the Champion, you have to be ready to take on all comers, anytime, anywhere… even at the card table.

That’s where we come in! Swing by and learn to play from some of the best trainers around, courtesy of Tabletop Village’s elite roster.

All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway – so start your climb to the top here, and aim to become the very best. You know it’s your destiny."

events.title: Random Kpop play dance 2010 -2020
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you a fan of K-Pop? Love the groups and their dancing? Then come join us for our Kpop random place dance! And show us those moves!

events.title: Pixel Perfect GMVs
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Need your gaming fix? Dive into the hub world of GMVs and celebrate with this AFK playlist of K.O. hits and rapid tap action!

events.title: AMV Editing in Blender: An Unlikely Engine
events.panelDescription:
[Fan Panel] Presented by Cybirdpunk

Come learn about the quirks of video editing in a 3D Modeling program, as well as discuss the process of picking media or music to edit for a project.

events.title: Take Your Heart: Celebrating 10 years of Persona 5
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Calling all Phantom Thieves! There will be a panel about the heartstealing JRPG known as Persona 5. A few Phantom Thieves will conduct the panel and we will be discussing about treasures, corrupt hearts, and maybe calling cards to bring forth a change of heart. if you must infiltrate Mementos or any Palaces as well as taking your time doing social activities during this panel, That's all good, This is a meeting for any Phantom Thieves that wish to attend! Warning: This panel may discuss topics such as abuse, self-harm, and other sensitive topics that Persona 5 depicts, Attendee discretion is advised.

events.title: Gyaru 101
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Curious about Gyaru? Want advice with your makeup and styling? Join Portland gals and learn about Gyaru history, di?erent substyles, and ask questions to aid your fashion journey!

events.title: D&D 5e: To Be The Very Best
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Festival bells cut loud through the air! Adventurers are gathering to celebrate the Wild Monster Capture Festival. Tame wild monsters, team up with them in honorable combat, and you could win not only gold, but fame and renown!
---
Game Host: Ryan Zimmerman
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players age 6 and up)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Fabula Ultima TTJRPG: PRESS START Tutorial: A Fated Descent
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "The award-winning Fabula Ultima is designed to recreate the wondrous worlds, characters and stories seen in videogames such as the legendary Final Fantasy series. In this BEGINNER FRIENDLY introductory adventure, set out on an airship to investigate a massive magic crater, the site of a mysterious, city razing arcane disaster.
---
Game Host: Andrew Harshman
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Old Gods of Appalachia: Best Leave Them Ghosts Alone
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Attending the funeral of a childhood friend is never an easy thing, but when it’s the funeral of someone you accidentally killed and then brought back to life years ago, well . . . you all have owes, and this one’s about to come due. Best Leave Them Ghosts Alone is an Old Gods of Appalachia horror adventure set in a small town full of memories, ghosts, and promises unpaid. This poignant adventure, rife with atmosphere, horror, and emotion, was an official Monte Cook Games event at Gen Con 2023, and is particularly well suited for use as a unique standalone experience.

The Old Gods of Appalachia Roleplaying Game is a horror game, and as such, is full of things that might disturb your dreams, follow in your footsteps, and haunt your waking hours. This adventure includes elements such child death, grief, funerals, ghosts, and hauntings.
---
Game Host: Juli
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: 18+ Due to horror theme

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: D&D 5e (2024): Notice Me Senpai! Improvised Rom-Com Adventure
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Help your friend get a date with their crush at Adventuring Camp! We will do a bit of mad-libs style of improv to create the love interest and the romantic rival as a group. The players will decide who is going to be the pursuer, and everyone else will be their wing-person in this adventure. Get your friend a date before camp ends! We will use pre-generated characters for this adventure that follow character trope archetypes. No DnD experience needed!
---
Game Host: Dario
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: 15+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: MARRIAGETOXIN Episode Screening Panel – Road to the Best Marriage!
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Come watch the world-premiere screening of the first episode of the new series MARRIAGETOXIN with the voice of the main character Hikaru Gero, Haruki Ishiya! Don’t miss the chance to see things unfold between the master assassin and marriage swindler!

events.title: Triple Fusion Tunes: A Tribute Concert To The Persona Series
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] This will be a tribute concert to the persona series with an assorted setlist of songs from Persona 3,4, and 5 performed by the host. She first did this concert almost two years ago as part of an event for her Music Production Club at UW Bothell and now she is bringing it to a wider audience of persona fans like herself.

events.title: Zapp's Cosplay Dressup Battle, of Love!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Go toe-to-toe with your friends, enemies, frenemies, or even random people, in a fast paced cosplay competition! Be given mystery cosplay ideas, then see who can best capture the theme as you dress up our talented, beautiful, and slightly dumb cosplay models! That’s right, no matter how embarrassing your idea, you don’t even have to wear it!

events.title: Music from Video Games: Live!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Live performance of music from some of our favorite video games; arranged for wind quintet, plus fantastic guest artists. Video game music encompasses an incredible variety of genres and styles, setting the mood for your immersive experience. We will bring that magic to your con, showcasing music from the soundtracks of Hades, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, Legend of Zelda, and more.

events.title: From AUs to OCs: the Jump from Fanfiction to Self-Publishing
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] You write amazing fanfiction, but now you've got original characters and stories, too. This panel will discuss how the skills you gained through writing fanfic will help you in the self-publishing market and answer your questions about how to self-publish your original fiction.

events.title: Doomed Yaoi Tournament: How doomed are they?
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Doomed yaoi? We hardly know her! Or do we? With BL on the rise, we bring you a bracket-style tournament that includes audience debate participation and voting. We aim to answer the ultimate question: Who is the most doomed yaoi pairing? Players will volunteer to argue on behalf of a doomed yaoi ship on the bracket (if no volunteers, then panelists will step in, don't worry), and then the audience will vote on who they feel is more doomed.

events.title: A Decade of Osomatsu-san: A Fan Retrospective
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ten years ago, Osomatsu-san brought gag manga characters from the 1960s into the modern age with a bombastic reboot. Since then we’ve had four anime seasons, movies, games, and oh so much merchandise featuring the iconic identical sextuplets. How has Osomatsu’s success changed the landscape of anime, and how has Osomatsu changed? Whether you can't tell the Matsunos apart or you're a die-hard Karamatsu Girl, come join us!

events.title: Kari and Lisa chat Dandadan!
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Aliens or ghosts? Join Kari and Lisa as they chat about their experiences working on the hit series Dandadan!

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: I Watched the Lowest-Rated BL Animes: Bigger and Longer
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Notorious titles you've heard about- Legend of the Blue Wolves, Boku no Pico- I watched the worst-rated BL animes of all time. I'm here to tell you if they were misjudged, or if they deserve to be purged from the face of the earth. Join us for the drama, the insanity, the genuinely sweet and the genuinely traumatizing moments. (Extended Edition)

events.title: Inside GeekGirlCon
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] GGC is an annual convention that takes place in fall every year that celebrates the contributions of under-represented communities in comics, gaming, STEM, sci-fi/fantasy, fandom, and more! Join a panel of our volunteer staff, contributors, and community members to learn about what makes this convention so special and get a peek at what’s coming in 2026!

events.title: Grand Archive – Free Starter Deck Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Step into the arena with FREE ready-to-play Silver Age preconstructed decks, officially sponsored by LSS. Experience the thrill of strategic 1v1 combat while making new friends in a fun and welcoming tournament setting! This event is a great chance to learn your first hero or try out a new class.All participants also receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Grand Archive – Free Starter Deck Tournament
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] You’ve learned how to start the fight, Champion – now let’s find out if you can finish it. Take up your provided starter deck, and go toe-to-toe with your peers in a test of skill alone. But remember: even in defeat, there is victory… in the form of a ticket to the Grand Giveaway.

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

All material graciously provided by Grand Archive’s developer Weebs of the Shore. Thank you for your continued support, WotS!

events.title: Grand Archive – Free Starter Deck Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] If you've heard of any single TCG it's probably Magic: The Gathering. If you've never dabbled in TCGs, or have just somehow never learned to play MTG, we have skilled volunteers who will make it their mission to ensure that you learn to start slinging spells and forgetting to untap your mana for the seventh time this game. If you want to learn the grand-daddy of them all, come over to TCGs and we'll get you started! All participants will receive a ticket to the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: Magic: The Gathering - Free Dragonstorm Tarkir Draft
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come and enjoy a completely free Draft experience at Sakura-Con!All drafts are three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway, as well as one pack per win!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Free Sealed Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Magical Girl Tea-Time: And Meetup
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Tired of having nobody to voice your hot magical girl anime takes to? Well join some of your favourite magical girls in a discussion-like panel where your hosts share some of their hottest (and possibly controversial) takes on some of the most iconic magical girl animes! THE END WILL ALSO BE A COSPLAY MEET-UP, SO COME IN YOUR FAVORITE MAGICAL GIRL COSPLAY FOR PICTURES TOWARDS THE END!

events.title: Nyan Neko Sugar Girls Nyaow and Forever <3
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you ready for the Ultimate Nyan Neko Sugar Girls experience of the decade? New to the fandom and want to learn deep lore from the series? Well then see you here neko chans! Nya rawr <3 ?^•?•^?

events.title: 10 Years of the r/anime Awards!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] This year marks the 10th year of Reddit's Anime Awards! Join some of this year's jurors as we explore the history of the awards and look back on this year's winners.

events.title: The Evolution of the Magical Girl Genre: 2010 - present
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A follow up to the Magical Girl history panel! In this one, we'll be covering more contemporary works ranging from 2010 onward! I'll be focusing on the different directions creators have gone with the tropes. Please note that this panel covers a wide variety of magical girl anime, some of which are intended for adults.

events.title: Battles With and Without Honor: Exploring Yakuza in Film
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Family and betrayal. Hope and oppression. Gun and sword. Join us as we explore the history of Japanese gangs across the country’s cinema, from the roots of the genre in honorable samurai films to the explosive action films of modern times. We’ll touch on a variety of topics—such as the influences of Japan’s post-war prosperity and class struggle, major directors such as Kinji Fukasaku and Takashi Miike, and a slew of must-watch yakuza films, both the violent and the contemplative. Then stick around for an open audience discussion on the yakuza genre—whether you want to share your own facts or just talk about your favorite movies with others, you’re welcome to join!

events.title: NEEDY GIRL OVERDOSE - Special Feature Screening!
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Are you ready to gaze upon the greatest “Internet Angel” to captivate the world wide web?Join KAngel and the rival group Karamazov in this chaotic internet of the modern day, as we bring you an exclusive US showing of episodes in a special arrangement previously shown only in Japanese theaters!Come enjoy a special nearly 60-minute preview screening of the TV anime NEEDY GIRL OVERDOSE!(This screening contains mature content. Viewer discretion is advised.)

events.title: Is That An Isekai? You As An Attendee Can Cast A Vote!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join this panelist & co. by voting on a variety of both Eastern and Western shows/movies/books/etc. as to whether you think they count as Isekai or not! This will be a casual panel with some back-and-forth by the two panelists and a majority vote from the audience for each concept. Every time a vote is taken, the winning result will be recorded. This panel will be held at multiple other conventions in the US over the next few years in order to get data on how regional differences may affect these opinions.

events.title: Gei Komi: A History of Gay Erotic Manga
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Come learn about gei komi (aka bara) and all its thick-bodied history. CONTENT WARNING: muscles, beefcakes, chubsters, and more ;)

events.title: Fifth Gen Isn’t Pokemon
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Generation five of pokemon introduced a lot of new concepts to the series like ripping off their previous aesthetic designs, min-maxing stats to optimize the removal of actual pokemon from competitive play, and whatever Garbador is. When it comes down to it the abominations that were introduced in Pokemon Black and White are nowhere near as majestic as classic pokemon like Muk, Mr. Mime, and Electrode.

events.title: Cosplay Contest Doors Open
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage]

events.title: Digimon Card Game - Free Starter Deck Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: AMV Iron Editor Seating
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Seating for AMV Iron Editor

events.title: AMV Iron Editor
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] One of the AMV Theater's most popular events! 2 Editors will go head to head in a live editing competition where they will have to make an AMV on the spot to the theme “Totally 80s!" There will be games and prizes so audience participation is a must!

events.title: D&D 5e (2024): Eight Kolbolds and a Baby (dragon): An Intro adventure
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "You have been hired to find and return the egg of a friendly copper dragon.
 A band of kobolds recently stole said egg and brought it to a nearby cave to eat. But when the egg hatched, and a screeching dragon wyrmling emerged, the kobolds quickly realized they bit off more than they could chew. The kobolds then tried to appease the fussy wyrmling with treasure, but they just can’t agree on what to do next. Should they return the wyrmling to its nest? Should they raise it themselves? They don't know what to do! The choice of how best to handle these draconic thieves? Now that is up to you! 
This is a lighthearted intro meant to take 100-110 minutes and give a taste of most D&D mechanics
---
Game Host: Courtney V
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players age 6 and up)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Blitz Shogi Handicap Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] A fun blitz Tournament. Games are handicap so all players have a chance to win.

events.title: Pokemania: A Look Back at When Pokemon Took the World by Storm
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Move over Mickey, there's a new mouse in town! When Pokemon was first released in 1996, few people realized just how big it would become. Over the next few years, it took the world by storm and started a Pokemon obsession that would lead to the most profitable media franchise in the world. Whether you're hoping to relive those glory days or wishing to get a glimpse into a time when you weren't even born yet, join us as we look back at the Pokemon craze of 1999 in North America.

events.title: Mecha Modeling Contest
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] The contest will have prizes across five categories:

1. Best Over All
2. Custom Build
3. Advanced Build
4. Straight Build
5. People’s Choice

While we do recommend pre-registering for the contest, on-site registration will also be available until 2 pm on Saturday, with judging and the awards ceremony taking place on Saturday at 6pm.

Pre-registration can be done using the form here :

https://forms.gle/Hp2gxn8mH3soNG3ZA

To participate in the contest, you will need to bring your kit to the Mecha Modeling room anytime on Friday or Saturday before 2pm. Only one entry is allowed per person. When registering, please choose the appropriate category for your kit. We reserve the right to move your kit to a different category if we feel it is necessary. People’s Choice voting will take place during the official judging so come to our room at 6pm on Saturday if you’d like to make your voice heard.

events.title: Improvaganza
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A whole different kind of improv comedy show from the panelists who bring you Whose Line is it Anyway. With all new games to play, such as Mission Improbable, Time Warp, Film Noire, and more. Come join us for all the laughs and fun.

events.title: Cosplay Contest
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage] See your favorite characters come to life on stage!  The Sakura-Con Cosplay Contest is a competition showcase of costume construction and stage performance, open to Cosplayers of all levels of experience.

events.title: HoloLive TCG - Free Beginner Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Whether you've just learned to play or are already a fan, this beginner tournament is for you. Loaner decks will be available for this event as well, so come on over and we'll get you some cool prizes for your effort as well! And as always, all participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday.Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: One Piece TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] This one's new! Echoes of Astra is a brand-new TCG from Parhelion Studios, an anime art themed game that mixes traditional TCG Gameplay with the tactical depth of war gaming! All wrapped in the package of some beautiful cardboard. If you want to learn a brand new TCG that is just starting this is your chance to get in on the ground floor! All participants will receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!

All our materials for Echoes of Astra were graciously donated by Parhelion Studios, so a big thank you to them for allowing us to show their game to you. Come over to the TCG Room and one of our experienced volunteers will teach you all about this tactically rich, exciting new game!

events.title: One Piece TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come learn the Netrunner TCG directly from the team developing it! Null Signal Games has sent us their very best to teach you how to be the best Runner you can be! All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: One Piece TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Obtain Fame, Wealth, and Power beyond your wildest dreams by coming to the TCG Room and learning to play the One Piece TCG! Will you be able to find the buried treasure- Or perhaps the treasure is actually the friendships you formed in your journey? No matter what, if you complete a learn to play session you will receive a ticket for our Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: FGC Trivia 2
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Round 2 of fighting game knowledge! More questions, more rounds, more chances to flex your FGC expertise. Grab your team and dominate! Win prizes!

events.title: FGC Trivia 2
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The indie fighting game with AAA animation! Infinite combo protection, incredible art, and a passionate community. Win prizes!

events.title: FGC Trivia 2
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The parry game that defined a generation! Show Sakura-Con why 3rd Strike is still the thinking player's Street Fighter. Every match is a chance to go down in convention history. Win prizes!

events.title: Tekken 8 Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The King of Iron Fist Tournament continues! Heat system brings the aggression. Family drama has never been this hype. Top 3 win prizes!

events.title: Inuyasha and Trauma Informed Care
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] For the second year in a row, come learn about the principles of trauma informed care through the lens of one of your favorite characters, Inuyasha! We'll discuss how Inuyasha's trauma has informed his behavior and connect trauma informed care to the positive interactions that lead to his character development. Open to all, healthcare providers and those interested in learning about caring for loved ones with trauma.

events.title: Seattle Cosplay Resources (On a Budget!)
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] This panel is designed to help cosplayers on a budget achieve their prop and costume dreams. As someone who makes cosplay out of a lot of recycled materials, I wanted to share the resources in Seattle I've found that have helped with that. This panel will also include local fabric/material stores that have relatively cheap options compared to options online. After Joanne's closed down, I think it's important to spread awareness of the small businesses our community has easily accessible to us. Cosplay is for everyone!

events.title: How to Idol: Dance Workshop
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you interested in learning to dance like your favorite idols? Come learn tips & tricks from experienced idols & dancers! We'll be teaching a short J-pop dance - all skill levels welcome, no experience required!

events.title: Monthly Girls’ Nozaki-kun Episodes 1-4
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed)

High schooler Chiyo Sakura finally works up the courage to confess her feelings to her crush, Umetarou Nozaki—only to discover he’s a renowned shōjo manga artist who mistakes her confession for fan admiration. Instead of romance, she receives an autograph and an offer to become his assistant. Determined to get closer to him, Sakura steps into the chaotic, behind-the-scenes world of manga creation.

events.title: Asobi☆Pawtime: Pawsitively Kaigai Idols!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join Asobi☆Pawtime and friends for a setlist of fun and interactive kaigai idol performances! Bring your penlights and learn some wota, let's hear your loudest cheers!!!

events.title: Break!!RPG: A Completely Normal Adventure
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "The peaceful domain of Millbrook faces its darkest hour. A monstrous beast stalks the woods, leaving destruction in its wake. The just Lord Vance grieves for his kidnapped daughter, Princess Helena, taken from her very tower. His scheming Vizier whispers plots in shadowed halls while a mad street prophet warns of apocalyptic doom. Anarchists stir in the taverns, an orphan child claims mythic power, and a traveling merchant dealing in secrets watches it all unfold with knowing eyes. 

You've been called to save the princess, slay the beast, and restore order to this troubled land. It should be simple, every hero knows this story. But as you peel back the layers of Millbrook's tragedy, you'll find things are not as they seem. 

Even monsters, they say, can love.

Expect twists, moral complexity, and the chance to either prevent-or survive-a spectacular disaster.
---
Game Host: Mia
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Exalted Essence: Spiritual Warfare
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "You and your allies are working to demonstrate the illegitimacy of the opressive Realm by undermining their divine mandate. Use your social-fu and sorcerous might to talk,  cajole, seduce (pg-13), bind, and ensnare the gods and spirits of the Blessed Isle to rebel. 

This will be more role play and social combat heavy rather than physical combat. 

Choose from premade characters including subversive anarchist punk rock kings of hell, shapeshifting infiltrators with useful backers, to inheritors of the divine god kings making amends for their past lives' sins.

CW structural oppression and systems of coercive control. 
---
Game Host: Jeremy Gwinner
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Pokemon Legends: ZA Tournament (Switch)
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Team Scyther's ZA Royale! Take part in a Pokemon battle royale tournament using Legends ZA pokedex (with DLC content, no legendaries). Bring your Switch and copy of Pokemon Legends ZA to participate! We will offer prizes to the best play records and free play will be available! Using the all pokemon available in Legends ZA and Mega Dimension DLC other than legendaries. 4 on 4 battles alongside 1 on 1 battles will be played to determine who takes the title of Royale Champion. Item clause will be in effect.

events.title: Q&A with Chris Hackney
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Favorite character? Memorable moments? Best places to eat in LA?! It's your time to ask Chris anything (within reason) you want and get the scoop straight from the horse's mouth! Come chill, ask your Q's, and let's learn about each other.

events.title: Why BL isn’t Just Eye Candy: Representation in ALL Flavours
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] If a panel about the wonders of BL sounds even remotely up your alley, then have we got the chance encounter for you! This is the perfect opportunity to hear about BL from an enthusiast of the genre, and why it's perfect for all kinds of readers and viewers. From manga to anime to book, there's lots to talk about with this expansive media category. This lecture-style talk about BL is perfect for the open mind, and who knows, maybe there's a thing or two to be learned!

events.title: Mecha History: Armageddon
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] In the history of mecha anime, there's not much that can't be discussed in an ordinary panel... but what's there is pretty funny! The first half of this panel will be a shortened version of the history discussed in the other panel, with a few extra details I can't cover. While the second half is all about the history of mecha in video games, which I just don't have time to cover in the other panel!

events.title: Intro to Vinyl Anime Dolls
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A presentation on vinyl anime dolls like Dollfie Dream, Obitsu, and Smartdoll. We will cover brands, doll care, and how to customize your doll. We will demonstrate aspects such as pose-ability and customization. This panel is SC-13 but contains sensitive content as vinyl dolls are intended for a 15+ audience. The dolls and panel are not intended for young children.

events.title: Anime Murder Mystery
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A body has been discovered! There has been a murder at Sakura-Con and it is up to you to help in the criminal investigation. Question suspects and find the culprit in this thrilling, interactive anime-themed mystery presented by Anime Discovery Project at UW.

events.title: The Alfee: The Best Japanese Band You've Never Heard Of
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] With 51 years of continuous activity, 59 consecutive Top 10 singles, and nearly 3000 concerts, The Alfee is Japan’s premier arena rock band. We’ll talk about their contributions to anime and tokusatsu music, along with their killer vocal harmonies, Biblically-accurate guitars, the time bandleader Toshihiko Takamizawa played Ultraman in a short, and much more.

events.title: SCARYS! Haunted Stage
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Local Kaigai Idol SCARY! performing a setlist of your anime opening faves!

events.title: Daggerheart: Rising Through the Fell Graves
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Finally, on this rainy, dreary night, you and your party have almost caught up to Dr. Sincipital, an infamous and prolific necromancer. The only thing standing in your way is a labyrinth of a graveyard and the feeling that something is tethered to your spirit. Fight, claw, and blast your way through undead to reach the man you're hunting with the Daggerheart system! 
---
Game Host: Eden
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Ryuutama: Eat Your Veggies Before They Eat You – A Delicious In Dungeon Inspired Adventure
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Calling all dungeon delvers with an appetite for adventure! Rumor abounds of a mythical, monstrous vegetable that grants amazing strength and magical powers. You and your party are tasked with acquiring this legendary vegetable while eating your way through the dungeon one monster at a time. Cook up your kills and see how it effects your skills. Will you create a stat-boosting feast, or flop with status-effecting slop?

Learn to play Ryuutama, described as “Hayao Miyazaki’s Oregon Trail,” during this homebrewed adventure with level 2 characters. Bring your imagination and let's get cooking!
---
Game Host: Camille
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players 6 and older welcome)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Faye Mata AMA
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Meet a useless Goddess, a pink haired boy, a sexy genius fox girl, and a tiny mischievous yordle creature with a fairy all at once! Faye Mata has voiced over 100 credited characters in video games and animated series, worked as a games industry program manager, an eSports competitor, and a wholesome content creator, and you can ask her anything!

events.title: Blue Encount Concert
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Anime so bad it's good ... the second course
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] We all know about great anime, wonderfully crafted stories, and breathtaking performances. But on the other end of the spectrum is camp! Media that is so bad, it’s good. Join us O_O ...as we serve up a brand new menu of some of the campiest and schlockiest anime.

events.title: Hear Me Out
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Is there a character from an anime or other piece of media that you’ve got the hots for, even if there’s no logical explanation for it? We want to hear your most galaxy brained, unhinged takes on which characters (or abstract concepts) you have a crush on!

events.title: JET Program Alumni Meet-Up
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] “Once you’re a JET, you’re always a JET!” The Consulate-General of Japan in Seattle is teaming up with the PNW JET Alumni Association to host the JET Alumni Meet-Up! There is life beyond the BoE, and truth can be stranger than fiction when it comes to the daily life of a JET working in Japan! We know you’re here: come meet other nerdy alumni to connect, reminisce, and share your real-life anime-zing stories! If you're not a yet a JET, come join us to learn about the “JET Experience” from those who have “been there and done that” at this relaxed, fun, and informal gathering.

events.title: Magic: the Gathering – Free Curated Chaos Draft
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Presented by MTGPlatinum.com!

Come and enjoy a completely free, curated draft experience reaching through MTG history to provide you with an unpredictable experience you won’t find anywhere else

All Curated Chaos drafts are three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate Top 8 Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The best of the best clash in the grand finals! Watch the tournament champions crowned in the hypest Smash matches of the weekend!

events.title: The Realities of Found Family: Adoption in Japan
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] From SPY×FAMILY to Ace Attorney, there are many heartwarming and dramatic portrayals of families formed by means besides genetic lineage. How does real life compare though? What facets of adoption don't make it into your favorite anime or JRPG? What differences are there between adoption in the USA and adoption in Japan? If you're interested in learning more, you can come listen to me yap about all this.

events.title: Asexuality in Manga and More
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Looking for asexual and/or aromantic characters in manga? There’s more than you may think! Come and learn about asexuality in Japan and the representations of asexual and aromantic identity in Japanese media.

events.title: Daggerheart: Witch of the Meadowbrook Bog
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "There is a distraught parent in the tiny town of Meadowsbrook, asking for assistance to have their lost infant returned.  With the challenge of political vying between the mayor Montgomery and the witch Agatha, duplicitous dealings from community members, and no right answer, the party will be thrust into a situation that challenges their morals, ethics, and notions.  This session will include topics of natalism, memory manipulation, and small community dynamics.  Character sheets and ability cards provided!
---
Game Host: Æther K (they/xe)
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Fallout Roleplaying Game: The Needle of the Emerald City
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Discover the world of Fallout from the pacific northwest. See how the world has changed and stayed the same. Find out why the city is called the emerald city, what is the needle and deal with different raiders.
---
Game Host: Dave
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Monster of the Week : The Curse of Whispering Pines
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Love dark modern fantasy? Love wacky punk antics? Then you'll love Monster of the Week! Each party memeber picks one role to form their team of radical monster hunters!

""The small town of Whispering Pines, WV was once a picturesque site along winding state roads where city folks would drive up to take photos of the fall colors as the dense forest trees turned in the autumn. In recent years, however, strange disappearances have left the otherwise-peaceful town worried that someone dangerous is on the loose."" 
---
Game Host: Silver Pendragon
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: 18+ I like to get a tad edgy :3

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Alexandria RPG Library Presents: Classic, Modern & Indie RPGs
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Visit the Alexandria RPG Library and explore over a thousand RPG rulebooks, modules, and player aids from countless game systems. From classic D&D to indie darlings, from cyberpunk noir to magical pirates. We bring all the dice, maps, books, and supplies for you to play in or run a game.

Browse our books, roll up a character, or grab a ticket to join our events. Tickets for each day’s games are available in-person at the RPG Library (Arch Tower, Tahoma 4) at 10AM.

The Alexandria RPG Library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit based in Tacoma, Wash.

events.title: Board Game Library - Presented by Dragonflight Game Con
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Since 1980, Dragonflight Game Con has been introducing board game enthusiasts to a wide variety of games through their convention's extensive lending library of games. Now, you can experience that library here at Sakura-Con! Visit the Dragonflight Game Library in the Board and RPG Gaming Room, located on the Tahoma level of the Gaming Annex, to browse the collection of available games, check out a game, and play in the room's free-play area.

Dragonflight Game Con is a nonprofit, volunteer-run gaming convention that takes place every August in Bellevue, WA. Visit their website at https://dfgamecon.org/ to learn more.

events.title: Learn Para Para!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Learn a short Para Para dance routine with gyaru! Para Para is a Japanese synchronized dance style performed to Eurobeat music. It became popular among gyaru in the 90's and early 2000's! Para Para mainly involves side stepping and arm movements, so it's perfect for both very beginner dancers, and is fun for those more experienced as well!

events.title: 18+ Lip Sync Battle Doors Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Spicy Pokemon Jeopardy
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Hey guys, did you know that a Gardevoir is coming to you tonight with a brutally spicy Jeopardy game? Do iconic Pokemon like Vaporeon, Lucario, and Lopunny make you feel a certain way? In this fast-paced barrage of cultured Pokemon content, teams of enlightened adults compete against each other and the entire audience for prizes. This Python-based Jeopardy game that some random Gardevoir cosplayer coded from scratch will feature thighs, grippers, memes, and other stimulating categories. Regardless of previous experience with the Pokemon franchise, attendee participation is crucial in each of the two rounds that will be played! Come, cringe, and laugh while indulging in the chaos flowing through the screen! Warning: After this panel, you may be able to 1) identify Pokemon based on conspicuous features, 2) perceive certain Pokemon in ways you never have before, and 3) more fully enjoy your convention experience.

events.title: Multiverse Comedy Show
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] The world, nay the universe needs to laugh! Using the Improbability Drive to out-maneuver the Vogon fleet; we’ve gathered personalities from across the galaxies for a brand new show! Introducing "Multiverse Got Talent", a cosplay comedy show that unites fandoms to prove that humor brings us together. Using the Improbability drive; your host, Steve Katz, will be your guide for the evening, as you journey across the bridge of the Enterprise; or the swamps of Far Far Away; or into the TARDIS itself. No matter who pops in, you will leave wanting more. Remember, Don’t Panic… cause its time to laugh! (Simply put: comedians in cosplay performing in character; and it’s a ton of fun!)

events.title: Japanese Music on the Rise
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] How did Japanese music find so much success in the East Asian (and worldwide) market when genres such as KPop exists? This panel aims to answer that question with statistics, facts, and some rocking music!

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: 18+ Lip Sync Battle
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Unleash your inner superstar in this high-energy lip sync battle for adults only! Whether you’re channeling your favorite diva, rocking out to a power ballad, or bringing the heat with a sultry performance, this is your chance to take the stage and captivate the crowd. With no limits on creativity (or a bit of sass), participants will battle it out to claim the title of Best In Show in this electrifying 18+ Lip Sync Battle.

events.title: Anime Survivor
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Anime Survivor takes your favorite anime characters and strands them on an island where they have to compete until only one is left. We utilize a web-based simulator to find out once and for all which anime character has what it takes to be the sole survivor.

events.title: Cosplay Figure Drawing
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Need inspiration for your next piece? Want to reference your favorite character irl? Just want to practice fun poses? Come to our figure drawing studio! The sessions will consist of several rounds of incrementally timed sets with different poses and models in cosplay. Come with your art tools of choice, or pencils and paper will be provided We will have models of different body types and from multiple fandoms. All models will be clothed in accordance with Sakuracon dress code and policies.

events.title: Riftbound - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Riftbound! A TCG based off of the world of League of Legends, but this time in physical card form! A brand new game from UVS Games & Riot Games, Riftbound provides League of Legends gameplay without the searing rage of going 0/8/3 on Summoner's Rift. Unfortunately when your opponent defeats you however, you will not be able to brush it off as 'support diff'. On the other hand, if you win you experience the joy that can only be obtained from getting 22 kills as Garen while the enemy top laner prays for the surrender vote to succeed.

All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

Disclaimer: Riftbound is very new, extremely popular, and impossible to get product for, so unfortunately we will not have anything extra to send you home with, sorry!

events.title: Flesh and Blood - Free Draft Night Party!
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Swing big for a knockout as cheers and boos amp up the volume in this Super Slam booster draft! Build a deck on the fly as a face (Revered) or heel (Reviled) to play in a limited environment that will 100% surprise you with its technical depth and intricacies. Brownie points if you watched that one SuperEyePatch Wolf video that uses anime to explain pro wrestling! Also we respect Brody King in this house.  All participants also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

BONUS: If you see a bald guy in a power-chair (Or just ask for Stein) and tell him your favorite wrestler, he'll give you an extra promo card! 

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Beyond Ito: A Horror Anime and Manga Iceberg
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] This panel takes audiences on a guided descent through the horror anime and manga iceberg, beginning with familiar titles and plunging into the genre’s most obscure and unsettling depths. Hosted by the Seattle Scare Society, the discussion explores how horror evolves across anime and manga while highlighting hidden gems and extreme themes. Attendees can also expect interactive trivia throughout the panel, with chances to win prizes as they test their knowledge and journey deeper into the iceberg together.

events.title: Eco Friendly Cosplay
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you a cosplayer concerned about the environment? Join a veteran cosplayer to learn about ways you can cosplay while reducing your environmental impact, from material choices to techniques.

events.title: Oh, You Call Yourself a Fujoshi!?: Guess That BL Manhwa Quiz
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A presentation and quiz for those obsessed with Boys Love and think they know every Boys Love media known to man! A three level difficulty quiz game where the audience will be given clues about a manhwa/manhua and have to guess which manhwa/manhua it is, with a special BL Manga category at the end for the BIGGEST Fujoshis in the room! THIS IS STRICTLY BOYS LOVE, YAOI, AND SHOUNEN ALTERED CONTENT

events.title: BLUE ENCOUNT Post-Concert Autograph
events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.title: D&D 5e: To Be The Very Best
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Festival bells cut loud through the air! Adventurers are gathering to celebrate the Wild Monster Capture Festival. Tame wild monsters, team up with them in honorable combat, and you could win not only gold, but fame and renown!
---
Game Host: Ryan Zimmerman
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players age 6 and up)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Himblogy 101
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Do you like dumb, yet incredibly attractive fictional men? Do you want to learn more about them or just appreciate them more? Then come on down to this fun, yet educational panel on the history, love, and appreciation of himbos in anime and pop culture!

events.title: Idol Game Night in the School SEKAI ?
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Oh my goodness gracious! A moth-like cryptid has hacked into the School SEKAI and is holding someone hostage! The only way for you and Leo/Need to save him is to play... idol franchise-themed minigames!? Join us in four rounds of games catered towards fans of various idol medias in order to earn points! Anything from fan favorite to niche is more than encouraged- your elite ball knowledge will be needed for the trivia rounds...

events.title: Women in Jrock
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join us for a fun listening session where we’ll dive into the music of the female artists making waves in the J-rock scene. Who knows, you might discover your new favorite artist!

events.title: AMV Tournament Bracket
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] AMVs going head to head! Action vs Trailer? Linkin Ball Z vs Madoka Chaos? Who won? Who's next? YOU decide!

events.title: AMV Sommelier
events.panelDescription:
[Fan Panel] Presented by August Jonson and Marlon Jequinto

Whether you’re new to AMVs or a seasoned connoisseur, we will help you find the right videos for any occasion. Come and enjoy our array of choices as we present flight after flight of AMVs to satisfy any taste — all hand-picked and free of additives and pretentiousness!

events.title: Race to the "Top”!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ever looked at a character in a totally-not-yaoi anime and thought, “Yeah… he’s definitely a seme”? Well, tonight is your night. Join us for the ultimate bracket battle where eight “Seme” contenders and eight “Uke” icons from across anime go head-to-head — and you help decide who claims the crown. Expect chaotic debates, unhinged logic, passionate shipping energy, and possibly several friendships being questioned. As the bracket narrows, we’ll reach the Final Showdown where the winning Seme and Uke face one last fate-defining question: Do they ship? Audience participation is highly encouraged. Judgment will be loud. Logic will be questionable. And yes, we’re talking about that kind of power dynamic.

events.title: Delta Green: Welcome to Delta Green
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "As a member of Delta Green your job is to investigate where normal ends. Unexplained deaths, disappearances, secrets and legends, that kind of stuff. It’s your first mission, a former agent has died. They say it’s natural causes, you just need to make sure…
---
Game Host: Matt
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Nachatte Murder Mystery: A Pseudo Murder Mystery : A Snowed-In Tragedy
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "In the remote, snow-covered mountains, a secluded cabin offers warmth and refuge to weary travelers. A place to drink, laugh, and escape the bitter cold. But not all who enter seek shelter. Last night was supposed to be a night of celebration, but when you wake with a pounding headache and a hazy memory, you realize something is terribly wrong. A body lies cold in the living room, and none of you can recall what happened. Was it an accident? A crime of passion? Or something far more sinister? With the storm raging outside and no way to leave, there’s only one way forward; piece together the events of last night before the truth is buried beneath the snow forever.
---
Game Host: NiiNya
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: BrightNRose Kpop Dance Workshop
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Haven't you heard? We're the internet group. *insert zucchini here* BrightNRose is excited to return and wants to teach more K-pop choreographies! We will be teaching 1-2 new/recent song choreographies. This workshop panel is welcoming to dancers of all skill levels. You just have to come ready to have fun!

events.title: Paul Tesi (NES/SNES speedruns)
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Paul Tesi (NES/SNES speedruns)- Ninja Gaiden 3- 1991 NCC- 1992 SNCC- Skyblazer

events.title: Two Rooms and a Boom
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social deception game of hidden roles and deduction, players are divided into 2 different rooms. Find your teammates, establish trust, and exchange hostages before time runs out and the bomb explodes!

events.title: Two Rooms and a Boom
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social deception game of hidden roles and deduction, players are divided into 2 different rooms. Find your teammates, establish trust, and exchange hostages before time runs out and the bomb explodes!

events.title: Anime Offensive Show
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] This show is the same format as popular late night shows with the subject being anime, convention, and video game related.

events.title: Yuri Jeopardy
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Test your yuri prowess in this team based Jeopardy-style trivia game! Featuring both popular and niche titles with a focus on manga, yuri fans will be put to the test in multiple rounds of varied categories. Players of all knowledge are welcome!

events.title: Cute or Criminal: Romance? In Anime
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Whoop whoop! That's the sound of the police! They're coming for you because there are some things in anime that are just down right illegal. Join us for a spin off of our long time panel, Cute or Creepy: Romance in Anime, where we will explore the more mature side of romance in anime. This is an 18+ panel that relies heavily on audience participation, so be sure to bring your wit and old-timey detective hat, as we decide whether clips are cute or criminal.

events.title: Anisong Trivia: Hard Mode
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you an anime song super fan? Do you have esoteric anime knowledge many others don't? Then this is the panel is for you! With anime songs ranging from the mainstream to the obscure, this panel is geared towards the most seasoned anime music fans. Competitors will be seeded in a live qualifier and placed will be placed into rounds where competitors pick stylized categories similar to Jeopardy. The best players will be placed into a grand final to determine the champion!

events.title: Grand Archive – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Welcome to the Archive, Champion – do you have what it takes to forestall an apocalypse?

Come find out and learn Grand Archive, a growing new TCG fusing anime aesthetics with the best of western game design! Along with your demo kit, all participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

All material graciously provided by Grand Archive’s developer Weebs of the Shore. Thank you for your continued support, WotS!

events.title: Random Game Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The legacy Guilty Gear experience! Faster, more technical, and absolutely cracked. For the players who want their fighting games with extra spice. Win prizes!

events.title: Random Game Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Literally anything could happen! Every single match is a different game! Adaptability is your best friend here.

events.title: The Dying Art of Whump
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] It goes by many names—whump, hurt/comfort, hurt/no comfort, major character injury, and more. Whether it's an arrow to the knee or something more serious, fanfic writers like hurting their favorite characters. This craft panel looks at the fine art of whump as a form of storytelling nearly unique to fanfiction, its history, popular tropes, and asks a central question: is the genre endangered?

events.title: Sephirothsword57 - A Testament to Irony
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Sephirothsword57 is a Swedish content creator who claims to be "Spiritually Japanese" and notorious for his hatred of Hideo Kojima, Fromsoft, Nintendo, and western culture. For 11 years, he has regularly put out content that for many is hard to distinguish as parody. A Certain Pole chronicles him, his beginnings, and most importantly what makes something "Super Sugoi!"

events.title: Bad Anime, Good Time
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you ever watched an anime that you will never admit to your friends that you watched? Or worse, that you ENJOYED? This panelis for you! Find more of the cringiest stuff that you can't believe you actually kind of liked?

events.title: Oriana Peron Drag Show Doors Open
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage]

events.title: Digimon Card Game – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Di-Di-Digimon! Digital Monsters, The Card Game! Come help to save the digital world and learn to play this rich card game that has captivated audiences since 2020! Change yourself into a digital champion and save the digital world. And after you're done digivolving, you'll receive a pack on us to start your journey and a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: Moon Prism Power, Deconstruct!: The Magical Girl Genre Examined
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A beautiful transformation sequence, a cute animal familiar, and plenty of villains of the week – is that all you need for a magical girl anime? What is the real core of the genre, beyond selling toys to children? Join us as we take an in-depth look at the development of magical girl anime, from early works like Princess Knight to genre-definers like Sailor Moon to supposed subversions like Madoka Magica. Together, we'll tease out those true magical girl themes and transform our understanding of the genre.

events.title: Heaven is a Lie! A Hazbin Hotel Sing-Along!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Feeling like a Loser, Baby? Well, I bet You Didn't Know that today is gonna be a Happy Day in Hell with our jam packed sing-along! Not even Gravity can bring us down, because When We Get Up There, we're gonna Poison your mind with Hazbin Hotel musical brain rot! See you there!

events.title: Twisted Wonderland: Once Upon a Slumber Party
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Diasomnia has decided to throw their first ever slumber party! Under Lilia’s guidance, Sebek and Silver have been tasked to organize it. It absolutely HAS to go perfectly for Lord Malleus, so they have asked a few other students to assist in this strange human tradition.

events.title: Digi Paint, Pixles & Polygons: GCI in Anime
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] CGI in anime has a unique history when it comes to this art form. Come and learn about the evolution of this medium and its use in anime.

events.title: Late Night Go Variants
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Play different variants of the game of Shogi

events.title: Late Night Go Variants
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Play different variants of the game of Go

events.title: Magical Henshin Matsuri - The Saturday Sakura-Con Dance
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] Get ready to reveal your final form and flex your power levels on the dancefloor at this year's official dances - Magical Henshin Matsuri!
With special DJ guests:
Quackson, Assertive, DJ MADAM FILTH, Kingly, Zoe VanWest & E*Tank, SakeBomb, and LuckDragon!

Free to attend for all convention attendees!

events.title: Rhythm Gaming Freeplay
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Get your groove in with up to three other players in Just Dance, mimic silhouettes to iconic pop hits! Featuring other rhythm surprises as well!

events.title: Social Deception: After Hours
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social Deception will keep running as long as we have volunteers & players! Won't stop (lying), can't stop (deceiving).

events.title: Social Deception: After Hours
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social Deception will keep running as long as we have volunteers & players! Won't stop (lying), can't stop (deceiving).

events.title: Oriana Peron Drag Show
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: One Piece TCG - Free Starter Deck Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Footsies Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Footsies: the game about footsies! Strip fighting games down to their purest form: spacing, timing, and reading your opponent. Deceptively deep. Win prizes!

events.title: Footsies Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Two random fighting game characters enter. Only one leaves. Place your bets and watch the chaos unfold! You never know what will happen when Waluigi fights Goku's third cousin!

events.title: Coordinator's Top Picks
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] With so many fantastic entries it really is difficult for the Coordinator to choose just one as her favorite. Come see all the AMVs from this year that fought for that title.

events.title: Zapp's Spaceship of Love: Great Big PERV Debate! Doors Open
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel]

events.title: Essence of Ghibli: A Chronological History of Studio Ghibli through AMVs
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Every film from Studio Ghibli is unique and a masterpiece in it's own way. Join in on the journey through some of the greatest works of Studio Ghibli shown thought AMVs that focus on a single title and capture its magic and essence.

events.title: LADS Jeopardy: Battle of the Hunters
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you the most knowledgeable Hunter to play the game? How well do you know the aspects of the game? Do you know your favorite Love and Deepspace Love Interest inside and out? Prove yourself to be the top Hunter in the Battle of the MCs! There will be a trivia round at the start for all Hunters to participate in that will select our top twelve Hunters to be called to the stage to battle it out. There will then be two jeopardy rounds with six Hunters in each group. All participants in the main Jeopardy game will receive prizes based on placement! Prizes will include but are not limited to aurum passes, purple gem top ups, and/ or fanmade photocards / postcards. All panel attendees will receive a fanmade photocard freebie as long as supplies last!

events.title: Zapp's Spaceship of Love: Great Big PERV Debate!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Yaoi vs Yuri, which is the purest form of love? Cowgirl's vs Cow Girls, how much can we milk this joke? Was it difficult to get through this description, or was it not hard enough? Don't worry, it happens to lots of guys... Come watch our degenerates debate the perverse, the lascivious, and the highly inappropriate, in this special 18+ adult only extra spicy edition of the Great Big Nerd Debate! Who knows, we might even let YOU argue on stage!

events.title: Whose Initiative is It Anyway? (18+)
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Oh no! I forgot to plan this week’s session and my player characters are already here! Can you come help the S.Rangers come up with a grand 18+ rated adventure that will keep these intrepid heroes entertained? Or will this night end in another TPK? The only way to find out is to join us for this panel of big dice rolls and big laughs!

events.title: The Music of Visual Kei
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Visual kei is an artistic movement gaining fresh attention from younger generations thanks to social media, but it’s much more than just fashion. Music lies at the heart of visual kei’s identity. Join us for a fun listening session, where we’ll explore the music of classic and modern visual kei bands, and maybe you’ll discover your next favorite artist!

events.title: Yuri on Ice: Totally a Sports Anime… Right?
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] It’s been nearly a decade since Yuri on Ice broke hearts, boundaries, and genre expectations. Hailed as a BL gateway for many, it also captivated fans who never expected to care about figure skating—or its alleged romantic subtext. This panel is for the “Yuri-curious,” the skeptical, heteronormative-leaning, and the secretly converted. We’ll explore how Yuri on Ice blends sports, romance, and emotional storytelling to complicate these paradigms, and perhaps “manly” genres have always been a little softer on the inside. Join a PNW panelist, filmmaker, and lazy cosplayer alongside a Q&A panel with anime fans who can even provide deeper insight.

events.title: ERP Dos and Don'ts
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Do you struggle with writing? So do I! Making interesting characters is such a challenge, and don't even think about putting them in a situation until you have at least a PhD in conflict. There has to be a better way to refine your skills as a writer. Now there is! Today we'll be delving into the intricacies of writing Fanfic, roleplaying our favorites, and creating original content! To do this, we'll be taking a cue from painters who have long used nude models to best understand the human form. That's right; we're talking about smut and what it can do for you.

events.title: Is It Wrong to Try to Pick Up Girls in a Dungeon?  Episodes 1  - 4
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed)

In the bustling city of Orario, where gods and goddesses live among mortals and sponsor adventuring “familias,” 14-year-old Bell Cranel is the sole member of the Hestia Familia. Eager to prove himself in the deadly dungeon beneath the city, Bell’s life changes after a fateful encounter with the powerful “Sword Princess,” Ais Wallenstein—an encounter she’s unlikely to forget.

events.title: (Fanfiction Ad Libs) Bedtime Stories
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you a yearner? Have you been saved by fanfiction before? It's time to be that hero to someone else. We need YOU to help create the next hot fanfiction. From the pairing to the plot, it's all up to the audience in this game of Fanfiction Ad-libs. Join us in creating three fanfictions competing for the right to be published for the world to see.

events.title: Lucifer's Lounge: CLUB 666 - Hellaverse Dance Party
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Back by highly popular demand, Lucifer’s Lounge is back, going bigger, louder, and deeper into the 7 rings with CLUB 666, a full-scale Hellaverse dance party experience for fans of Hazbin Hotel and Helluva Boss! TO CLEAR UP CONFUSION WE RECEIVED LAST YEAR: This event is a nightclub-style DJ type dance party. It is NOT a choreographed dance panel. Attendees are encouraged to move freely, vibe, and enjoy the music in a high-energy club atmosphere. DJ SETS: Hyu Yoon - 45 minutes SCR4TCHK4T - 45 minutes Music will include official tracks, fan-favorite songs, and remixes from across the Hellaverse fandom, including material from Hazbin Hotel Season 2 and the newly released Helluva Boss studio soundtrack, along with other songs mixed in to keep the energy up! Cosplay is strongly encouraged, kandi trading is welcome, and audience participation is entirely free form. So if you're down to party like in Beelzebub's hive, JOIN US IN HELL! ❤️ ⚠️ This event is intended to feel like a club environment and will include loud music and/or flashing lights.

events.title: The Midnight Run: Toonami Tribute
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Ride aboard the Absolution Mk. XIV as TOM and SARA host a late night AMV tribute to our favorite Toonami shows, both old and new. Stay gold!

events.title: Overnight TCG Freeplay - Saturday Night
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Did you just get out of the Rave, or a late-night panel, perhaps? Are you looking to still have some fun at the Con overnight? Well, if you brought your own cards, we've got space! The TCG Room may close its outer doors at midnight, but we have a number of free-play tables available for BYO style play. Want to jam out some 2 AM Commander? We got you.

This area is not staffed between the hours of Midnight and 9 AM. All tables are available for use, but we ask that you please clean up after yourselves and behave responsibly in the game space. Failure to comply with this is a violation of intergalactic law. Thank you, and see you bright and early for more scheduled content!

events.title: Smash or Pass: FFXIV Edition
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Queue up, Warriors of Lust! In this alliance raid, we’ll be rating your favorite Eorzean characters on the only scale that truly matters: Smash or Pass. From Y’shtola’s murder-mommy energy to Thancred’s midlife-crisis trench coat arc, no NPC is safe. Bring your opinions, your glamour, and your thirst… just don’t roll Need on my man!

events.title: AMV Showcase
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] AMVs to make you laugh, cry, cheer and just plain enjoy.

events.title: Registration Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Social Deception: Open-Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social Deception invites you to come play games from our collection! Self-guided, or run w/ staff help!

events.title: BOTC: Continuous Games
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Blood on the Clocktower: A social deduction game for 5-15 players! As many games as we can fit in the timeblock! Sign up for the next game, or join in midway as a traveler!

events.title: AMV Contest 2026 Encore
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Encore presentation of this year's AMV contest. Join us in Main Events afterward for the winners presentation.
