events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Learn to play Japanese riichi mahjong with one of our teachers!

events.title: Mahjong freeplay
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Play mahjong with other attendees at one of our many tables. An annual classic.

events.title: Manga Library Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Manga Swaps Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: Prize Room Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Scavenger Hunt Open
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] Have questions about this year's SakuraQuest Scavenger Hunt or ready to turn it in and get your pin? This is the place.

Go to sakuraquest.org or pick up a paper form here or at any Info Booth to get started.

events.title: Rhythm Gaming Freeplay
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Get your groove in with up to three other players in Just Dance, mimic silhouettes to iconic pop hits! Featuring other rhythm surprises as well!

events.title: Retro/Console Freeplay
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Check in at Console Gaming for live content and more all weekend!

events.title: Digimon Card Game – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Di-Di-Digimon! Digital Monsters, The Card Game! Come help to save the digital world and learn to play this rich card game that has captivated audiences since 2020! Change yourself into a digital champion and save the digital world. And after you're done digivolving, you'll receive a pack on us to start your journey and a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: Flesh and Blood – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Dear Con-goer, do you desire Flesh? Do you desire Blood? Perhaps even both at the same time? In that case, we at the TCG Room have you covered. Come see us and one of our Flesh Experts (Blood Experts available upon request) will teach you all the ins and outs of the Flesh and Blood TCG. Should you survive the trials, you will be rewarded with a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

All materials for Flesh and Blood were graciously donated by Legend Story Studios, who are a fantastic partner.

events.title: Magic: the Gathering – Casual Commander w/ Loaner Library
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Come play a casual Commander game with friends, strangers, or even DRAW PHASE Staff!

Pods of 4 will be seated on-demand. No deck? No problem! We've got a library of pre-constructed decks you can borrow for the duration of your game. All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

events.title: DRAW PHASE Presents TCGs @ Sakura-Con - Sunday
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Welcome to the Sakura-Con x DRAW PHASE 2026 Trading Card Games room! We'll be running totally FREE events wall to wall all weekend long, from Learn to Play events, to Drafts, Sealed, Constructed, and more for some of your favorite games! Any event you do in the room will earn you a ticket that you can jealously hoard until Sunday when we have our Grand Giveaway, full of prizes generously donated by our lovely sponsors, donors, and friends. 

Whether you're a seasoned pro or a you've never played a card game in your life, we'll have content for you! Come learn a game you have always wanted to play or one you've never even heard of. And best of all, every single thing in this room is FREE! So if you want to come hang out with your fellow con-goer, meet some new friends and learn some fun games along the way, our team of dedicated volunteers will be here all weekend to guide you through the wonderful world of cardboard.

DRAW PHASE is a 501(c)(3) Non-Profit Organization that provides TCG Content in the PNW at no cost to the attendee! If you're interested in learning more, ask a volunteer.

events.title: AMV 201: Advanced Techniques
events.panelDescription:
[Fan Panel] Presented by CMDR Sariah and Winterblade AMVs

Take your AMVs to the next level! If After Effects plugins leave you puzzled, you're struggling to break into finals, or you just want some cool new techniques to add to your toolkit, then you've come to the right place. We'll answer your questions and help troubleshoot common problems. We'll show off the tools and processes that have helped us the most. Whether you're trying to impress a YouTube audience or a Sakura-Con judge, this panel is a must for all aspiring AMV editors!

events.title: How to Plan, Prepare and Maximize your Time Enjoying Japan (in 17 easy steps)
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] If you are planning to go to Japan, this is the panel for you. I will cover a multitude of topics including flights, hotels, immigration process, transportation, how to attend festivals, ticketed events, how to navigate the metro system and more. I am not going to tell you what the best plan is to go to Japan, rather I will give you the information and tools that you will need to create the best plan for you. If you are going to attend only one Japan travel panel this year, make it this one… we have prizes too!

events.title: Grand Archive – Free Constructed Tournament
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] You’ve learned how to start the fight, Champion – now let’s find out if you can finish it. Take up your provided starter deck, and go toe-to-toe with your peers in a test of skill alone. But remember: even in defeat, there is victory… in the form of a ticket to the Grand Giveaway.

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

All material graciously provided by Grand Archive’s developer Weebs of the Shore. Thank you for your continued support, WotS!

events.title: Surviving Life After Con
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Come join us as we discuss why we love con so much, our favorite memories from this year, and how we'll survive reality until next time. This is a discussion-style panel with the audience, and we equally welcome those who want to share their voices and those who come to listen.

events.title: OV3RTURE Sekai Games and RDP!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join us for an unforgettable Sekai-themed experience put together by Idol Group Project: OV3RTURE presenting as characters straight from the Sekai! Be sure to join us for our very own RDP that you can participate in and dance with us!

events.title: Opening Notes: Analyzing the Musical Heart of Anime
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ever wondered about the musical structure of anime openings? Join us as we explore the musical complexities behind iconic openings like Attack on Titan and Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End. We’ll break down the harmony, form, and techniques that make these openings unforgettable. No prior music knowledge is needed—we’ll make it accessible for all attendees.

events.title: Cosplay Sewing 102
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Cosplay Sewing 102 introduces new sewists to the essential elements of sewing. We will walk through the parts of a sewing machine, different types of needles and threads, and how to work with various kinds fabrics. NO SEWING EXPERIENCE NECESSARY!

events.title: A Test of Courage: Yokai Tales
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Hear haunting tales of some of Japan’s scariest yokai in hopes to last until the end and pass our summer’s (Spring Edition) test of courage! An informative panel about some of Japan's most spookiest Japanese yokai!

events.title: Princess Tutu and Beyond: Making Ballet Inspired Cosplays
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] From sparkly tutus to lovely bodices, ballet costumes have inspired costume design in a variety of media, including anime. Learn the intricacies in the construction of ballet costumes, their applications to your own cosplays, fabric and pattern recommendations, and tips and tricks from a ballet teacher and cosplayer for how to look and feel your best.

events.title: Let’s Talk About The Ranma ½ Reboot
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] In 2025, Ranma ½ received the reboot treatment—an updated take on a globally beloved series known for blending shonen and shojo storytelling. Long praised for its playful exploration of gender expression and fluidity (literally involving water of varying temperatures), Ranma ½ remains an influential martial arts rom-com for many lifelong anime fans. Does the reboot honor its source material, or does it fall short? How might it realign with the original 1980s manga? This panel is for Ranma ½ fans ready to dive deep into the lore, debate its legacy, and at least agree on one universal truth: Ryouga has the worst sense of direction. Join a PNW panelist, filmmaker, and lazy cosplayer alongside fellow local fans for an interactive discussion and Q&A.

events.title: Oshikatsu for dummies!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Come learn everything you need to know to show your true dedication to YOUR oshi!! Don't know what an oshi *is*? no worries! Today i will teach you the true art of dedication to one character.

events.title: Burning Rubber: An Introduction to Racing In Anime
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Want to check out some racing anime but don't know where to start? Seen some episodes of Speed Racer back in the day and want to see how it stacks up to more modern series? Come through and learn about some of the marquee shows and movies in the genre, and a little of the racing series that inspired them!

events.title: Artist Alley Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Origami Workshop with PAPER
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Discover the fascinating craft of origami and learn how to fold decorative, amusing and practical items with a sheet of paper.

events.title: Arts and Crafts Room Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: RPG Character Creation and Solo RPGs
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Learn to build an RPG character, or play one of the Alexandria RPG Library's collection of solo RPGs! Alexandria's crew of Librarians and GMs will be on hand to help you flesh out your character concept, or demonstrate RPG games that can be played entirely on your own.

events.title: Starfinder Society: Mystery of the Frozen Moon [SFS2 1-02]
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "A Starfinder Society Scenario designed for 1st- through 2nd-level characters, playable in 2-3 hours.
Known to the Starfinders only as Vast-723-Arctic-Moon, or VAM-723 for short, Luvoth is one of four small moons in orbit around an unexplored gas giant in the Vast. Initial scans of the moon indicate there’s no sapient inhabitants, but there are some artificial structures—an intriguing mystery! Believing VAM-723 could be the perfect jumping off point to explore the local stellar bodies and surrounding system, the Starfinder Society dispatches a team of agents to perform initial surveys of the moon and investigate the structures.
---
Game Host: Steven S
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Starfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Alexandria RPG Library Presents: Classic, Modern & Indie RPGs
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Visit the Alexandria RPG Library and explore over a thousand RPG rulebooks, modules, and player aids from countless game systems. From classic D&D to indie darlings, from cyberpunk noir to magical pirates. We bring all the dice, maps, books, and supplies for you to play in or run a game.

Browse our books, roll up a character, or grab a ticket to join our events. Tickets for each day’s games are available in-person at the RPG Library (Gaming Annex, Tahoma 4) at 10AM.

The Alexandria RPG Library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit based in Tacoma, Wash.

events.title: Board Game Library - Presented by Dragonflight Game Con
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Since 1980, Dragonflight Game Con has been introducing board game enthusiasts to a wide variety of games through their convention's extensive lending library of games. Now, you can experience that library here at Sakura-Con! Visit the Dragonflight Game Library in the Board and RPG Gaming Room, located on the Tahoma level of the Gaming Annex, to browse the collection of available games, check out a game, and play in the room's free-play area.

Dragonflight Game Con is a nonprofit, volunteer-run gaming convention that takes place every August in Bellevue, WA. Visit their website at https://dfgamecon.org/ to learn more.

events.title: Exhibits Hall Open
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Love and Deepspace: Unleashed
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Does Xavier or Zayne get your heart racing? Do you have a crush on Caleb? Want to smooch Sylus or ravish Rafayel? Or maybe you want… everyone to kiss? Join your favorite love interests to play games, ask questions, and unleash your inner MC!

events.title: Shogi lessons/Free play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come learn/play the ancient game of Go! Beginners very welcome

events.title: Shogi lessons/Free play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come learn/play the ancient game of Shogi! Beginners very welcome

events.title: Photo Booth Room
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents]

events.title: Top Percentage: Into the Joeyverse
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] And Arceus said, Let there be Rat: and there was Rat. And Arceus saw the Rat, and it was TOP PERCENTAGE.

events.title: Law for Creatives and Weebs and Creative Weebs
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] When do you need a lawyer? What do lawyers look for in contracts? What is a copyright and how do you get one? Is Phoenix Wright accurate? Learn the answers to all these and more from a practicing lawyer and illustrator in this educational panel for content creators and anyone else curious about the law and why it matters to you! (Not legal advice!)

events.title: Defying Fate: A Higurashi Panel
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Higurashi: When They Cry is a series that takes great joy in redefining itself at every turn. You think you have it figured out, but you were only looking at a fragment of the whole picture. For newcomers: let’s dig in to how to approach this unique work of art. For veterans: let’s talk about what makes it so special, what keeps it in our hearts even 20 years later.

events.title: From Lore to Loom: How Story Decides Design
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] From fabric choices to techniques- how do you make your cosplay feel rooted in reality? When does a cosplay call for fine silks vs. simple cottons. What characters would walk around with gold work broaches and handmade intricate lace? Learn about a variety advanced cosplay techniques and how to pick and choose when to use them.

events.title: Extra Life Game Show (and Tell)!
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Join Seattle Extra Life in an informational gameshow about how YOU can raise funds and support your favorite children's hospital through gaming! From signing up, to hitting your donation goals, you'll become a pro through a gameshow full of mayhem, fun, and a potential pie.

events.title: One Piece TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Obtain Fame, Wealth, and Power beyond your wildest dreams by coming to the TCG Room and learning to play the One Piece TCG! Will you be able to find the buried treasure- Or perhaps the treasure is actually the friendships you formed in your journey? No matter what, if you complete a learn to play session you will receive a ticket for our Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: Netrunner -- Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Presented by MTGPlatinum.com!

Come and enjoy a completely free, curated draft experience reaching through MTG history to provide you with an unpredictable experience you won’t find anywhere else

All Curated Chaos drafts are three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway!

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Netrunner -- Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come learn the Netrunner TCG directly from the team developing it! Null Signal Games has sent us their very best to teach you how to be the best Runner you can be! All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: Pokemon TCG – Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Sure, maybe you can identify all the original 151 just by their silhouettes, sing every word of “Gotta Catch ‘Em All” to yourself in the shower, and have a full shiny living ‘dex on Pokémon HOME - but if you really want to be the Champion, you have to be ready to take on all comers, anytime, anywhere… even at the card table.

That’s where we come in! Swing by and learn to play from some of the best trainers around, courtesy of Tabletop Village’s elite roster.

All participants will also receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway – so start your climb to the top here, and aim to become the very best. You know it’s your destiny."

events.title: Cosplay Swap Meet
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Bring any cosplays, accessories, or materials you no longer want and discover new items you’ll love! Half of the room will be reserved for attendees who want to manage their own "tables" on a first-come, first-served basis. The other half will be open tables to leave and take items. Swaps only, no sales.

events.title: Blades in the Dark: Cache Grab
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "You scoundrels have been approached by a broker in the sunless city of Doskvol, offering lucrative coin for running a score.  The job is to steal as much as you can from a weapons cache in the district of Coalridge, known for its abandoned railcars and derelict coal mine.  Players will be set into a session of warring factions, labour rights, and a resistance movement that threatens not just the Akorosi Empire, but the common man.
Character sheets and dice provided!  
---
Game Host: Æther K (they/xe)
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Call of Cthulhu : Poison Soup (Doku-iri Sūpu)
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "After going to sleep in your bed, you awake in the night to an unnerving scene. You find your self in a square room surrounded by strangers and in the center is a lone desk and chair. On the desk sits a bowl of bright red soup and a note that reads, ""If you want to leave, you have one hour to drink the poison soup, or you will be collected..."" Will you escape with your lives? Find out in this scenario straight from Japan from their number one TTRPG, Call of Cthulhu! New players welcome, pregens provided. 
---
Game Host: AJ
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: 18+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Exalted Essence: Wedding Crashers
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "A mixed circle of Exalts crash a destination wedding to cause chaos and stop (or start) the Civil War. If you don't know anything about Exalted, this is a beginner friendly game, characters are provided up to 12 options. 
---
Game Host: Jeremy Gwinner
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Fabula Ultima TTJRPG: Fabula Ultima - New Beginnings
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Fabula Ultima brings a classic jRPG feel to tabletop. Each player MUST multiclass, and classes are designed to interact to create a unique to you hero. Over 20 different classes to mix and match,  the only limit is your imagination. We will cover character creation and pick out a scenario to play.
---
Game Host: Brandon Crawford
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Old School Essentials Advanced: The Grotesques Grotto
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Three weeks ago an earthquake cracked open the Babbling Grotto, an outlawed temple of Iakos, wild god of secrets and prophecy. Now the cave “speaks”: ghost-chants, impossible laughter, and visions that feel too true.

Three days ago, royal witch hunters entered to stamp out the heresy…and never returned. In their place, something called the Grotesques keeps the temple’s prisoners, steals memories, and puppets the dead with runic stones.

You’ve been hired to go in, bring the hunters back (or their bodies), and haul out whatever relics survived a millennium of forbidden worship. But the grotto doesn’t just kill intruders—sometimes it remembers you differently when you try to leave

---
Game Host: Steve Bulla
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players 6 and older welcome)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Mystery Game Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] What game will it be? You'll play something different each round! Bring your universal fighting game skills and adapt on the fly. Win prizes!

events.title: CHUNITHM Tournament - UPPERS
events.panelDescription: [Gaming]

events.title: Charity Auction - Live Auction
events.panelDescription: [Charity Auction]

events.title: Niji no Hana Idol Random Dance Play
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Come dance to a random selection of idol songs of various medias ranging from Project Sekai and Ensemble Stars to K-pop, hosted by Niji no Hana!

events.title: Dreamland Maid Cafe
events.panelRoom: Dreamland Maid Cafe - Elliot Bay Room

events.title: REMOW Industry Panel
events.panelDescription: [Industry Panel] Introducing REMOW's current and future lineups including "LIAR GAME" and more.

events.title: Crunchyroll Presents: Dr. STONE Season 4 and The Drops of God Premiere Screening
events.panelDescription: [] Fermentation is a science, right? One of these guys drinks exquisite wine and the other… makes cola? OK. Two different drinks, but the food science is there! Join us for two premiere screenings of The Drops of God and Dr. STONE S4 Cour 2!

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: Feathered Friends Idols!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Welcome to the wonderful world of Idols! From Kaigai to cosidols, the Feathered friends Idolfest is here to usher in a new era for new and old idol fans! To those making their performance debuts to long time performers, come join us as we celebrate all the idol world has to offer!

events.title: Two Rooms and a Boom
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social deception game of hidden roles and deduction, players are divided into 2 different rooms. Find your teammates, establish trust, and exchange hostages before time runs out and the bomb explodes!

events.title: Two Rooms and a Boom
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social deception game of hidden roles and deduction, players are divided into 2 different rooms. Find your teammates, establish trust, and exchange hostages before time runs out and the bomb explodes!

events.title: Danmei 101 & Trivia!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] An interactive panel about the wide world of Chinese BL, where after learning some fun facts, audience members can try their luck at some jeopardy questions! A panel perfect for beginners and fun for experts, there's something for everyone here! From Heaven Official's Blessing to more obscure picks like White Cat's Divine Scratching Post, there's sure to be fun for even the grumpiest of cultivators.

events.title: Emi Nitta Concert Doors Open
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage]

events.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Free Edison Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Come and enjoy a completely free Draft experience at Sakura-Con!All drafts are three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway, as well as one pack per win!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Free Edison Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] If you've heard of any single TCG it's probably Magic: The Gathering. If you've never dabbled in TCGs, or have just somehow never learned to play MTG, we have skilled volunteers who will make it their mission to ensure that you learn to start slinging spells and forgetting to untap your mana for the seventh time this game. If you want to learn the grand-daddy of them all, come over to TCGs and we'll get you started! All participants will receive a ticket to the Grand Giveaway on Sunday!

events.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Free Edison Tournament
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Travel back in time to 2010 and relive the format as it was in the days of the last Shonen Jump Championship – as held in the eponymous Edison, NJ.

Three rounds of best-of-one games. All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway, as well as one pack per win! (Modern product only – sorry!)

Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Two loaner decks are available upon request as part of your reservation. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Uma Musume: Pretty Derby Tournament 3
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The final race of the weekend! One last chance to pick the winning uma musume and score those prize tickets. Choose wisely!

events.title: Rock Paper Scissors: Hit and Cover Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] The final chapter of the BlazBlue saga. Complex systems, incredible characters, and Daisuke Ishiwatari doesn't work here but the music still slaps. Win prizes!

events.title: Rock Paper Scissors: Hit and Cover Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Not just RPS—Hit and Cover rules from Japan add layers of mind games! Best of luck, champion of children's games.

events.title: It All Starts Here! An Anime Intro Sing-Along
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Who doesn’t love a good theme song? They set the tone for the show! Some are so catchy they stay in your head rent free for years and years. Join us as we celebrate some of the most popular and beloved anime intros in this sing-along!

events.title: Analyzing anime using Erick Erickson’s Psychosocial Stages of Development
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ever wonder why you like you favorite anime? Well in this panel we will talk about some ways that people connect with anime through the eyes of psychosocial stages. We will also discuss "objectively" what anime is best ;) Come for a good time and hopefully learn something.

events.title: Cosplayer and Photographer Safety in the community
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] This panel discusses best practices for keeping photographers and cosplayers safe during photoshoots. Topics include clear communication, consent and boundaries, shoot planning, location safety, online interactions, and recognizing red flags in the cosplay community.

events.title: Pikmin Trivia & Meet
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Love Pikmin? Come test your knowledge with our original Pikmin trivia game! Winners will receive a custom prize designed by us. After the game, stick around to make some new friends and exchange Pikmin Bloom friend codes, where you can find people to regularly do weekly challenges or party walks. While chatting, you can make a personalized Pikmin headband or work on a Pikmin-themed coloring sheet. Come have fun, get creative, and leave transformed as your favorite Pikmin!

events.title: Todoroki Family Truth or Dare
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ever wanted to meet the family members of the Todoroki family, from My Hero Academia if they were all pro-heroes, just like the Incredibles Family? Join us for an exciting roleplay-style panel featuring Japan’s top pro-hero family as they take on a game of “Truth or Dare.” As part of the audience, you'll get to suggest questions for the heroes to answer in-character or challenge them with fun dares. You'll also have the chance to take photos with your favorite pro-heroes. Don’t miss this opportunity to engage with these heroes in a fun, entertaining, and laughter-filled setting. We look forward to seeing you there! (Please note: This event is open to all, but may include strong language and light swearing.)

events.title: All About Cosplay in Tokyo
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you planning a trip to Tokyo or curious about what cosplay is like there? Come learn about the best spots to buy supplies, popular events and studios in the city, how to navigate cultural differences, and more!

events.title: Failing Isn’t a Bad Feeling—It’s a Superpower
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] In today’s world of social media and instant gratification, failure is often seen as something to avoid at all costs. We hate it, ignore it, and sometimes even fear it. But here’s the truth: I’ve failed more times than I can count—and I’ve learned to love it. Failure isn’t the end; it’s the beginning of growth. Every setback teaches resilience, creativity, and perseverance. My biggest inspiration? Anime characters who fail constantly yet keep pushing forward. They stumble, fall, and face impossible odds—but they never quit. That mindset changed everything for me. Failure means you’re trying. It means you’re learning. And in a world obsessed with perfection, embracing failure might just be your greatest strength.

events.title: The First Evangelion Fansub
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Neon Genesis Evangelion, an anime that has captivated audiences since its premiere in 1995. Most Americans first encountered the series thanks to the ADV localization, but what about audiences before that? What was the first translation of Neon Genesis Evangelion in English, who wrote it, when did they write it, and how does it hold up? Join Marimo on a journey through early fandom history to find out!

events.title: Food Wars Episodes 1 - 4
events.panelDescription:
[Sakura-Con Presents] ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed)

Soma Yukihira dreams of surpassing his father as a chef in their family diner. When his father suddenly closes the restaurant and enrolls him in the elite Totsuki Culinary Academy, Soma must prove himself among the nation’s most talented young chefs. There, students settle rivalries through intense cooking duels known as shokugeki, turning every dish into a high-stakes battle.

events.title: Welcome to Five Star Stories
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] The Five Star stories, one of the longest running mecha space opera's you have probably never heard of. First published in Newtype magazine in April 1986, it tells the history of the Joker Star Cluster, the wars, the mecha, and it's gods. After 40 years, there is a lot to cover, and this will be an intro to the first few volumes with your panelist trying to convince you to become as obsessed as they are.

events.title: Maid: The Roleplaying Game: At the Masquerade of Maidness!
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Join your fellow maids at an abandoned estate, cleaning it up for a night of revelry, dark secrets, and jealousy  as you dance to the tune of a long-dead master.

You all arrive at a palatial estate, as the sun sets and storm clouds roll in. Each of you, a maid of renown. Each of you, burdened by a tragic past and dark secrets. And each of you, clutching a mysterious invitation from the estate's master: restore the manor, bring revelry back to it, and receive your heart's most secret desire in return.

Only, the master isn't here. For that matter, nobody's lived here as long as you can remember. And as you all get the mansion clean and ready to host the masquerade ball, more and more creepy crawly scary guests arrive at the revelry...and they want you to stay, and entertain them. Forever! Unless you can all somehow fulfill the master's last wish and win back your freedom...
---
Game Host: Saoirse
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: 18+ (vulgar humor, sexual references, horror played for comedy)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Square Enix's SaGa Series
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Never heard of SaGa? We'll cover the entire series, from 1989's Final Fantasy Legend (actually a SaGa game, rebranded to sell better) to 2024's SaGa Emerald Beyond!

events.title: Sakura-Con and Me: My First Step into Anime Conventions with Hiroshi Nagahama
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Hiroshi Nagahama grew up inspired by American comic book heroes and dreamed of visiting the U.S. Sakura-Con made that dream a reality. He looks back on his first trip to Seattle in 2002, the excitement of his first anime convention, and how meeting fans in America influenced his creative work.

events.title: The Voices Behind the Heroes!
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Come meet some of the voices behind your favorite heroes! Sonny Strait, Kristen McGuire, David Matranga, and Brittney Karbowski come together to chat about My Hero Academia.

events.title: AMV Winners Doors Open
events.panelDescription: [Main Stage] Seating for AMV Contest Winner's Showcase

events.title: J-Walk Seattle Streets Fashion Show
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] J-Walk Seattle Streets Fashion show is back with another show full of lovely models and fun music. If you like j-fashion, but don't know how to coordinate your outfit, or simply just want to watch models of all shapes, sizes and genders strut their stuff in the coolest j-fashion, come watch us!

events.title: Fabrication for Cosplay: 3D Printing and Laser Cutting
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Are you having trouble getting the quality you want from your 3D-printed props? Or maybe you want to learn more about laser cutting EVA foam. Join FreckleBytes to learn everything you need to know to get started with 3D-printing, laser-cutting, or CNC-ing your cosplay props and armor!

events.title: Pokemania! Celebrating 30 Years of Pokemon
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Pokemon has been an international sensation for 30 years! Let's look back at how far the series has come and all the fun we've had on our journeys from special movie theater exclusives to fruit jam. Whether you're a gen 1 veteran or a new trainer, this panel is for all pokemon fans to celebrate the franchise!

events.title: Emi Nitta Concert
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage]

events.title: Digimon Card Game - Free Sealed Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] I've run out of Digimon puns and lyrics, so unfortunately you'll just have to settle with a Sealed tournament for free! Come visit us in the TCG Room and play a round of sealed Digimon on the house, complete with prizes and a ticket to all entries for our Grand Giveaway on Sunday!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Made in Abyss Episodes 1-4
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] ANIME SHOWING (Dubbed) Young cave raider Riko dreams of finding her missing mother in the mysterious, monster-filled Abyss. After discovering Reg, a powerful robot boy with no memory of his past, the two set out together into the Abyss’s depths.

events.title: Task Ranger 2026
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] The Sakura Rangers present a Taskmaster style game show with a live audience. Come see your favorite jokers make fools of themselves with high energy shenanigans for the favor of our Task Ranger. Ps. the points are totally made up, but don't tell the competitors.

events.title: Crochet Basics
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Hands on learning if you ever wanted to learn to crochet. You will learn the basic stitches to get started with any project.

events.title: Moriguchika Autograph
events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.title: Eyes on the Prize: Catseye Crown
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Eyes on the Prize is a collaborative game of court intrigue and fake dating, in which players assume the role of characters in a couple pretending to be very much in love in order to climb the social ladder of the court for personal gain: be that wealth, status, their families approval, or whatever other motive. Players enact a series of scenes to develop their characters, the relationship -however it turns, and to advance their goals. 

For this session, the backdrop of the drama is the courtiers, some legitimate, some less so, vying to be chosen as the successors to, or position themselves in advantageous relationship to, the hairless Catseye Queen. 

Will your character find wealth, power, friendship, failure, embarrassment, or even true love of one kind or another? Let's find out!
---
Game Host: Jack
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: D&D 5e (2024): Uni and the Hunt for the Lost Horn
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Uni the unicorn is in trouble. The evil spellcaster Kelek has stolen Uni’s horn to power a fell ritual. If the characters don’t stop Kelek, Uni’s horn will be destroyed forever in Kelek’s quest for power.

Uni and the Hunt for the Lost Horn is part of a yearlong celebration of Dungeons & Dragons and its 50th anniversary. The adventure presented herein involves characters from the beloved 1980s Dungeons & Dragons animated series.

This adventure is designed for four to six level 4 characters. Accompanying the adventure are character sheets for six of the animated series’ protagonists: Bobby, Diana, Eric, Hank, Presto, and Sheila, all presented in young adulthood. The seventh character sheet presents Niko, a Cleric from a different set of adventurers who recently tumbled into the D&D multiverse. Each player can choose one of these characters or provide a character of their own.
---
Game Host: Joseph 
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Legend of the Five Rings (1st Edition): Lesser of Two Evils
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Your group of magistrates has been summoned from the territories of all the Great Clans, to a remote stronghold on the edge of Crab Clan territory. The locals believe an evil is plaguing the land, and recent tremors have them whispering that the earth spirits are discontent. It is your job to quell their superstitions and get production back on schedule.. No need to worry about the monsters of the Shadowlands way out near the Shinomen Forest, after all. And certainly no chance you will have to do something you regret to avoid a greater catastrophe!

Legend of the Five Rings is a game of samurai drama and danger, focusing on the seven Great Clans of Rokugan and the Clan War that consumes the nation after the death of the Emperor. Characters will be samurai, priests, or monks who work together for the good of the Empire but also try to further the aims of their own Clan.
---
Game Host: Ryan
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Go 13x13 Handicap Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] A fun blitz Tournament on 13x13 boards. Games are handicap so all players have a chance to win.

events.title: AMV Contest Winners Showcase
events.panelDescription: [Main Stage] Come see all the winners from our AMV Contests!

events.title: Zapp's Arcade... Of love?
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Look, we all know that it's bad to play with yourself, so come play with us instead! Compete with your friends, complete strangers, and maybe even one of us, in a bunch of completely bizarre mystery games we are 95% sure you have never even heard of (and for most of you, are probably older than you are)!

events.title: Social Deception: Open-Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social Deception invites you to come play games from our collection! Self-guided, or run w/ staff help!

events.title: BOTC: Continuous Games
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Blood on the Clocktower: A social deduction game for 5-15 players! As many games as we can fit in the timeblock! Sign up for the next game, or join in midway as a traveler!

events.title: Sogetsu Ikebana Demonstration
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Shroughout the world.  Sogetsu emphasizes individual creative expression using the materials of nature as the medium.  Megumi Schacher is a certified instructor with the rank of riji, which is the highest possible rank. She is an active member of the ikebana community, teaching, exhibiting, and demonstrating the art and practice of Sogetsu ikebana. Today she will create arrangements for you that introduce the concepts and techniques of ikebana.

events.title: Godzilla TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Ever wanted to try out an Anime card game known for being easy to learn and incredibly fun to master? Cardfight Vanguard!! is right up your alley then! Come and play with one of our Starter Decks and be guided through a game by one of our volunteers! Complete a Learn to Play session and receive a ticket for the Sunday Giveaway!

events.title: Godzilla TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] This one's new! Echoes of Astra is a brand-new TCG from Parhelion Studios, an anime art themed game that mixes traditional TCG Gameplay with the tactical depth of war gaming! All wrapped in the package of some beautiful cardboard. If you want to learn a brand new TCG that is just starting this is your chance to get in on the ground floor! All participants will receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!

All our materials for Echoes of Astra were graciously donated by Parhelion Studios, so a big thank you to them for allowing us to show their game to you. Come over to the TCG Room and one of our experienced volunteers will teach you all about this tactically rich, exciting new game!

events.title: Godzilla TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Step into the arena with FREE ready-to-play Silver Age preconstructed decks, officially sponsored by LSS. Experience the thrill of strategic 1v1 combat while making new friends in a fun and welcoming tournament setting! This event is a great chance to learn your first hero or try out a new class.All participants also receive a ticket for the grand giveaway on Sunday!Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Godzilla TCG - Learn to Play
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Oh no! Godzilla is attacking the TCG Room! Come and learn to play the Godzilla TCG before he can unleash his Atomic Breath on us!All participants will receive a ticket for the Grand Giveaway on Sunday.

events.title: Japanese Tea Ceremony
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Presentation of Japanese culture through Japanese Tea Ceremony demonstration and lecture.

events.title: How To Cosplay Chess - Creating Chaotic Cosplay Content
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] What do you get when you cross live theater, improv, and a bunch of cosplaying nerds? You get Cosplay Chess! If you've ever been curious about the event or have never heard of it before today, this is the panel for you! Join our panelists as we discuss how 50+ people get organized into 2 hilarious games of Sakura-Con Cosplay Chess! Learn how the selection process works, how easy it is to apply, and the many different roles required to make Cosplay Chess happen! Get answers to questions like, "How do you choose who makes it into Cosplay Chess? What are Stage Ninjas? Can I be [CHARACTER NAME] next year?" And more!

events.title: Celebrating Live Action Adaptations
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] There are so many problems in the world: from famine to war to the discontinuation of All You Can Eat Shrimp at Red Lobster. But there are still so many things that have gone right in the world for us to enjoy… like the live action Death Note film directed by Adam Wingard.

events.title: Kintsugi Your Life: Using Forgiveness To Mend The Cracks Of The Past
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Were you bullied or socially shunned for enjoying anime or video games? The pain from these experiences often persists into our adult lives, manifesting as anxiety, self-doubt, and a detachment from our own hearts. Through the healing metaphor of Kintsugi — the Japanese art of repairing broken pottery with gold — you will learn a practical method for releasing past trauma and finding forgiveness for others and yourself. You'll walk away feeling lighter, more empowered, and more at peace with the story that made you who you are.

events.title: Joseimuke Name That Tune!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] How well do you know your boy idols? Come join us for a Name That Tune focusing only on joseimuke series, and anything with an all-male cast and music in it is fair game - idol series, BL, otome, etc for a chance to win a prize!

events.title: Extremely Hard Take-Home Anime Quiz, Answers and Awards
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] On Sunday, we will go over the answers and award prizes. You must be present to win. Non-participants are welcome as spectators. This session will be an educational experience for all.

events.title: Madfics
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Remember Mad Libs? Let's do some but make it nerdy! Join the S.Rangers for a [adjective] time where we will [verb] original Mad Libs inspired by fanfiction with prompts provided by our [adjective] audience! If you enjoy laughing, [verb ending in "ing"], and audience participation, this is the panel for you!

events.title: Cosplay Contest Judging Feedback
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Did you participate in the Cosplay Contest and want to know what the judges notes were on your cosplay? This is the panel for you! Come speak with the team and get their thoughts.

events.title: Bootlegs in anime merch and how to spot it
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] With the rise of anime merch and media, there are bootlegs hidden amongst the real merchandise. How do you tell what is real and what is fake? This panel will provide you knowledge to help spot bootlegs in media!

events.title: Call of Cthulhu: The Son of Nyx (Nyukusu No Musuko)
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "The year is 2098 and you are crew on the spaceship Sakigake conducting a mineral survey on the surface of Pluto. But when you begin your journey back to earth, disaster strikes. A red-black swirling celestial body crashes into Pluto causing untold destruction. The last thing you see before losing consciousness are the eyes of an unspeakable cosmic horror rising from the chaos. Can you escape your doom and return to earth? Find out in this scenario straight from Japan from their number one TTRPG Call of Cthulhu! New players welcome, pregens provided.
---
Game Host: AJ
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: 18+

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Idol Random Dance Play
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ready to show off what you know? Come to our hour-long random dance play, hosted by Lilium Stars and filled with songs from your favorite idol animes, games, and Vocaloid producers!

events.title: Who is Yaoi Jesus? An introduction to Dramatical Murder
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join us for an introduction to the controversial boy's love visual novel Dramatical Murder! Everything to get you started, give you a refresher, or just provide an opportunity to meet other fans of the franchise.

events.title: Cosplay Family Feud
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Come bring your cosplay teams of 3-4 and duke it out head to head in a family feud style game show hosted by the CWU Cosplay Club. Do you think like the cosplay club members? Survey say Yes! Small prizes included.

events.title: Indentifying & Navigating Fabrics without Joanns
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Fabrics are amazing, and also super confusing. Losing Joanns Fabrics and forcing lots of us to buy online has made it all the more intimidating to start making a cosplay. With this panel, hopefully it will be less intimating, as we cover topics like a basic intro to textiles, discussion on how fabrics change perception, and a discussion on where to buy. Whether you're a new sewist who has a hard time knowing what fabric is which, or your a veteran who isn't sure where to buy online, join this panel and lets mourn the loss of Joanns together.

events.title: Anime Character Tierlist Roulette
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Have you ever wondered if your favorite shonen protagonist would be good at running a massive tax evasion scheme? What about how your favorite ship would stack up against the rest in a knitting competition? In this zany panel, we're going to use a wheel to spin for strange arbitrary hypotheticals to rank anime characters by, and we'll all have to live with however the results end up. If you're the kind of person who wonders whether Sakura from Naruto would be a better F1 driver than Hatsune Miku, this might just be the panel for you!

events.title: Emi Nitta Autograph
events.panelDescription: [Autographs]

events.title: Pathfinder Society: A Star's Journey [PFS2 7-16]
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "A Pathfinder Society adventure for 1st-2nd level characters, playable in 2-3 hours.
During the exploration of an ancient Jistkan ruin in Katapesh, the automaton Star goes missing, leaving their archeologist companions worried for their safety. The head archeologist calls upon the Pathfinder Society for assistance, but warns the agents that arrive that the ruin is filled with dangerous creatures and traps and that they should be careful while exploring. A strange frozen automaton meets the Pathfinders on each floor, slowly pointing the way deeper and deeper into the ruin. Will they find Star? Or does the automaton have more malicious motives?
---
Game Host: Nick H
Session duration: 3 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This Pathfinder session is hosted by the Pathfinder Society Emerald City Lodge. To sign up for this game, see the RPG signup desk in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Break!!RPG: A Completely Normal Adventure
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "The peaceful domain of Millbrook faces its darkest hour. A monstrous beast stalks the woods, leaving destruction in its wake. The just Lord Vance grieves for his kidnapped daughter, Princess Helena, taken from her very tower. His scheming Vizier whispers plots in shadowed halls while a mad street prophet warns of apocalyptic doom. Anarchists stir in the taverns, an orphan child claims mythic power, and a traveling merchant dealing in secrets watches it all unfold with knowing eyes. 

You've been called to save the princess, slay the beast, and restore order to this troubled land. It should be simple, every hero knows this story. But as you peel back the layers of Millbrook's tragedy, you'll find things are not as they seem. 

Even monsters, they say, can love.

Expect twists, moral complexity, and the chance to either prevent-or survive-a spectacular disaster.
---
Game Host: Mia
Session duration: 4 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: How to crush at Mahjong
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Not a mahjong beginner, but not yet a pro? Learn some tips and tricks to crush at mahjong more often.

events.title: Lauren Landa AMA
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor]

events.title: Crunchyroll Presents: LIAR GAME Premiere Screening
events.panelDescription: [] College student, Nao Kanzaki, suddenly receives an odd letter and a suitcase containing 100 million yen. She’s been chosen to participate in the LIAR GAME, a high stakes psychological game where deception and betrayal is necessary. To survive, the painfully honest Nao must team up with former con artist, Shinichi Akiyama to outwit her opponents. Who can she trust? What lies ahead in these twisted games? Join us for the premiere screening of LIAR GAME

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: Seibu Ryu Iai-Battōjutsu - Art of The Japanese Sword
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] Our presentation is a 1-hour demonstration of the Japanese martial art of Iai-Battōjutsu. In the presentation, we will give a history of the art while demonstrating a series of our techniques. We begin with basic draws of the sword and cuts. Next, we move on to kata (forms) demonstration, including a few basic, intermediate, and advanced kata. Finally, the last part of our demonstration is tameshigiri, or “test cutting” of tatami mats with live blades.In the past, we have also performed a choreographed sequenced and performed our tameshigiri at the closing ceremonies of SakuraCon, which we would be happy to do again.

events.title: The Lost Art of English Dub Openings
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Whatever happened to over-the-top, cheesy anime dub openings? Join us as we explore the evolution of English dub anime openings, from their origins to the present.

events.title: One Piece TCG - Free Starter Deck Tournament
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Reservation tickets will be available at the TCG Infodesk on the day of the event. Unclaimed spots will be filled first-come-first-serve from attendees present at event start time.

events.title: Angel Hearts Idols Live, Learn, and Shine!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Step into the world of idols with Angel Hearts, a veteran kaigai idol group! Join us for a short live performance, a heartfelt look at our group’s 20+ year history, and helpful tips for anyone dreaming of becoming an idol. We'll also teach you the basics of ParaPara in a fun, beginner friendly tutorial. Come cheer, dance, and sparkle with us, and experience the magic of idols up close!

events.title: Japanese Urban Legends 2.0
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Japanese urban legends are like wasabi: they’ll make your eyes water, leave you a little shaky, and you’ll still come back for more. From eerie phone calls from the dead to bathroom ghosts who love asking awkward questions, these tales have haunted generations. And the best part? A couple of these legends aren’t just stories — you can actually go visit the real-world spots where they’re said to lurk. Pack your courage (and maybe some extra tissues), and let’s dive in.

events.title: AMV Showcase
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] AMVs to make you laugh, cry, cheer and just plain enjoy.

events.title: Subtitling AMVs
events.panelDescription:
[Fan Panel] Presented by MarshmallowGoop

Have you ever seen a video on YouTube with colorful or even karaoke captions and wondered how they're done? It's easier than you might think and is all possible with free tools! Learn how to make both accessible and stylized subtitles for your AMVs (or other videos!) in this session focused on helping your work reach more people.

events.title: Studio Orange: Creative Minds Behind Anime
events.panelDescription: [Guest of Honor] Studio Orange returns to Sakuracon!From Land of the Lustrous to BEASTARS, LEVIATHAN, TRIGUN STAMPEDE, and STARGAZE, Studio Orange has redefined 3D anime animation!Join producer Yoshihiro Watanabe, director Masako Sato, and director Naoya Nakayama for an exclusive look at how The innovative anime creatives is approaching the making of modern anime production at studio Orange.

events.title: Rhythm Gaming Freeplay
events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Get your groove in with up to three other players in Just Dance, mimic silhouettes to iconic pop hits! Featuring other rhythm surprises as well!

events.title: Be Our Princess!! (An Ensemble Stars Panel!)
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join Waggle Bees as Special for Princess (Esupuri) as they take to the stage!! You’ll be getting the princess treatment as they perform for you!

events.title: Point of Inspiration! Studio Ghibli in Your TTRPG World
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] You want to write a TTRPG campaign- where to start? Take a point of inspiration from your favorite stories! Join us as we discuss how to create a campaign you love. From world building to storytelling, we will explore how to write a game that shines with the same wonder as your favorite Ghibli films.

events.title: Mech Identification Quiz: Again!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Guess the mech! Or guess the Tokusatsu hero! Get points for guessing from a multiple choice list. Get more points by guessing it before the multiple choice phase! If you can guess what it is just by its shape, you’ll get the most points! Of course, what game would be complete without prizes? Place in the top 3 and win an embroidered patch!

events.title: Welcome to Teyvat!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] You came for the waifus, and instead you found a massive world, a lore deeper than the ocean depths, and story that is still taking players by surprise five and a half years later. Whether you're a longtime Traveler or thinking of setting out on your journey for the first time, take a step back and learn where you are, where you're going, and what exactly it means to play Genshin Impact.

events.title: Pattern Making Tips, Tools and Tricks
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Do you want to make patterns for cosplay but don't know where to start? Have you tried to draft your own but struggle to learn how? Join a professionally trained pattern maker to go over some of the ways they were taught but also rethought the way to approach patterns as a cosplayer. Bring a notebook because I'm hoping to give you a ton of tools and tricks to take home with you as you move forward in your costume making career.

events.title: 日本語を話そう〜！ Let's speak Japanese!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] ひさしぶり！We're back for another year of skipping Japanese class for SakuraCon; make up the lost practice time in an informal conversation practice circle!

events.title: Choose Your Own AMV Adventure
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Choose your own adventure, AMV-style! Join us as we tell stories through AMVs, with you, the audience, deciding the direction each one takes!

events.title: 40 Years of Adventure and Exploration in The Legend of Zelda
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] In 1986, Nintendo released the video game "The Legend of Zelda". It became a big success and the rest is history. This adventurous franchise has shaped gaming over the years with several games having influenced other game developers. Sure, some of those games are considered some of the greatest games ever made. What is certain is that The Legend of Zelda has a legacy that has stood the test of time by being innovative and evolving throughout the ages in video games. To this very day, it still captivates the imagination and continues to capture the hearts of millions.

events.title: Alexandria RPG Library Presents: Classic, Modern & Indie RPGs
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] Visit the Alexandria RPG Library and explore over a thousand RPG rulebooks, modules, and player aids from countless game systems. From classic D&D to indie darlings, from cyberpunk noir to magical pirates. We bring all the dice, maps, books, and supplies for you to play in or run a game.

Browse our books, roll up a character, or grab a ticket to join our events. Tickets for each day’s games are available in-person at the RPG Library (Arch Tower, Tahoma 4) at 10AM.

The Alexandria RPG Library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit based in Tacoma, Wash.

events.title: J-Pop Random Play Dance
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join a Kaigai Idol Duo for two hours of non-stop fun! This j-pop only Random Play Dance features all of your favorites from Project Sekai, to Hololive, to AKB48. Bring your water, your friends, and let's dance!

events.title: Warrior's of Light: Queue For Art Party Pre-Fan Fest
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join our third annual fan-hosted art party! We’ll display randomized reference sheets on-screen for live sketching and creative inspiration. Connect with fellow artists, share your work, and join our Discord to access the full gallery of references. Space is limited and fills fast—arrive early!

events.title: Cosplay Lip Sync Doors Open
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage]

events.title: A Trip To Edo: Learning to Tie a Traditional Kimono
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] An informative panel with volunteer models and host instructors guiding the audience through a step-by-step tutorial on how to tie a traditional Japanese Kimono. Models are by a volunteer basis, so we would love to have YOU as a model! Head over to our group’s Instagram tokyo_tea_fl to see the model criteria: Models WILL be required to dress accordingly to ensure the kimono fits appropriately.

events.title: Gender, Genre, and Games in Sports Anime
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] What makes a sports anime a sports anime? Is it the thrill of the competition? The forced team building? The homoerotic subtext? Join us to trace the development of shojo and shonen sports anime tropes/conventions from the 1970s (Ace wo Nerae! and Ashita no Joe) all the way up to the modern day (Birdie Wing and Sk8 the Infinity).

events.title: Funding your Convention Life!
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Fund Your Future, Fuel Your Fandom! Love conventions but worried about money—and your parents nagging you to get your act together? Learn how to start early by funding your retirement at 18–21, then focus flexible jobs. Learn how to budget for tickets, travel, and lodging. With smart savings and flexible jobs, you can live your fandom life guilt-free while your future grows quietly in the background.

events.title: Wander Witch: A Cozy RPG of Fairies and Witches: A Spoonful of Sunlight, A Saucerful of Stars
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "As students and staff if witchery and wizardry at the vibrant Brandysprite Castle, it isn't unusual for you, your classmates, and charges to be sent as envoys to friendly fairy communities. Whether it be to gather resources, catch up on the latest tales, aid villagers, or recruit newcomers, the school trusts you to be clever, creative, and kind as you lend your aid. This time, you are responding to reports of a mysterious dreaming sickness that has taken hold in the nearby village of Gossamer. It's up to you to explore, solve riddles, and forge friendships needed to restore the easy rest of its inhabitants. Will this cozy mystery be best solved through quick wits, an open heart, or a bit of both?
---
Game Host: Jack
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players 6 and older welcome)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Super Mario Bros Super Panel
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Mario games have been a pinnacle of consistency for the video game industry. Mario media adaptations have been the same thing, but in the opposite direction. Let’s delve into old Mario media, from the live action movie to the Super Show, and decide once and for all which spaghetti is the most overcooked.

events.title: Free Build Session
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] There will be a limited number of FREE kits available at each Free Build Session. These kits are first come, first serve. We highly recommend queuing in advance of each session. Registration will be required at the door for you to enter and work on your kit at one of our stations. Tools and assistance will be available along with both beginner and more advanced kits. You are also welcome to bring a kit of your own to any build session and use our tools or get assistance from one of our room staff members. All free build stations will need to be cleared at the end of the session to prepare for the next session.

events.title: Aged to Perfection
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Fandom and Cosplay are often seen as “childish” but they’re hobbies that can grow with you! Join us for a discussion of participating in cosplay and fandom as you age.

events.title: Anime Music Trivia
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Do you skip past the anime openings, or do you have these melodies memorized? You'll have 30 seconds to guess each anime OP. Volunteer to play on stage for ribbons, prizes, and glory! ... Or just enjoy the tunes from the safety of the audience!

events.title: Fanfic Mad Libs
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] An interactive panel about fanfiction, where audience members help us collaboratively create fanfic Mad-Libs style, and we’ll do our best to act it out! Come learn about fanfiction, test your knowledge with trivia, and see your favorite anime characters interact in ways they never have before!

events.title: Kyoto Animation Arson Attack - 7th Year Memorial Service
events.panelDescription: [Cultural Panel] In July 2019, the studio and publisher Kyoto Animation experienced a horrific and devastating arson attack that resulted in the deaths of 36 young animators, making it one of Japan’s worst massacres in recent history. Because the 1st, 3rd, and 7th year memorial services are of particular importance in Japanese tradition, we feel that the people who lost their lives that day were not given the appropriate opportunity to be mourned by their fans due to the interruption caused by the COVID 19 pandemic.  We, as a local Buddhist temple that is a direct descendant of the 1,200 year old Japanese Shingon lineage, believe that being able to provide fans the opportunity to participate in a 7th year memorial service would be particularly significant. We feel that it is our obligation to provide the opportunity for people to grieve and mourn the lives of the victims and honor their memories. Through the Buddhist memorial service, we will be able to convey to the spirits of the deceased that their lives, works, and legacies are meaningful, and will be cherished. This will also help mourners attain the understanding that their lives are one with the victims of the attack. When a work of art moves someone, it becomes an inseparable part of them, and so we carry its legacy on with us through our own lives. In this way, we are all on a shared voyage, helping those people who have positively impacted our lives continue to live on through us, and we hope to allow fans of the works made by the fallen animators to experience a deeper connection with this understanding.

events.title: CONVENTION HORROR STORIES
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Ever had a bad convention experience you or your friends laughed about later? Of course you have. And the longer you go to a convention the more of those stories get gathered. Join us for some of the funniest and most tragic stories of cons gone wrong ever experienced.

events.title: Sakura-Con's Cosplay Lip Sync EXTRAVAGANZA!
events.panelDescription: [Summit Stage] Lip Sync Extravaganza is a family-friendly, all-ages event. Step into the spotlight and show off your best lip-syncing skills in this fun and dynamic event! Whether you’re channeling your favorite pop star, belting out a rock anthem, or grooving to an anime opening theme, the stage is yours to dazzle and entertain. Participants of all ages are welcome to showcase their talent and charisma, with songs suitable for all audiences. Bring your best moves, props, and enthusiasm to impress the crowd and our panel of judges.

events.title: Into the Yuriverse
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] We'll be walking through the recent history of yuri, now illustrated with AMVs! Join us as we explore the modern multigenre explosion of yuri, with mecha, isekai, workplace romances, and more! Have your yuri with a side of some of the most awesome yuri AMVs!

events.title: StarBloom Micomet Dance Live: Presented by Gossipgirliesyvr
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] StarBloom Micomet Dance Live is a high-energy performance by Gossip Girlies inspired by the dynamic duo style of Hololive’s MiComet. This special set blends sharp choreography, expressive idol staging, and bright, character-driven presence to create an engaging live experience. Featuring a curated selection of J-Pop and anisong tracks, the show highlights synchronized formations, interactive moments, and the signature charm of the group.

events.title: The Chessies - Cosplay Chess Awards
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Come relive the glory of the 2025 Cosplay Chess presentations and celebrate the pieces, fights, and fun! A great panel to also meet and greet the Chess team, get to know them, and ask questions about Chess events!

events.title: AMV Gauntlet Finale: The Ship To End All Ships!
events.panelDescription: [Sakura-Con Presents] Witness the finale of Sakura-Con's weekend-long editing showdown! In true Iron Editor style, teams battle against merciless time limits, diabolical hurdles, and creative hell to forge their AMVs. Watch the final ships live and cheer for your OTP!

events.title: Cosplay in a Post Joann World
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] With Joann Fabrics gone, a lot of cosplayers are feeling the void in our resource pool. Come join our experts, from cosplayers to costume professionals as we outline resource hunting for the modern cosplayer. From fabric to foam, leather and beads, thermoplastics and more, our panel will give you all the resources to bounce back from losing our favorite crafting store. Now with special prizes!

events.title: On Borrowed Wings: Gender, Labor, and Exploitation in Armored Core 6
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Armored Core VI: Fires of Rubicon has a lot to love in its engaging combat design, spreadsheet-perfect mech optimization, and a distinctly lovable (while being totally faceless) cast of characters. You might know it from “mechsploitation” posts in certain circles of the internet or other riffs on the various voices that have your ear as a player. This panel presents a queer reading of the ways that AC6 uses those voices alongside its broader themes of corporate violence, and presents one possible answer for a pressing post-game question: could estrogen really have saved G5 Iguazu?

events.title: The Not-So-Informational Danmei Panel
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Know nothing about danmei? In this game-centered panel, neither do we! Come join us as we present danmei novels we’ve never read, and then play some silly games about danmei lines, tropes, and more. This panel is great for repeat novel enjoyers and interested readers alike!

events.title: Wig Styling for Beginners
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] If you’ve never styled a wig before or want to refresh your fundamentals, this panel is for you! Come learn all about how to select the right wig, plus some versatile materials and techniques that every wigmaker needs. Stick around for a Q&A session to get your questions answered!

events.title: Costume Design Principles for Cosplayers
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] Join a Cosplay Specialist as they bring their theatrical costuming experience to cosplaying to demonstrate how to use costume design principles to research, choose materials, and create a cohesive costume. Whether translating anime and video game character designs to real life or creating original cosplay designs from scratch, costume design principles will help you create grounded and artistic costume interpretations as you craft your cosplays.

events.title: D&D 5e: To Be The Very Best
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "Festival bells cut loud through the air! Adventurers are gathering to celebrate the Wild Monster Capture Festival. Tame wild monsters, team up with them in honorable combat, and you could win not only gold, but fame and renown!
---
Game Host: Ryan Zimmerman
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Everyone! (players age 6 and up)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.title: Nachatte Murder Mystery: A Pseudo Murder Mystery : A Snowed-In Tragedy
events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "In the remote, snow-covered mountains, a secluded cabin offers warmth and refuge to weary travelers. A place to drink, laugh, and escape the bitter cold. But not all who enter seek shelter. Last night was supposed to be a night of celebration, but when you wake with a pounding headache and a hazy memory, you realize something is terribly wrong. A body lies cold in the living room, and none of you can recall what happened. Was it an accident? A crime of passion? Or something far more sinister? With the storm raging outside and no way to leave, there’s only one way forward; piece together the events of last night before the truth is buried beneath the snow forever.
---
Game Host: NiiNya
Session duration: 2 hours
Age rating: Teen (13+)

This RPG session is hosted by the Alexandria RPG Library. To sign up for this game, see an Alexandria Librarian in the Board and RPG Room, located on the Tahoma floor of the Gaming Annex. Signups for each day's sessions open at 10am, and are first-come, first-served."

events.panelDescription:
[Gaming] "W-W-Welcome to the World Dungeon! Congratulations on making it to the most exciting (and probably last…) adventure of your lives! As your friendly dungeon AI, my job is to make sure your time in the dungeon is as exciting as possible for our viewers! Welcome to the first floor meatbags!

events.title: Seattle Video Game Orchestra Live Concert
events.panelDescription: [Fan Panel] A live orchestral performance of music from your favorite games! The Seattle Video Game Orchestra is a local community orchestra made up of players from all musical backgrounds and skill levels, all with a love for video games.

events.panelDescription: [Gaming] Social deception game of hidden roles and deduction, players are divided into 2 different rooms. Find your teammates, establish trust, and exchange hostages before time runs out and the bomb explodes!
